  - **`PollEntity` (`AppEntity`, `@available(iOS 16.0, *)`)** — `id` = poll short_id (canonical addressable id) + `title` + `groupShortId` + `groupName`; `displayRepresentation` = title with group-name subtitle. `static func fetchAll()` POSTs `/api/groups/mine` (`{include_results:false}`) with the bridged `X-Browser-Id` (Phase 2 App Group; bearer added when present), prefers `questions[0].title` over the display `title` (avoids the group-name-override collapse, same rule the detail-page header uses), sorts newest-first by `created_at` (ISO-8601 lexicographic), caps 50, and **NEVER throws** (returns `[]` on no-identity/network/parse failure → consumer degrades to "no polls to pick").
  - **`PollEntityQuery` (`EntityQuery, EntityStringQuery`, iOS 16+)** — `entities(for:)` id-resolve, `entities(matching:)` case-insensitive substring voice-match over title+group name, `suggestedEntities()` for the Spotlight/Shortcuts picker; all delegate to `fetchAll()`.
  - **`OpenPollIntent` (`AppIntent`, `openAppWhenRun`, iOS 18+)** — the thin consumer that proves the entity end-to-end: `@Parameter var poll: PollEntity` → opens `/g/<group>/p/<short>` (legacy `/p/<short>` fallback) via the same `OpenURLIntent` loopback as Phase 1/3. Gated iOS 18 ONLY for `OpenURLIntent`; the entity+query are iOS 16 so a future headless iOS-16 intent (e.g. spoken "who's winning…") reuses them. Added to `WhoeverWantsShortcuts` ("Open \(\.$poll) in …" / "Show \(\.$poll) in …" / bare "Open a poll in …").
  - **Visibility is BROWSER-SCOPED** (documented limitation, same as Phase 3 headless create): the App Group bridges name + browser id, not the bearer, so `fetchAll` resolves the groups THIS browser is a member of — every poll created/joined on this device. Cross-device-only polls won't surface until opened here.
  - **No backend/JS change** (`/api/groups/mine` already returns the needed shape). Native-only — not unit-testable in the JS suite or demoable on the dev server (like Phases 2/3); the `IntentDescription` keeps "siri" out per ITMS-90626. When wiring vote-by-voice / query-results, add a `@Parameter var poll: PollEntity` and reuse `PollEntityQuery` — don't refetch/re-resolve polls a second way.
- **Phase 4 `GroupEntity` + create-in-group SHIPPED (2026-06-05, owner device-verify owed).** The group-level analog of `PollEntity`, plus a create intent that targets a named group ("Add a poll to the trip group"). All colocated in `AppDelegate.swift`:
  - **`GroupEntity` (`AppEntity`, iOS 16) + `GroupEntityQuery` (`EntityStringQuery`, iOS 16).** `id` = group **short_id** (addressable `/g/<id>`); `groupUuid` = the `groups.id` UUID carried SEPARATELY because the addressable id ≠ the create key for groups (they coincide for polls, where `PollEntity.id` is both). `title` = group name (override → participant-names → "Group", mirroring the FE `buildGroups` default-title incl. dropping the current user via the bridged `identity.name`); `pollCount` drives the subtitle. `fetchAll()` runs `POST /api/groups/mine` (`{include_results:false}`, collapsed by `group_id`) **concurrently** (`async let`) with `POST /api/groups/empty` (membership-only groups), browser-scoped via the bridged `X-Browser-Id` (Phase 2 App Group; bearer added when present); NEVER throws (`[]` on no-identity/network/parse), newest-first by `created_at`, cap 50. Same browser-scoped-visibility limitation as `PollEntity`. The query does id-resolve + case-insensitive title match + `suggestedEntities()` for Spotlight/the Shortcuts picker.
  - **`QuickPollInGroupIntent` (`AppIntent`, iOS 18)** — required spoken `prompt` + required `group: GroupEntity` (resolved via `GroupEntityQuery`). Runs the SAME headless-or-fall-back flow as `QuickPollIntent`, factored into the shared `QuickPollService.quickPollOutcome(prompt:group:)` (iOS-18 extension; both intents call it, so the device-verified no-group path is byte-identical — only the success dialog gains "… in <group>"). **Headless:** the group UUID rides the create POST's `group_id` (server attaches; unknown id → mint fresh, no 404 — so a stale uuid degrades gracefully). **Fallback:** the deep link routes to `/g/<short>?create=1&title=…` (NOT the empty `/g/` placeholder), so the WebView create form attaches via the existing `<body data-group-id>` flow — group targeting survives the fallback with zero web changes. Kept a SEPARATE intent (not an optional `group` param on `QuickPollIntent`) to (a) leave the verified no-group path untouched and (b) avoid mixing parameter-bound + bare phrases in one `AppShortcut`. Added to `WhoeverWantsShortcuts` with `\(\.$group)`-bound phrases ("Add a poll to \(\.$group) in …" / "Create a poll for \(\.$group) in …") — using "to"/"for" before the group so the app-name "in" suffix doesn't read "in … in …".
  - **No backend/JS change** — `/api/groups/mine`, `/api/groups/empty`, the `CreatePollRequest.group_id` field, the `?create=1&title=` deep-link prefill, and the `data-group-id` attribution all already exist (this change only COMBINES them natively). Native-only → not unit-testable/demoable. When wiring a future group-scoped op (e.g. "create in <group>" with richer params), reuse `GroupEntity`/`GroupEntityQuery` + `quickPollOutcome` — don't re-fetch groups a second way. Note the short_id-vs-uuid split: use `GroupEntity.groupUuid` for any API call keyed on `group_id`, `GroupEntity.id` for any `/g/<short>` URL.
- **Phase 4+ remaining (backlog):** vote-by-voice (`POST /api/polls/{id}/votes` + `PollEntity` param), query-results (read-only GET, speak the leader, iOS-16 headless), richer creation (multi-question, deadlines). Phase 0 (the deferred WWDC watch) runs last and feeds a re-validation pass over Phases 3–4. See the plan doc.
- **Temporal window detection in the search box — SHIPPED (`parseTemporal` / `stripTemporal` in `lib/pollTextParse.ts`, search-box only).** A free-text phrase with a date/time hint ("dinner this friday", "games tonight", "thursdays this month", "9-5 friday") surfaces an additive **Time** suggestion row whose `dayTimeWindows` is PREFILLED into the form's window field. Design rules:
  - **Category stays keyword-driven; temporal text NEVER reclassifies.** It only fills the window. The Time row is ADDITIVE in the suggestion list (the keyword-matched category stays the top pick); a typed time keyword ("time"/"when"/"meet") just attaches the windows to that row instead.
  - **`parseTemporal(raw, today): DayTimeWindow[]`** is pure + deterministic-given-`today` + degrades to `[]` on any failure. It combines a **day-set D** (`parseDays`: today/tonight/tomorrow, this/next `<weekday>` incl. plurals, this/next weekend, this/next **week** = today→Sunday / Mon–Sun, this/next **month** — month ONLY expands via a weekday filter, "in N days/weeks", "couple/few days", midweek, early/late next week, start/end of week/eow) **×** a **band-set T** (`parseClockWindows` explicit clocks "7-9pm"/"at 7"/"after 6pm"/"7ish"/noon/midnight + the `BAND_LEXICON` colloquial table: morning/evening/dinner/late night/business hours/all weekend/weeknights/…). **Combos** like "thursdays this month" / "nights this weekend" / "weeknights next week" fall out of D × T (a scope + a weekday/band filter). Gating: needs a concrete day OR an explicit clock (a lone band word like "dinner" → `[]`). Bare day, no band → a narrow **evening** default (`DEFAULT_SUGGESTED_BAND` 17:00–21:00). Never past dates; capped at `DAY_CAP` 14 days; windows clamped within the day (no cross-midnight v1, `DAY_END_MIN` 23:30).
  - **`BAND_LEXICON` is ordered multi-word-first** (so "late night" beats "night") and the band loop blanks matched spans (`BAND_BLANK_RES`, precompiled) so a phrase suppresses its single-word constituent. The 4th tuple element `isMeal` marks meal NOUNS (dinner/lunch/breakfast/brunch) — those are KEPT in the title/context by `stripTemporal`; pure time-of-day words are lifted out.
  - **`stripTemporal(text)` removes the day/time indicators from the title/context** (so it reads "Time for dinner?" not "Time for dinner this friday"), and the resolved range is shown instead as a blue inline annotation **after the "?"** ("· Fri Jun 12, 6–8 PM") via `formatTemporalLabel` in `app/create-poll/page.tsx`. `STRIP_RE` is built once from the SAME `WEEKDAYS` keys + non-meal `BAND_LEXICON` sources so the weekday/band halves can't drift; the day/clock phrase literals are hand-mirrored (kept aligned by the test suite). Meal nouns survive; the annotation segment is a label-less `colorText`-only `SuggestionSegment` (the render's non-underline branch honors `colorText`).
  - **NOT part of the Siri `decidePoll` alignment contract** — `parseTemporal` is web-only (the deep link can't carry windows yet), so the Swift `PollTextParser` does NOT mirror it. Tests: `tests/__tests__/poll-temporal-parse.test.ts` (anchored to a fixed Monday `today`). When adding a phrase, update the parser, `STRIP_RE`, and a test case together.
- **Type-aware headless creation — SHIPPED (`lib/pollTextParse.ts` ⇄ Swift `PollTextParser`, no network parse).** Headless Siri creation used to hardcode a single yes/no poll for ANY spoken phrase. Now the phrase is parsed LOCALLY (on-device, no network) into the right shape — the same decision the in-app search box's top suggestion makes. **`lib/pollTextParse.ts` is the single source of truth**: `parseForContext` ("X for Y") + `parseOptionsFromText` (comma/"or"), both LIFTED OUT of `app/create-poll/page.tsx` (the box re-imports them — same code path), plus `detectCategory` + `decidePoll`. `decidePoll` precedence: **≥2 options → options (fixed-options `ranked_choice`, `winner_method:"consensus"`); leading yes/no stem (should/can/will/is/are/do/…) → yes/no; category trigger word (eat→restaurant, movie, game→video_game, when/meet→time, where→place, showtime) → that built-in category; else → yes/no.** `ios/App/App/PollTextParser.swift: PollTextParser` (extracted out of AppDelegate.swift June 2026; pure Foundation — keep UIKit/Capacitor imports out of it) is a faithful Swift PORT run IN the intent process (no parse round-trip). **Alignment is test-pinned ON BOTH SIDES by the SHARED fixture `tests/fixtures/poll-parse-cases.json`** — `tests/__tests__/poll-text-parse.test.ts` (vitest/CI) asserts the JS half, and `scripts/ios/test-parser.sh` (run by `ios-build.yml` on the Mac runner before every archive) swiftc-compiles the parser standalone with the harness in `scripts/ios/parser-harness/main.swift` and asserts the SAME JSON against `PollTextParser.decide` — so a drifting Swift port fails the iOS build (don't re-duplicate cases in Swift — an earlier `alignmentCases` array was deleted in `/simplify` as un-run dead weight + drift risk). **Change a rule → update the TS, the fixture, AND the Swift port together.** `QuickPollService.quickPollOutcome` creates `.options`+`.yesNo` HEADLESSLY (with an explicit "A, B, or C?" title for options); `.category` is NOT headless (those polls need the create form — time windows / suggestion entry / reference location) — it opens the form prefilled via a new deep-link shape `?create=1&category=<cat>&for=<context>` (the `&for=` param + its handling in the create-poll prefill effect are the only WEB changes; the web auto-titles "<Category> for <context>"). Spoken confirmation is type-aware. `QuickPollInGroupIntent` inherits all of it via the shared `quickPollOutcome`. The JS parser + `&for=` are CI-tested + canary-live; the Swift port + headless bodies are native-iOS-only (device + fresh `latest` build to verify, like every prior Siri phase).

---

### iMessage Extension (Messages app target — `MSMessageLiveLayout` plan)

> **Plan: `docs/imessage-extension-plan.md`.** Goal: interactive poll bubbles in
> the Messages transcript via a Messages app extension. **Phase 0 (target
> scaffold + CI wiring) SHIPPED** via #712. **Phase 1 (share a poll from the
> drawer) SHIPPED** via #713 — drawer
> picker (the `PollEntity.fetchAll` fetch via the App-Group identity),
> `MSMessageTemplateLayout` insert (canonical URL public / poll-scoped invite
> URL private), bubble-tap native summary with live results; full shape in the
> plan doc's Phase 1 section; device verification owner-owned. **Phase 2 (live
> read-only transcript bubble) SHIPPED** on
> `claude/imessage-extension-phase-2-lhsi3a` — inserts wrap the Phase 1
> template in `MSMessageLiveLayout(alternateLayout:)` (no-app/macOS/SMS
> recipients unchanged); a new identity-free `GET /api/polls/{short}/summary`
> (server-rendered per-question `result_text`/`label` via the same helpers as
> the push copy; tests `server/tests/test_poll_summary.py`) feeds the bubble
> AND the Phase 1 expanded summary through a process-level `SummaryStore`
> (20s TTL + in-flight coalescing); full shape + the no-redeem-on-render
> decision in the plan doc's Phase 2 section; device verification owner-owned.
> **Phase 3 (INLINE VOTING in the transcript) SHIPPED** via #718 —
> **Swift-only, NO server /
> migration / entitlement / CI / pbxproj change** (`/summary` already returns
> `poll_id` + per-question counts; voting reuses the atomic batch
> `POST /api/polls/{id}/votes` + own-vote `GET /api/questions/{id}/votes` + the
> Phase 1 App-Group identity). Full shape in the plan doc's Phase 3 section;
> device verification owner-owned. **Phase 4 COMPOSE-A-POLL-IN-MESSAGES
> (decision D) implemented** on `claude/imessage-integration-plan-b52mln` —
> a "New poll" drawer entry opens an expanded text field; the typed prompt is
> parsed LOCALLY by the **shared `PollTextParser` (now compiled into the
> extension target — the documented pure-Foundation-shared-file precedent, one
> minimal pbxproj change adding a PBXBuildFile that reuses the App-target file
> ref; bundle-id sed count unaffected, parser parity harness untouched)** with
> a live preview; **options/yes-no → headless create** (`PollAPI.createPoll`
> mirrors `QuickPollService.createPoll`: POST `/api/polls`, App-Group identity,
> no bearer → PUBLIC group) then inserts the fresh poll's bubble; **category →
> "Open WhoeverWants to finish"** via the SAME `?create=1&category=…&for=…`
> deep link Siri uses (the composer can't collect time windows / suggestions /
> reference location). iOS 16+ (the parser is gated there); all New-poll entry
> points behind `#available`, with `#available` as the SOLE branch condition so
> the result builder erases the iOS-16-only `ComposeView` type. **Swift-only
> EXCEPT the one pbxproj change; no server / migration / entitlement / CI-logic
> change.** Full shape in the plan doc's Phase 4 section; device verification
> owner-owned (Simulator works for the inner loop). **EXPANDED-VIEW BALLOT
> (deferred Phase 3/4 item) implemented** on `claude/imessage-integration-plan-z5wtsj`
> — the tapped-bubble summary (`SummaryView`) is now an inline ballot: a vote
> row per OPEN `yes_no`/`limited_supply` question (per-question via
> `ExtensionModel.isBallotVotable`, so MULTI-question polls work, unlike the
> transcript's single-question gate), edit-not-duplicate via the own-vote fetch,
> live summary refresh on vote — all reusing the batch `POST /api/polls/{id}/votes`
> + the App-Group identity (NO server/migration/entitlement/CI/pbxproj change).
> **In-extension name entry** (the keyboard works in `.expanded`): a recipient
> with a bridged browser id but no name gets a `TextField` → the typed name is
> the vote's `voter_name` + `BridgedIdentity.rememberName` mirrors it into the
> App Group (NOT a `POST /api/auth/account/name` mint — that bearer-less path
> would orphan an existing browser-tied account via `create_name_only_account`;
> caveat: the app's `NativeIdentitySync` clears the App-Group name on next
> launch if the app has no name, so a fully-nameless app user may re-type). A
> never-opened-the-app recipient (no bridged browser id) gets "Open WhoeverWants
> once to vote here." Shared `VoteChoiceButton` + top-level `VotingTarget`
> extracted so transcript + expanded can't drift.
> **Phase 5 FIRST INCREMENT — native RANKED-CHOICE expanded ballot** implemented
> on `claude/imessage-integration-plan-hgt316`: the tapped-bubble summary now
> lets a recipient RANK a ranked_choice question inline (per-question, so a
> multi-question poll mixes yes/no + ranked rows). ONE additive server field
> (`PollSummaryQuestionResponse.options`, surfaced by `_summarize_question` ONLY
> for ranked_choice with finalized options — null for other types / a ranked
> poll still in its suggestion phase, which stays read-only; options text only,
> no metadata) + Swift only (`QuestionSummary.options`, `BubbleVote.rankedChoices`
> for edit-restore, `submitVote(rankedChoices:)` → `vote_type:"ranked_choice"` +
> `ranked_choices` through the SAME atomic batch endpoint, STRICT ranking no
> tiers; `ExtensionModel.ballotRankOrder` working-order + `toggleRank` /
> `submitRanking`; `BallotQuestionRow.rankedSection` tap-to-rank-with-badges +
> explicit Submit/Update). NO migration / entitlement / CI / pbxproj change.
> Transcript inline voting stays yes_no/limited_supply only (a scrolling
> transcript is the wrong place to build an order). Tests:
> `server/tests/test_poll_summary.py`. The remaining "Phase 5" halves (expanded
> TIME/showtime ballots or a WKWebView for every-type-at-once) stay gated on
> earning it.
> Owner decisions: ship additive (degraded no-app fallback OK); embed a
> poll-scoped invite token for private-group bubbles (auto-join); v1 inline
> voting = yes_no + limited_supply only; pursue compose-in-Messages keeping
> in-app create exposed.

- **This is the project's FIRST second Xcode target.** Everything else native is
  colocated in `AppDelegate.swift` because CI can't drive the Xcode GUI for
  pbxproj surgery. A `.appex` target (Sources/Frameworks/Resources phases +
  product reference + an "Embed Foundation Extensions" copy phase + a target
  dependency on the host) is real cross-referenced-UUID surgery — so it's
  generated by **`scripts/ios/add-messages-extension.rb` (the `xcodeproj` ruby
  gem, idempotent)** and the resulting `project.pbxproj` is COMMITTED. CI does
  NOT run the gem. **The gem runs on Linux**, so the whole target structure can
  be generated AND validated from the sandbox (re-open the project, assert the
  target/embed-phase/dependency) without a Mac — the single biggest de-risker
  for editing the project from here. Re-run the script to regenerate; it's a
  no-op if the target exists.
- **Phase 0 had NO entitlements, on purpose; Phase 1 added the App Group.**
  Phase 0's bare scaffold let automatic signing self-provision the new bundle ids
  (`com.whoeverwants.app[.latest].MessagesExtension`) with ZERO manual Apple
  Developer portal steps — the unattended green-canary-build trick. Phase 1
  added `MessagesExtension/MessagesExtension.entitlements` carrying ONLY the
  App Group (`group.com.whoeverwants.siri` — the same separate-process identity
  channel Siri uses; the extension is its own process, so plain Keychain won't
  cross to it), wired via `CODE_SIGN_ENTITLEMENTS` on both ext configs (the gem
  edit is reflected in `add-messages-extension.rb`). The group is TIER-SHARED,
  so no per-tier entitlement-scoping step is needed in `ios-build.yml` (unlike
  the host app's associated-domains). **One-time manual prerequisite: register
  the "App Groups" capability + assign the group on BOTH extension bundle ids
  in the Apple Developer portal** (automatic signing does not auto-create App
  Groups — same caveat the host app's `App.entitlements` documents); without it
  the archive fails at provisioning for the extension target.
- **Phase 1 implementation notes (`ios/App/MessagesExtension/MessagesViewController.swift`,
  self-contained):**
  - The extension CANNOT import App-target sources without pbxproj surgery, so
    the tiny tier/identity helpers (`isCanaryBundle`/`feHost`/`apiBase` +
    the `BridgedIdentity` App Group reader) are DUPLICATED from
    `AppDelegate.swift` — keep them in lockstep. Canary detection is
    `bundleIdentifier.hasPrefix("com.whoeverwants.app.latest")` (the ext id is
    `<host id>.MessagesExtension`, so equality won't match).
  - Poll picker = the same `POST /api/groups/mine` fetch/title-rule/sort/cap as
    Siri's `PollEntity.fetchAll`, with X-Browser-Id from the App Group. No
    identity → "Open WhoeverWants first" state (the name-gate, mirroring Siri).
  - Private-group shares mint a poll-scoped multi-use no-expiry invite
    (`POST /api/groups/<route>/invites`, admin-only server-side) and embed
    `/invite/<token>?wwPoll=<pollShort>` as `message.url`; `wwPoll` exists
    because the token URL doesn't otherwise name the poll — only the extension
    reads it (the contract is registered in the `POLL_QUERY_PARAM` docblock in
    `lib/groupUtils.ts` so web changes don't strip it). The minted token is
    cached per-poll for the SESSION (re-shares reuse it); cross-session shares
    mint fresh invites — accepted, each stays revocable from /info (a server
    get-or-mint would require storing raw tokens, breaking hash-only-storage).
    Mint failure (non-admin member, network) falls back to the canonical
    `/g/<g>/p/<p>` URL → recipient hits the normal request-access wall. Public
    shares use the bare canonical URL (no payload params; the path names it).
  - The recipient summary (and Phase 2's transcript bubble) consume the
    identity-free `GET /api/polls/{short_id}/summary` (see the Phase 2 notes
    below) — Phase 1's original poll-read + per-question results fan-out and
    the Swift label/slot-formatting mirrors (`categoryLabels`,
    `questionLabel`, `prettySlot`) were RETIRED with it; the server renders
    `label` (getQuestionSectionTitle rules incl. the yes_no/limited_supply
    null-label) + `result_text` (`_format_slot_label` slot shape) and counts
    respondents with `voter_name_counts` multiplicity, so the extension can't
    drift from the web/push copy.
  - `MSMessage` is inserted with `conversation.insert` (NEVER auto-send) +
    `dismiss()` on success; the template-layout image is RENDERED AT RUNTIME
    (👋 on black via `UIGraphicsImageRenderer`) because the extension icon is a
    `.stickersiconset`, which `UIImage(named:)` can't load.
  - "Open in WhoeverWants" = `extensionContext.open(url)` with a copy-link
    fallback on failure (the API is known-finicky from Messages extensions);
    a separate always-visible Copy Link button covers the rest.
  - The drawer label (ext `CFBundleDisplayName`) is stamped per-tier in the
    existing display-name workflow step ("Whoever" / "Whoever α") so testers
    with both apps can tell the two drawer entries apart.
- **Phase 2 implementation notes (same file + `routers/polls.py`):**
  - Inserts wrap the Phase 1 template in
    `MSMessageLiveLayout(alternateLayout: template)` — recipients WITH the app
    render the live bubble; everyone else gets the unchanged template. Messages
    dedicates a VC instance per visible bubble (`presentationStyle ==
    .transcript`), so `MessagesViewController` mounts its UI on FIRST
    `willBecomeActive` keyed on presentationStyle (NOT in viewDidLoad — the
    style isn't reliably set there), with the drawer/transcript models `lazy`
    so each instance only allocates its own role's model.
    `contentSizeThatFits` returns a fixed `TranscriptBubbleView.bubbleHeight`
    (148pt in Phase 2; bumped to 168pt in Phase 3 to fit a vote-button row);
    >2 questions collapse into "+N more" so the fixed height never clips.
  - **Messages overlays the extension's APP ICON on the live bubble's
    top-left corner** — the OS draws it, the extension can't remove or move
    it, and it doesn't show in any non-device mockup (device-found: it
    covered the title's first characters). Only the first-line region is
    overlapped (~35pt past the content edge), so `TranscriptBubbleView`
    indents its first line by `iconBadgeClearance = 44` and the rows below
    keep the full width. Any future live-bubble layout must keep that corner
    clear.
  - **(Phase 2, SUPERSEDED by Phase 3) The bubble WAS read-only; the
    transcript VC handled its own tap.** Live bubbles get NO template-style
    tap-to-open from Messages (device-verified: an unhandled tap does nothing —
    an earlier assumption that it "falls through" was wrong). Phase 2 made the
    SwiftUI tree hit-testing-disabled (`allowsHitTesting(false)` +
    `isUserInteractionEnabled = false` on the hosting view) so a
    `UITapGestureRecognizer` on the VC's root view got the tap and called
    `requestPresentationStyle(.expanded)`. **Phase 3 reverses this** (the tree
    is interactive, the recognizer is gone — see the Phase 3 notes below). The
    DEVICE FINDING still stands: a transcript-style controller may request ONLY
    `.expanded`, and the system displays a NEW instance in that style activated
    with the bubble's message selected → the summary view.
  - **`GET /api/polls/{short_id}/summary` (`get_poll_summary` in
    `routers/polls.py`, model `PollSummaryResponse`)** is identity-free like
    `/preview` (decision B: a bubble is a deliberate capability share) and
    writes NO membership — rendering while scrolling a conversation must not
    auto-join. Per-question `result_text` + `label` are built with the same
    helpers as the push copy (`_compute_results`, `_format_slot_label`, the
    auto-title `_CATEGORY_LABELS` — no new label map); structured
    yes/no/secured/supply counts ride along for the bubble's yes/no bar and
    Phase 3's buttons. `response_deadline` strips microseconds (Swift's
    `ISO8601DateFormatter` rejects 6-digit fractional seconds). Group name
    resolves via `group_display_name` (participant-names fallback, not just
    the title override). Tests: `server/tests/test_poll_summary.py`.
  - **`SummaryStore` (Swift) is the process-level cache** (20s TTL + in-flight
    coalescing via @MainActor reentrancy; stale-on-error) shared by every
    transcript instance AND the expanded summary — several bubbles
    re-rendering in one conversation produce one round-trip. The bubble shows
    closed-ness via a COMPUTED `PollSummary.isClosed` (server flag OR deadline
    passed, evaluated at render) so a cached summary stays honest across the
    deadline crossing — the FE `isPollOpen` rule mirrored natively.
  - **Private-group bubbles render WITHOUT redeeming the embedded invite
    token** (supersedes the plan's original redeem-before-fetch sketch): the
    summary endpoint can't 404, and redeeming on a passive transcript render
    would join the viewer to a group because they scrolled past a bubble.
    Redemption stays on the explicit paths (web `/invite/<token>` fallback,
    Open in WhoeverWants, Phase 3 vote-time join).
- **Phase 3 implementation notes (inline voting — Swift-only, same file):**
  - **NO server / migration / entitlement / CI / pbxproj change.** `/summary`
    already returns `poll_id` + per-question `yes_count`/`no_count`/
    `secured_count`/`supply_count`; voting reuses the atomic batch
    `POST /api/polls/{id}/votes` + own-vote `GET /api/questions/{id}/votes` + the
    Phase 1 App-Group identity. The ONLY new summary parse is `PollSummary.pollId`
    (the POST target); limited_supply uses the server-rendered `result_text`, so
    no structured-count parse was added.
  - **The transcript SwiftUI tree is now INTERACTIVE** — Phase 2's
    `allowsHitTesting(false)` + `isUserInteractionEnabled = false` + the VC's
    `UITapGestureRecognizer` are all GONE. Vote buttons are SwiftUI `Button`s;
    the title / result rows / footer are EACH wrapped in a plain
    `Button { model.requestExpand() }` (→ `host?.requestPresentationStyle(.expanded)`),
    so every tappable region is explicit — NOT a UIKit fall-through (an
    interactive hosting view consumes taps, so the old recognizer wouldn't fire
    anymore). The vote buttons are SIBLINGS of the content Button in the VStack,
    never nested (SwiftUI forbids Button-in-Button), so a button tap votes while
    a tap elsewhere expands. **This interaction model is device-only-verifiable**
    (Simulator works for the inner loop) — if SwiftUI gestures somehow don't fire
    in a live transcript, that's the finding to address.
  - **Inline voting is gated to a SINGLE-question poll whose one question is
    `yes_no` (Yes/No) or `limited_supply` (Claim a spot / No thanks)** —
    `PollSummary.inlineVotableQuestion` (also requires open + a non-empty pollId).
    Closed / multi-question / other types (ranked / time / showtime) stay
    read-only; tapping opens the expanded summary. The expanded summary view
    itself stays read-only this phase (multi-question + the nameless
    set-name-here flow route through Open-in-app) — deferred.
  - **Identity-gated** on the App-Group name+browserId: `BridgedIdentity.load()`
    now returns BOTH (`name` = the batch endpoint's required `voter_name`,
    `browserId` = `X-Browser-Id`); `loadBrowserId()` delegates. With identity the
    buttons are live; without it they render disabled under "Set your name in the
    app to vote" (a transcript can't take keyboard input — the nameless user taps
    through to expanded → Open in WhoeverWants → set name in app).
  - **Edit, don't duplicate.** On load (votable + identity) the bubble fetches the
    viewer's OWN vote (`PollAPI.fetchMyVote` → `GET /api/questions/{id}/votes`,
    ballot-privacy-scoped to their browser) into `myVotes[questionId]` → highlights
    the current choice + remembers the `vote_id`. A vote sends the batch POST with
    `vote_id` set (EDIT — server uses the row's existing vote_type + enforces
    browser-ownership) or null (INSERT, `vote_type` required). The bubble votes
    with the SAME browser_id that cast the original vote, so the edit-path
    ownership check passes. Re-tapping the current choice is a no-op.
  - **`TranscriptBubbleModel.voting: VotingTarget?`** (the exact `{questionId,
    yesNoChoice, isAbstain}` being submitted) drives the spinner on the SPECIFIC
    tapped button + gates re-taps. NOT a "differs from current selection" guess —
    that spins BOTH buttons for a first-time voter (neither matches the nil prior
    selection). On success, `myVotes` is updated straight from the POST response +
    `SummaryStore.refresh(shortId:)` force-re-fetches (bypassing the 20s TTL) so
    the aggregate counts update at once. A transient POST failure leaves the prior
    bubble untouched (buttons re-enable when `voting` clears via `defer`).
  - **Vote-time join for free** — the batch endpoint `join_group_for_poll`s the
    voter, so voting on a private-group bubble auto-joins them (the plan's
    vote-time join); voting doesn't gate on visibility server-side (the privacy
    gate is read-only), so no separate invite redeem is needed.
  - **No post-vote `MSSession` "bump"** (the plan's optional cosmetic) — skipped:
    adds chat noise, correctness never depends on it (the server is the source of
    truth; other viewers' bubbles refresh on their own ≤20s `SummaryStore` TTL).
  - **Browser-scoped identity caveat** (same as Siri / Phase 1): the bridged
    browser_id is per-device, so a user who voted on a DIFFERENT device (no
    bridged bearer to union accounts) won't have their prior vote found by
    `fetchMyVote` → a fresh insert → double-count across devices. Rare; accepted
    for v1, matching the app's own anonymous-browser limitation.
- **Phase 4 implementation notes (compose-a-poll-in-Messages — Swift + ONE pbxproj change):**
  - **NO server / migration / entitlement / CI-logic change.** Reuses POST
    `/api/polls` (the same create the FE + Siri use) + the Phase 1 App-Group
    identity. The ONLY non-Swift change: `PollTextParser.swift` is compiled into
    the extension target (one PBXBuildFile in `project.pbxproj` reusing the
    existing App-target file ref). No new file + no new target → the `ios-build.yml`
    bundle-id sed count is unaffected (still app×2 + ext×2, asserted by the step),
    and the parity harness (`scripts/ios/test-parser.sh`, compiles the file
    standalone) is untouched.
  - **`scripts/ios/add-messages-extension.rb` is now idempotent end-to-end** — it
    finds-or-creates the target (was an early `exit 0` when it existed), THEN
    idempotently ensures the shared parser ref is in the extension's Sources
    phase. Re-running it on the committed project adds only the parser build file.
    Run on Linux (`gem install xcodeproj`); the result is committed (CI never runs
    the gem).
  - **The shared parser, NOT a duplicate.** This is the documented precedent the
    extension's own header comment calls out ("If a THIRD copy of the polls fetch
    ever appears, extract a shared pure-Foundation file compiled into both targets
    — the PollTextParser.swift precedent"). The parser carries the JS↔Swift parity
    contract (`tests/fixtures/poll-parse-cases.json`), so a copy would rot — share
    it. (The tier/identity/fetch helpers stay duplicated; only the parser is
    shared.)
  - **`PollAPI.createPoll(parsed:name:browserId:)` mirrors
    `QuickPollService.createPoll`** (AppDelegate.swift — keep in lockstep): builds
    the single question from the parsed decision (`.options` → fixed-options
    ranked_choice, `winner_method:"consensus"`, the "for X" tail as `context`;
    `.yesNo` → yes/no whose prompt is both title + context), POSTs with
    `creator_name` + X-Browser-Id (no bearer bridged), and parses the
    `PollResponse` into a `SharablePoll` (questions[0].title over the display
    title). **No bearer → the new group is PUBLIC** (`_resolve_or_create_group`
    keys privacy on the SIGNED-IN user only, None here), so the inserted bubble
    uses the canonical URL with no invite mint — exactly the Siri headless-create
    behavior. `createPoll` + `optionsTitle`/`yesNoTitle` are iOS 16 (the parser).
  - **`.category` → open the app, not headless.** A category poll needs the create
    form (time windows / suggestions / reference location), so `createFromCompose`
    routes it to `PollAPI.createCategoryURL` (mirrors `whoeverwantsCreatePollURL`:
    `/g/?create=1&category=…&for=…`, no literal title so the web auto-titles
    "<Category> for <context>") via `extensionContext.open` (copy-link fallback).
    Same fork as Siri Phase 3's `.category` deep link. The button label flips to
    "Open WhoeverWants to finish" so it's announced up front.
  - **iOS 16 gating, result-builder-safe.** The parser is `@available(iOS 16.0, *)`,
    so `ComposeView`/`ComposePreview`/`createFromCompose`/`PollAPI.createPoll` are
    too; every "New poll" entry point is behind `#available(iOS 16.0, *)`, and the
    `RootView` routing puts `#available` as the SOLE condition of its branch
    (`if model.composing { if #available { ComposeView } else { Picker } }`) — a
    MIXED boolean+availability condition (`if composing, #available`) is NOT
    guaranteed to trigger the builder's `buildLimitedAvailability` type erasure for
    the iOS-16-only `ComposeView` type. iOS 15 keeps the prior empty-state guidance,
    no composer (additive).
  - **Compose state lives on `ExtensionModel`** (`composing` bool + `ComposeState`
    enum, Equatable for the `.creating` spinner compare); `activate` preserves an
    in-progress compose session across a Messages background/foreground (the no-URL
    branch returns early while `composing`) and a bubble tap (selectedMessage)
    supersedes it (`composing = false`). On successful insert, `composing` resets so
    a reopen lands on the picker; on insert failure (poll already created) it
    reloads the picker so the new poll is re-shareable from the list.
  - **Device-only-verifiable** like every native phase (Simulator works for the
    inner loop): keyboard focus timing in an extension + `extensionContext.open`
    from Messages are the on-device unknowns. Not unit-testable in the JS suite, not
    demoable on the dev server.
- **CI wiring (`ios-build.yml`), two load-bearing fixes for a second target:**
  1. The bundle-id sed must patch BOTH targets, **extension-first with anchored
     `;`** (`com\.whoeverwants\.app\.MessagesExtension;` before
     `com\.whoeverwants\.app;`). The old single greedy
     `com\.whoeverwants\.app[^;]*;` would strip `.MessagesExtension` and collide
     both targets onto one id. The ext id is derived `${BUNDLE_ID}.MessagesExtension`;
     the validation asserts app=2 + ext=2 occurrences pre+post.
  2. The Archive step's global `PRODUCT_BUNDLE_IDENTIFIER="$BUNDLE_ID"` xcodebuild
     override was REMOVED — it applies to EVERY target, so it forced the
     extension's Info.plist `$(PRODUCT_BUNDLE_IDENTIFIER)` to the app's id (two
     bundles, same id → invalid). The per-target pbxproj patch is the source of
     truth (automatic signing ignores the CLI override anyway, per the step's own
     comment); `CURRENT_PROJECT_VERSION` stays global (both targets must share
     the build number). `ExportOptions.plist` already uses `signingStyle:
     automatic`, which auto-resolves both bundle ids — no export change needed.
- **iMessage App Icon ≠ AppIcon: it's a `.stickersiconset`, not a `.appiconset`,
  and needs the COMPLETE 13-image set.** Two distinct actool failures hit the
  first two canary builds:
  1. **Wrong folder type.** A Messages-extension icon set MUST be
    `iMessage App Icon.stickersiconset` (actool compiles it with
    `--include-sticker-content --stickers-icon-role extension`). Putting the same
    images in a `.appiconset` fails ARCHIVE with `error: The stickers icon set or
    app icon set named "iMessage App Icon" did not have any applicable content`
    \+ `warning: ...has N unassigned children` — the dead giveaway that the
    Messages sizes are being read as a regular (square) app icon. `ASSETCATALOG_COMPILER_APPICON_NAME = "iMessage App Icon"` (name only, no extension).
  2. **Incomplete set.** The canonical set is 13 images: square `29x29` app-icon
    sizes (58²/87²) + a square `1024x1024` marketing icon + the 3:2
    `60x45`/`67x50`/`74x55`/`27x20`/`32x24` sizes + the `1024x768` Messages App
    Store icon. A partial set (e.g. only the 9 3:2 sizes) is also a hard actool
    archive failure. The per-entry `platform: "ios"` field is present ONLY on the
    `universal` + the `1024x768` entries (mirror Xcode exactly).
  Both are ARCHIVE failures (not just TestFlight rejects). `scripts/ios/gen-imessage-icon.py`
  composites the app's transparent-corner 👋 mark (`public/icon-512x512.png`) onto
  opaque black canvases (square entries square, 3:2 entries 3:2) and drives BOTH
  the PNGs and Contents.json from one 13-row SPEC so they can't drift. Output PNGs
  are committed via a `.gitignore` override
  (`!ios/App/MessagesExtension/Assets.xcassets/**/*.png`), mirroring the existing
  AppIcon override; `ASSETCATALOG_COMPILER_APPICON_NAME = "iMessage App Icon"` on
  the extension target. When in doubt about the exact set, copy a real
  Xcode-generated `iMessage App Icon.appiconset/Contents.json` rather than
  hand-rolling — guessing the slot list burns Mac CI cycles.
- **Verification split (same as every native phase):** CI green (archive + embed
  `.appex` + TestFlight upload) is the sandbox-validatable half; the Messages
  drawer showing the app + placeholder on a real device is owner-owned. Simulator
  CAN run Messages extensions (unlike Siri), so future-phase inner-loop dev
  doesn't strictly need TestFlight, but on-device remains the ship gate.

---

## Group Member Roster (`/info` Members list)

The `/info` "Members" list is built from the ACTUAL membership table
(`group_members`), NOT from `Group.participantNames` (poll creators +
voters). The participant-derived roster missed anyone who joined via
approve / invite-link / "Add people" but hadn't voted on a poll yet — on an
empty group the list only ever showed the viewer (the reported bug: approve
Bob → list still says only Sam). Shipped on
`claude/group-invite-privacy-controls-fn3prr`.

- **`GET /api/groups/{route_id}/members`** (`routers/groups.py`, model
  `GroupMembersResponse {members: [{name, user_id}], anonymous_count}`).
  Named members are listed individually + de-duped account-aware (one person
  across N browsers = ONE entry — collapses on `COALESCE(user_id, browser_id)`,
  mirroring `load_user_visibility`'s `user_browsers` union); members with no
  resolvable name roll up into `anonymous_count` (drive-by URL visitors who
  auto-joined a public group, per the owner's "roll anon up" choice — don't
  list them as blank rows). **Members-only for private groups** (404 to
  non-members, same gate as `/summary`); public groups open.
  `services/groups.py: load_group_members(conn, group_id) → (named, anon)` is
  the shared query — name = account `display_name`, else the most-recent
  `votes.voter_name` for that browser (correlated subquery, fine at roster
  cardinality + `votes_browser_id_idx`), else anonymous.
- **FE: `apiGetGroupMembers(routeId)` → `GroupRoster`** (`lib/api/groups.ts`;
  404 maps to an empty roster, like `apiGetGroupPoll`). `/info` fetches it and
  renders named rows + an "N anonymous" roll-up `<li>`, keeping the "you
  always see yourself" guarantee (if the viewer isn't a resolved named row
  they're pulled out of `anonymous_count` and shown as themselves / "You").
  While the roster is loading it falls back to the prior `participantNames`
  list so there's no empty flash. The HERO AVATAR + TITLE still use
  `participantNames` (unchanged).
- **Refresh wiring — two mechanisms by structural distance (deliberate, do
  NOT unify):** `JoinRequestsSection` is an in-tree CHILD of `/info`, so it
  reports approvals via a plain `onDecided(action)` prop callback → `/info`
  bumps `rosterTick` → refetch. The "Add people" / invite-members screen is a
  SEPARATE slide-route (no React-tree link to the still-mounted `/info`
  underneath the overlay), so it dispatches the cross-route
  `GROUP_MEMBERS_CHANGED_EVENT {routeId}` (`lib/eventChannels.ts`) on a
  successful add; `/info` listens + refetches on a `routeId` match. Routing
  the in-tree child through the global event would be a downgrade (it'd
  pretend a parent/child relationship is cross-route). `/info` also refetches
  on tab `visibilitychange → visible`.
- **`require_group_read_access(conn, route_id, *, browser_id, user_id) → group_id`
  (`routers/groups.py`, SHIPPED) is the single members-only-read privacy gate.**
  Collapses the "resolve route → 404 on miss → `get_group_metadata` → 404
  non-members of a private group" block that was copy-pasted byte-for-byte in
  `get_group_summary` + `get_group_members`. Public groups stay openly readable
  (NO auto-join — these endpoints only READ metadata/roster), private groups 404
  non-members; raises HTTPException(404, "Group not found") internally. It's the
  read-side sibling of `services/groups.py: resolve_group_for_visit` (the
  `/by-route-id` AUTO-JOIN variant — that one writes a membership row for public
  visitors, so the two can't be merged). Route any NEW members-only read through
  this helper so the privacy contract stays a single auditable point. NOTE the
  deliberately-stricter outliers that do NOT use it: `get_poll_voters`
  (`/poll/{ref}/voter-identities`) member-gates EVEN public groups, and `claim_group`
  gates on membership with a 403/custom detail — different contracts. `get_group_metadata`
  is still fetched directly at the non-read sites (push creator lookup in the
  join-request endpoint, the boot-private-only check) where the read-access gate
  doesn't apply.
- Tests: `server/tests/test_group_members_roster.py` (named members incl.
  not-yet-voted, account de-dup across browsers, anonymous roll-up, private
  members-only).

---

## Group Admin System (migration 142)

Every group has ≥1 **admin** at all times (when it has ≥1 account member). Admins
generalize the old single `groups.creator_user_id` into a set. An admin can:
toggle privacy, edit title/avatar, approve join requests, mint/revoke invite
links, add people directly, **promote** members to admin, and **boot** non-admin
members. Design decisions (owner-confirmed): **no demotion and no owner
hierarchy** (Q4 — once an admin, you stay one until you LEAVE the group, which is
the only event that shrinks the admin set); **boot works on PUBLIC groups too**
(the original Q3 "private-only" rule was REVISED by the owner — boot revokes the
member's invite + clears the current roster; on a public group the member can
still re-visit and auto-rejoin, but the owner wanted the removal action
available everywhere) and revokes the specific invite link the booted member
joined through; **admins are account-keyed** (`group_members` is browser-keyed,
but admin powers need a real account). **Anonymous (no-name) members can be
booted too** via an opaque per-roster handle (see the boot endpoints + the
`anonymous_member_handle` bullet below) — we never expose a member's raw
`browser_id` to the client (it's a cross-group identity token).

- **Schema:** `group_admins(group_id, user_id, granted_at)` PK `(group_id,
  user_id)`, both FKs CASCADE, index on `user_id`. `group_members.joined_via_invite_id`
  (FK → `group_invites`, SET NULL) records which invite a member joined through
  (set in `redeem_invite`, only on a genuine first join) so boot can revoke it.
  The migration WIPED all legacy NULL-creator groups (owner: disposable
  anonymous-era data — prod was ~97% NULL-creator since group creator was
  deliberately NULL for anonymous creators) and seeded surviving creators as
  admin #1.
- **`creator_user_id` is now vestigial historical data, NOT an authorization
  signal.** It is NOT NOT-NULL (its FK is ON DELETE SET NULL, so a creator
  deleting their account legitimately nulls it). The "every group has an admin"
  invariant lives in `group_admins` (seeded on create + auto-promotion), not a
  column constraint. Don't reintroduce creator-based auth gates.
- **No more NULL-creator groups going forward.** Both create paths always record
  a creator: `POST /api/polls` (`_resolve_or_create_group` uses the poll's
  always-set `creator_user_id` — the auto-account for anonymous creators —
  DECOUPLED from privacy, which still keys on genuine sign-in so anonymous groups
  stay public/URL-shareable) and `POST /api/groups` (resolves the actor, or
  auto-mints a nameless account for a bare browser). Both seed the `group_admins`
  row. The **claim** endpoint/flow is obsolete (every group already has a
  creator → claim always 409s); kept as harmless dead code, FE claim UI removed.
- **`_require_admin(conn, group_id, *, browser_id, user_id) → admin_user_id`**
  (`routers/groups.py`) is the single authorization gate. It resolves the actor
  via `resolve_actor_user_id` (bearer OR the browser→account link — so a
  browser-tied auto-account admin qualifies WITHOUT a bearer; 401 only when NO
  account resolves) then requires a `group_admins` row (403 otherwise). It
  REPLACED the per-endpoint `creator_user_id == user_id` gate on privacy /
  join-requests / invites, and NEWLY gates title + image edits + add-people (Q6:
  admin-only; these had no check before). `revoke_invite` is now group-scoped (any
  admin revokes any of the group's invites, not just the minter).
- **Service helpers (`services/groups.py`):** `is_group_admin` / `is_caller_admin`
  / `add_group_admin` / `remove_group_admin` / `group_admin_user_ids` /
  `promote_oldest_member_if_adminless` / `boot_member`. **Auto-promotion** runs
  whenever the admin set could empty: `leave_group` endpoint (leaving drops your
  admin row, then promotes the oldest remaining account-member by
  `MIN(joined_at)`), and `delete_user_account` (captures the user's admin groups
  before the cascade, promotes after). `merge_accounts` repoints `group_admins`
  (dest keeps its row on collision) so a sign-in absorb / explicit merge never
  silently demotes. No account member to promote ⇒ group stays admin-less (the
  accepted "≥1 account member" condition).
- **New endpoints:** `POST /{route}/admins {user_id}` (promote a member; target
  must be a member), `POST /{route}/members/{user_id}/boot` (any-privacy now,
  non-admin target, not self → removes membership across linked browsers +
  revokes their `joined_via_invite_id`), `POST /{route}/members/by-handle/{handle}/boot`
  (boot an ANONYMOUS member by their opaque roster handle — re-derives each
  anon member's handle server-side, matches, then `boot_member_by_browser`).
  All admin-only.
- **`anonymous_member_handle(group_id, person_key)` (`services/groups.py`)** is
  the opaque-id scheme for booting nameless members without leaking
  `browser_id`. `person_key` = the member's account user_id (account-no-name)
  else their browser_id (browser-only); the handle is
  `sha256(f"{group_id}:{person_key}")` — one-way (doesn't reveal the key),
  deterministic, group-scoped, and matchable server-side by recomputing it over
  the group's anonymous members. No secret needed (an attacker who could forge a
  handle already knows the browser_id; boot is admin-only + scoped to existing
  members). `load_group_members` now returns `(named, anonymous)` where
  `anonymous` is a list of `{user_id, browser_id}` (browser_id is a
  REPRESENTATIVE member row, kept server-side only); the endpoint maps it to
  `anonymous_members: [{handle}]` and keeps `anonymous_count = len(anonymous)`
  for older clients.
- **`GET /{route}/members`** now returns `viewer_is_admin` (top-level) +
  per-member `is_admin`, plus `anonymous_members: [{handle}]` (and the
  count-only `anonymous_count`) and `viewer_anonymous_handle` (the viewer's own
  anon handle, or null — the FE filters exactly this entry out of the displayed
  anon list so the viewer shows as the "You" row, never with a self-boot
  3-dots). Anonymous (nameless) members are never admins
  (account-keyed); a nameless admin (no display_name) is an admin but rolls up
  anonymously — FE admins always have a name via the name-gate.
- **FE:** `apiGetGroupMembers` surfaces `viewer_is_admin` + `is_admin` +
  `anonymous_members`; `apiPromoteGroupAdmin` / `apiBootGroupMember` /
  `apiBootGroupAnonymous`. The `/info` page gates ALL admin chrome on
  `roster.viewer_is_admin`: Privacy toggle (via `GroupPrivacySection`'s
  `viewerIsAdmin` prop — its claim flow is removed),
  JoinRequestsSection/InviteLinksSection `enabled`, Add-people, Edit-title, and a
  per-member **3-dots action sheet** (`components/MemberActionsSheet.tsx`, haptic
  on open) offering **Make admin** (named members only) + **Remove from group**
  (all members, incl. anonymous via handle), each routed through a
  `ConfirmationModal`. The expandable "N anonymous" roster row reveals individual
  "Anonymous" rows, each with its own 3-dots → Remove. `InviteLinksSection` is
  one compact line per invite (mode · usage · age) with a trashcan revoke, and a
  small `+` mint button in the section header (no separate bottom button). The
  per-POLL info page is UNCHANGED — its close/reopen/cutoff actions gate on POLL
  authorship (`poll.viewer_is_creator`), a separate concept from group admin.
- **Pitfall (the NOT NULL trap):** an early version made `creator_user_id` NOT
  NULL as a guardrail — it broke account deletion (FK SET NULL → NOT NULL
  violation) AND cascaded failures in instant-link / follow-state / account tests.
  The invariant must live in `group_admins` + app code, never a NOT NULL on a
  SET-NULL-FK column.
- **Tests:** `server/tests/test_group_admins.py` (13: create-seeds-admin signed-in
  + anon auto-account, roster flags, promote + guards, boot + revoke + all
  negatives, auto-promote-on-leave, title/image admin-gating). Updated
  group-privacy / claim / invite / join-request tests for the creator→admin +
  auto-account-creator behavior changes.

---

## Explore Feed (migration 143)

The globe icon on home opens `/explore` — a "global group" surface where anyone can post anything. **For now visibility is scoped to the user's OWN polls** (they can't see others'); the "anyone sees anything" half is a deliberate future change. `/explore` lists the caller's explore polls top→bottom (NO To Do/Relevant/Old sections) above the SAME bottom create bar (the `#draft-poll-portal` + button + search box) that group pages have.

- **Representation = a per-user "explore group"** (a real `groups` row, `privacy='explore'`, owned by the creator). One per account, minted lazily on the first explore poll. This reuses ALL the group machinery (short_id, poll detail pages at `/g/<exploreShort>/p/<poll>`, category recency keyed on group_id) while keeping explore polls OUT of the regular surfaces. Migration 143 only widens the `groups_privacy_check` constraint to admit `'explore'`; everything else is app logic.
- **Members-only, like private.** `resolve_group_for_visit` + `grant_group_membership_inline` + `require_group_read_access` all treat `'explore'` exactly like `'private'` (no auto-join; strangers 404 the group URL). The creator is a member (the create path's post-commit `join_group`), so their own poll detail pages + the `/g/<exploreShort>` group root resolve.
- **Excluded from home.** `/api/groups/mine` + `/empty` filter out `privacy='explore'` member groups via `services/groups.py: explore_group_ids(conn, gids)`. So an explore poll never appears on home, and the explore group never shows as an "empty group."
- **Create flow:** `CreatePollRequest.explore=true` → `create_poll` resolves `req.group_id = get_or_create_explore_group(conn, creator_user_id)` (overriding any group_id, clearing group_title) BEFORE `_insert_poll`, so the poll lands in the caller's explore group. The group is created with `creator_user_id` = the account (auto-minted for anon, like any poll) + admin #1. `find_explore_group` / `get_or_create_explore_group` / `explore_group_ids` live in `services/groups.py`.
- **List endpoint:** `POST /api/groups/explore` → `ExploreResponse {group: GroupSummary | null, polls: PollResponse[]}`. Resolves the actor's account (`resolve_actor_user_id`), finds their explore group (None → empty feed), returns its polls via `polls_for_poll_ids` (newest-first, inline results). FE: `apiGetExplore()` in `lib/api/groups.ts` caches polls into a SEPARATE `explorePollsCache` (`lib/questionCache.ts`) — NEVER `accessiblePollsCache` (which feeds home), so explore polls can't leak into the home/group build.
- **Suggestion isolation (the "don't leak into specific groups" requirement) is bidirectional, server-side:**
  - The create box's "recent polls" rows (`searchSuggestions` in `app/create-poll/page.tsx`) source from `getCachedExplorePolls()` when `<body data-explore="1">` is set (the explore page sets it), else `getCachedAccessiblePolls()`. So explore poll titles and group poll titles never appear in each other's recents.
  - **Category recency** (`load_category_recency`) + **category options** (`load_category_options`) take an `explore: bool`. The cross-group `general` list EXCLUDES `privacy='explore'` groups for a regular request and RESTRICTS to them for an /explore request. The users-router endpoints derive `explore` from the resolved group's privacy (`_group_is_explore`). Verified live: explore page sees only restaurant (its category), regular group sees only video_game — no cross-bleed.
- **FE wiring:** the explore page (`app/explore/page.tsx`) sets `<body data-explore="1">` (always) + `<body data-group-id>` = explore group UUID (once known, for category scoping), renders the `#draft-poll-portal` div + reserves `var(--bubble-bar-panel-height)` bottom padding, and re-fetches the feed on `EXPLORE_POLL_CHANGED_EVENT` (fired by the create form after an explore create — the explore page isn't a group page, so it skips the optimistic POLL_PENDING/HYDRATED group-state flow entirely) + `visibilitychange`. The create form (`handleSubmitClick`) reads `data-explore` at submit time → sets `explore: true` + `group_id: null`, skips the placeholder/POLL_PENDING/accessible-cache writes, and merges the new poll into the explore cache + dispatches `EXPLORE_POLL_CHANGED` instead of navigating to detail (stays on /explore). The card title uses `questions[0].title` (NOT `poll.title`, which resolves to the group's "Explore" name override — same trap as the detail-page header).
- **Card layout = compact rows: vote count + title only** (`ExplorePollCard` in `components/ExploreFeed.tsx`). Each row is `vote count (muted, in front) · title` — NO category icon, NO author/date/views metadata, NO chevron. Vote count = `namedVoterCount`-equivalent (sum `voter_name_counts` with `voter_names`-length fallback) + `anonymous_count`, inlined to keep the heavy `VoterList` module out of the /explore bundle (same rationale as the inlined `EXPLORE_ROW_DIVIDER`). Rows stay tappable → `slideToPollDetail`. **Title color reflects per-device opened state:** strong (`text-gray-900`/`font-medium`) until this device has opened the poll detail page, faded (`text-gray-400`/`font-normal`) after — keyed on `getPollViewedAt(poll.id) > 0` (`lib/unread.ts`, the same view watermark `markPollViewed` writes on detail-page mount; NOT the full two-mode `computePollUnread`, just "opened or not"). `ExploreFeedList` subscribes to `POLL_VIEWED_CHANGED_EVENT` with a bare re-render tick so titles fade when returning from a poll (a minimal local `useState` tick, deliberately NOT `useUnreadReactivity` which adds unwanted badge-settings subscriptions).
- **`ExploreTitleBar` (shared, in `components/ExploreFeed.tsx`) pins the "Explore" title in a top bar.** `fixed` variant (live page) is `position: fixed; top:0; z-20` rendered into `#header-portal` via `HeaderPortal` — so it's a swipe-back transform target and slides with the page during the back gesture, sitting below the z-30 back button; the page's content div reserves `paddingTop: calc(env(safe-area-inset-top,0px) + 3.5rem)` (the bar's `h-14` row + safe-area). Unfixed variant (the `ExploreBackdropHost` snapshot) sits in-flow at the top of the contained box — reads identically at scroll 0. Shared so the page + backdrop can't drift (recurring "backdrop mirrors the live page" theme). The old in-flow `page-title-safe-top` title block is gone from both.
- **Poll-detail back + swipe-back return to /explore.** Tapping an explore card marks the detail page as explore-origin (`lib/pollDetailOrigin.ts: markPollDetailFromExplore`, read by `PollDetailView` via `isPollDetailFromExplore`). When set, the back button → `navigateWithTransition('/explore', 'back')` and the swipe-back gesture dispatches `SHOW/HIDE_EXPLORE_BACKDROP_EVENT` + commits to `/explore` (instead of `slideToGroupRoot` / the group backdrop). `components/ExploreBackdropHost` renders a read-only snapshot of the cached explore feed (via the shared `ExploreFeedList`/`ExplorePollCard` in `components/ExploreFeed.tsx`) underneath the sliding detail page — mirroring GroupBackdropHost/HomeBackdropHost. The `/explore` mount dismisses the backdrop + releases `setSwipeScrollbarLock(false)` (the commit-race cleanup). Normal (non-explore) group polls are unaffected — the marker is only set by the explore card.
- **Known v1 limitation:** the explore card's detail-page slide reuses the group route `/g/<exploreShort>/p/<poll>`, so directly opening the explore group's root URL `/g/<exploreShort>` still renders a real members-only group page (To Do/Relevant/Old tabs) — it shows the same own polls. Creating FROM that group-root URL attaches to the explore group via `group_id` but doesn't set the `explore` flag, so it can transiently add the poll to `accessiblePollsCache` until the next `/mine` refresh clears it; the primary /explore flow is leak-free.
- Tests: `server/tests/test_explore.py` (7: empty feed, create→appears, not-in-mine/empty, regular-not-in-explore, stranger-sees-nothing, polls-reuse-one-group, group-url-members-only). Regression: groups/polls/poll_categories/category_options suites stay green.

## Poll Variant Evolution (migration 144)

The /explore feed is a "natural selection" tree. A user-submitted explore poll (restricted to a **single yes/no question**) is the TRUNK of a binary **spine**. On submit it spawns **2 LLM-generated yes/no variants** — one rendered ABOVE the trunk (direction `'up'`), one BELOW (`'down'`). Each spawned variant, once it accrues `SPAWN_VOTE_THRESHOLD` distinct voters, spawns **1** further variant in its OWN direction (up grows up, down grows down), so the spine extends away from the trunk, generation by generation. The LLM is told to make variants *meaningfully different* (different angle/scope/stakes), not synonym rewrites.

- **Schema (migration 144, all on `polls`, all NULL/0 for ordinary polls):** `variant_parent_id` (poll spawned FROM; NULL = trunk), `variant_root_id` (the trunk; NULL on the trunk — the FE groups a spine by `variant_root_id ?? id`), `variant_direction` (`'up'`|`'down'`, NULL on trunk), `variant_generation` (0 = trunk, drives indentation), `variant_spawned` (idempotency/claim guard). These are `polls.*` columns, so they ride `_SELECT_POLLS_WITH_GROUP` → `_row_to_poll` → `PollResponse` → FE `Poll`/`toPoll` with no extra plumbing.
- **LLM client `services/variant_llm.py`** is provider-agnostic (OpenAI-compatible `/v1/chat/completions` — Ollama on the Mac mini in dev, model `qwen3:14b` (unified with poll suggestions onto ONE model — see the "AI Poll Suggestions" § bake-off note); any hosted provider in prod). Env: `POLL_VARIANT_LLM_URL` / `POLL_VARIANT_LLM_MODEL` / `POLL_VARIANT_LLM_API_KEY` (optional) / `POLL_VARIANT_LLM_TIMEOUT`. **Graceful no-config degradation** (`is_configured()` false → the feed just doesn't evolve, logged — like `RESEND_API_KEY`/`TMDB_API_KEY`). Sync `httpx.Client` (runs in a BackgroundTask threadpool, like `services/email.py`). `generate_variant_titles(base, count, *, avoid)` **tops up via up to `_MAX_ATTEMPTS=3` re-asks** (feeding what it has into `avoid`) because small local models often return fewer titles than requested — load-bearing for reliably getting the trunk's 2 children.
- **Spawn engine `services/poll_variants.py`.** `spawn_variants(poll_id)` is the single BackgroundTask entry point, called from `create_poll` (when `req.explore`, the trunk spawns its 2 immediately — no votes) AND `submit_poll_votes` (any not-yet-spawned poll in an explore group, gated cheaply on `group_privacy == 'explore' && !variant_spawned`). Three phases so the slow LLM call never holds a DB connection/lock: (1) `_gather_and_claim` runs the eligibility guards (explore privacy, single yes/no, threshold for variants, generation cap) then an **atomic claim** `UPDATE … SET variant_spawned=true WHERE … variant_spawned=false RETURNING` (the loser of a concurrent race sees 0 rows and bails), committed before generation; (2) generate with no conn held; (3) insert the child variant(s) reusing `_insert_poll`/`_insert_question` (lazy import, mirrors `services/recurrence.py: materialize_due_instances`) then UPDATE lineage. **On empty generation (transient LLM failure) the claim is REVERTED** so a later vote retries; on a hard insert failure the claim stays set (fail-closed, no retry loop). `SPAWN_VOTE_THRESHOLD` (env `POLL_VARIANT_VOTE_THRESHOLD`, default **1**) and `MAX_VARIANT_GENERATION` (default 5) bound it. The base title read for the LLM is the poll's question `title` (for a real FE yes/no submission `req.title` == the prompt == `questions[0].title`).
- **yes/no-only enforcement (two layers):** server `_validate_request` 400s an explore poll that isn't a single yes/no question; the create-poll search box, when `isExplore` (a `MutationObserver` on `EXPLORE_ATTR`, mirroring the `GROUP_ID_ATTR` one), collapses the suggestion list to the single yes/no interpretation AND the bare "+" button opens a yes/no draft.
- **FE spine rendering (`components/ExploreFeed.tsx: buildSpines`).** Groups polls by trunk, orders each spine **up-chain (deepest first) → trunk → down-chain**, indents each `ExplorePollCard` by `variant_generation` (`INDENT_STEP_REM`, plus a faint guide line in the channel; trunk is `font-semibold`). Spines ordered newest-trunk-first. A plain non-variant poll is a one-row spine. The swipe-back `ExploreBackdropHost` inherits this via the same `ExploreFeedList`.
- **Dev wiring:** `scripts/mac-mini/dev-server-manager.sh` points per-branch dev containers at the Mac Ollama (`-e POLL_VARIANT_LLM_URL=http://host.docker.internal:11434/v1/chat/completions`, model `qwen3:14b`, plus `--add-host host.docker.internal:host-gateway` so the alias resolves on the custom devbox network). Both env vars use `${VAR:-default}` so the Mac can override.
- **Canary (`latest`) IS wired — to the Mac Ollama via an auth'd Caddy route (June 2026).** The 1 GB droplets can't host a model, so the Mac host's Ollama is exposed at `https://ollama.dev.whoeverwants.com` and the canary `.env.api` carries `POLL_VARIANT_LLM_URL=https://ollama.dev.whoeverwants.com/v1/chat/completions` + `POLL_VARIANT_LLM_MODEL=qwen3:14b` + `POLL_VARIANT_LLM_API_KEY=<bearer>` + `POLL_VARIANT_LLM_TIMEOUT=120`. The route is a host-matcher fragment in `~/devbox/caddy.d/ollama.caddy` on the Mac (NOT git — it holds the bearer; the same token is the canary `POLL_VARIANT_LLM_API_KEY`), covered by the existing `*.dev` wildcard cert. Two load-bearing details: (1) gate auth with a **block-form** named matcher (`@ollama_ok { host …\n header Authorization "Bearer …" }`) — the single-line form `@m host X header Y "Z"` silently parses as a `host` matcher whose extra tokens become bogus hostnames (reload "OK", header check IGNORED → every request proxies through unauthed); (2) `reverse_proxy localhost:11434 { header_up Host localhost:11434 }` — Ollama 403s a foreign `Host` (DNS-rebind guard), so the upstream Host must be rewritten (the per-branch dev path hits `host.docker.internal:11434` directly and Ollama happens to accept that Host, so dev needs no rewrite). `.env.api` persists across canary redeploys (not git-tracked; env_file re-read on container (re)create) — re-add after a from-scratch droplet rebuild, like the other `.env.api` secrets. **Prod is still NOT wired** — same recipe (point `.env.api` at the Caddy route or a hosted provider) when wanted; else prod explore polls just don't evolve. The chosen path makes evolution depend on Mac uptime — if the Mac/Ollama is down, `_one_call` returns None and the spawn no-ops + reverts (self-heals on a later vote), so it degrades gracefully rather than erroring.
- **`_one_call` returns `None` on hard failure vs `[]` on a flaky empty parse.** `generate_variant_titles` bails on `None` (down/erroring endpoint — don't hammer it `_MAX_ATTEMPTS`×timeout) but RETRIES on `[]` (small local models like nous-hermes2 intermittently reply with nothing parseable). Before this, a single empty reply aborted all attempts, so the trunk's one create-time spawn often produced no variants until someone voted (the vote path retries `spawn_variants` on any un-spawned explore poll). Tests: `server/tests/test_poll_variants.py`.
- **Demo caveat:** the explore feed is keyed on the caller's account/browser, so a fresh browser sees an empty feed — seed the spine as a dev instant-sign-in account and open its `/auth/instant?token=…&next=/explore` URL to screenshot. Ollama serves one model serially, so concurrent gen-2 spawns queue (be patient / vote sequentially). Title quality is an LLM-quality limitation (now `qwen3:14b`, a marked improvement over the prior nous-hermes2) — tunable via a better model or the prompt in `variant_llm.py`.

---

## Invite Members (In-App Address Book)

From `/g/<id>/info`, an admin taps the small circular **`+` button in the Members section header** (same `w-7 h-7 rounded-full bg-blue-600` pattern as the Invite-links header's mint button — the earlier full-width "Add people" button is retired) to open `/g/<id>/invite-members` — a screen that searches the caller's contacts, selects accounts with round checkboxes (whole row tappable), and adds them on **Update** (gated on ≥1 selection, behind a `ConfirmationModal`). Added accounts get a push. Shipped on `claude/group-member-invitations-DuZXc` (migration 126).

- **Contacts are an account-to-account address book (migration 126, `user_contacts(owner_user_id, contact_user_id, first_seen_at, last_seen_at)`, PK `(owner, contact)`).** We track ACCOUNTS (user_id), not names — the display name is resolved per-account at read time. The relationship is PERSISTED so the invite screen can still surface (and recency-sort) someone the caller no longer shares any group with.
- **`last_seen_at` is the persisted recency watermark; "current shared-group count" is NOT stored — it's computed live** in `list_invitable_accounts`. The two power the sort: primary = current shared-group count desc (accounts you're in more groups with float to the top), secondary = `last_seen_at` desc (accounts with 0 current shared groups, most-recently-seen first). The query's `ORDER BY shared_group_count DESC, c.last_seen_at DESC, u.display_name ASC` gives exactly that (0-count rows sort after any positive count automatically).
- **`services/contacts.py: reconcile_contacts(conn, owner_user_id)` is the maintenance primitive** — one idempotent upsert of every account the owner CURRENTLY shares a group with, bumping `last_seen_at=NOW()`. It runs on the read paths the owner already hits: inline in the candidates endpoint, AND decoupled (via `reconcile_contacts_safe` as a `BackgroundTasks` task) on `POST /api/groups/mine`. **Reconcile-on-`/mine` is the load-bearing always-running hook** — it's what captures the encounter + recency WHILE the owner still shares the group, so a contact survives both of them leaving it later. Reconcile-on-screen-open alone wouldn't suffice (someone who never opens the invite screen would never record contacts). Deliberately NOT wired into the vote/visit/approve/redeem membership-write paths — whatever the owner currently shares gets captured on their next home load.
- **Account-aware on both sides.** "The owner's memberships" unions every browser linked to the owner's account (mirroring `load_user_visibility`), and a contact's memberships union every browser linked to the contact's account. Same `browser_id IN (SELECT browser_id FROM user_browsers WHERE user_id = ...)` pattern used everywhere else.
- **Endpoints in `routers/groups.py`** (between the join-request and Phase-G-invite sections):
  - `GET /{route_id}/invitable-accounts` → `InvitableAccountResponse[]` (`{user_id, name, shared_group_count, last_seen_at}`). Reconciles the caller inline, then reads. **Authorization is MEMBERSHIP (`is_caller_member_of_group`), not creator-only** — consistent with the existing model where any member can already invite anyone by sharing the group URL. An account-less caller (no resolvable `user_id` via `resolve_actor_user_id`) returns `[]` rather than erroring (they have no contacts). 404 unresolvable route, 403 non-member.
  - `POST /{route_id}/members` body `{user_ids: [...]}` → `{added: N}`. Each id must (a) be a valid uuid (`_is_uuid_like`, skipped silently otherwise — NOT 404'd, so one bad id doesn't fail the batch), (b) NOT be the caller, and (c) be in the caller's `user_contacts` (`is_contact`) — so a member can't add an arbitrary stranger by guessing user_ids. `add_member_for_user` writes the `group_members` row keyed on the account's earliest-linked browser_id (same pattern as `decide_request`), returning `True` only when a NEW membership was established (already-a-member via any browser → skipped, no dup, no notification). Newly-added accounts get a `fan_out_to_user` push.
- **`fan_out_to_user(group_id, user_id, payload)` in `services/push.py`** is the generic "push one group-scoped notification to one account's browsers" primitive (renamed from `fan_out_member_added` once a third caller — plus-ones — appeared; callers build their own payloads). Targets `user_browsers WHERE user_id = user_id` (one specific account, not the group at large), gated on the same default-ON `notify_new_poll` pref as `fan_out_join_request` (a fresh member has no pref row → ON). Byte-for-byte the join-request fan-out shape. For the member-added case the payload is: title `Added to <group_phrase>` (via `group_name_phrase`), body `<inviter> added you to the group` (falls back to "You were added to a group" when the inviter has no display_name), `url: /g/<route>` (path form, not `?p=`).
- **FE: new slide-overlay kind `groupInviteMembers`** (`lib/eventChannels.ts` union + `lib/slideOverlay.tsx` `renderForKind` case + `slideToGroupInviteMembers` helper). The route `app/g/[groupShortId]/invite-members/page.tsx` exports the prop-driven `GroupInviteMembersView` (mirrors the `GroupInfoView` split). It is NOT a group-root view (`isGroupRootView` doesn't match it), so it gets the plain `max-w-4xl mx-auto px-4 pb-6` layout like `/info` / `/edit-title`. Back arrow → `slideToGroupInfo({direction:'back'})`.
- **Pitfall: a left-aligned `<h1>` at the top of a subroute collides with the floating `<HeaderPortal>` back/action buttons.** `/info` avoids this because its first content is a CENTERED hero (the buttons float beside it). The invite screen's left-aligned "Add people" title needed `paddingTop: calc(env(safe-area-inset-top, 0px) + 3.5rem)` (vs `/info`'s 1.05rem) to clear the ~40px button row. Any future subroute whose first element is left-aligned text at the top needs the larger top padding, not the hero-page value.
- **Round-checkbox markup is copied from `GroupListItem.tsx`'s selection checkbox** (`w-6 h-6 rounded-full border-2`, blue-fill + white check when selected). Each candidate row is a full-width `<button>` (whole line tappable) containing checkbox + `InitialBubble` avatar + name. Other accounts render initials only (`useMyUserImageUrl` resolves the CURRENT user's photo only — cross-user images are out of scope).
- **FE clients in `lib/api/groups.ts`**: `apiGetGroupInvitableAccounts(routeId)` + `apiAddGroupMembers(routeId, userIds)` (+ `InvitableAccount` type), re-exported from `lib/api/index.ts`. Search is purely client-side name filtering over the server-pre-sorted list (order preserved); the list is bounded by "people you share groups with" so no server `?q=` search endpoint was needed.
- **Direct-add semantics (not invite-they-accept).** The added account becomes a member immediately + gets notified — there is no pending-acceptance step (that's what Phase F join-requests are for, in the opposite direction). If an accept-first flow is ever wanted, it's a different shape; don't bolt it onto this endpoint.
- Tests: `server/tests/test_invite_members.py` (candidates exclude members + self, count-then-recency sort, member-gate 403, only-own-contacts addable, idempotent add, 404 route). Test setup writes `group_members` rows directly + sets `display_name` via DB rather than driving the full join/name flow.

---

## Solo-Group CTAs + Invite Auto-Copy

When the viewer is a group's **admin AND its only member** (a freshly-created group), the group page renders two tinted pill CTAs — **Add People** (blue) and **Create Invite Link** (emerald) — inline in the cards-wrapper flow, just below the "Scheduled ›" link and above any polls. They dismiss themselves the moment someone else joins. Shipped on `claude/new-group-empty-state-ytsszl`.

- **Solo check = roster-based with a client-side pre-filter** (`soloAdmin` effect in `GroupPage.tsx`). `group.participantNames`/`anonymousRespondentCount` (poll voters, viewer excluded) gate the round-trip: any OTHER participant ⇒ can't be solo ⇒ no fetch. Otherwise `apiGetGroupMembers(groupId)` decides via the shared `rosterIsSolo(roster)` predicate: `viewer_is_admin && members.length + anonymous_count <= 1`. Refetches on `GROUP_MEMBERS_CHANGED_EVENT` (the Add People screen slides back over the still-mounted page) + `visibilitychange` (someone may redeem the invite while backgrounded) — but the visibility refetch **quiesces once a roster proves the group non-solo** (the pre-filter can't see members who never voted, so without the quiesce every refocus would refire the GET forever). The effect mirrors `/info`'s roster wiring — keep in lockstep; extract a shared `useGroupRoster` hook at the 3rd copy. `apiGetGroupMembers` is in-flight `coalesced()` (the overlay handoff double-mounts GroupContent/Info, so both instances share one `/members` GET).
- **`soloAdmin` is seeded SYNCHRONOUSLY from a resolved-roster cache so the CTAs render on the FIRST commit** — without it the CTAs visibly **appeared mid-slide → disappeared → reappeared** on iOS (the reported flicker). Root cause: the overlay-slide handoff mounts `GroupContent` twice; with `soloAdmin` starting `false` and only flipped by the async `/members` fetch, the overlay instance's fetch resolved → CTAs appeared, then the fresh real-route instance mounted at `false` → CTAs dropped, then ITS fetch resolved → reappeared. Fix: `apiGetGroupMembers` now writes every resolved roster (incl. the 404→`EMPTY_ROSTER` case) into a bounded-LRU `groupRosterCache` (`lib/api/groups.ts`, **no TTL** — it's a "last-resolved" snapshot the effect always refreshes on mount, so a TTL would only re-introduce null-on-hit flicker; deliberately NOT routed through `questionCache.ts: setLru`, which is `CacheEntry`+TTL-shaped). `getCachedGroupRoster(routeId)` is the getter; `GroupContent` seeds `soloAdmin` in its `useState` initializer via `rosterIsSolo(cached)` (gated on `maybeSoloGroup` so the seed's SSR/hydration profile matches the existing `group` cache-seed). So the real-route instance shows CTAs on first commit, and any repeat visit (warm cache) shows them from the first frame of the transition. **General rule (recurring in this codebase):** any chrome gated on an async fetch that renders inside a double-mounted overlay/backdrop instance must be seedable synchronously from a cache, or it flickers at the handoff — same pattern as the swipe-back first-commit seeds (`buildGroupSyncFromCache`, `useState`-lazy-init) and the home-backdrop globe gate below.
- **`SlideToGroupDetail.chainTo` is the first-class "play a follow-up slide once this one lands" mechanism** (`lib/eventChannels.ts` + the host's unmount timer in `lib/slideOverlay.tsx`). The url-matched unmount branch dispatches `chainTo` instead of `setState(null)`; the safety-timeout branch deliberately DROPS the chain (if slide 1 never landed, playing leg 2 compounds the failure). The Add People CTA uses it — `slideToGroupInfo({ groupId, chainTo: groupInviteMembersSlideDetail({ groupId }) })` — so the history/back chain matches the manual path (invite-members' back returns to /info). **Don't sequence consecutive slides with a caller-side `setTimeout(SLIDE_DURATION_MS + slack)`** — that was the first implementation; the host owns the real "slide landed" signal (pathname match + duration), and the timing guess breaks on slow route commits. Detail-builder pattern: `groupInviteMembersSlideDetail(opts)` is exported alongside `slideToGroupInviteMembers` so callers can construct the chain leg; mirror it when another kind needs chaining.
- **Invite creation ALWAYS auto-copies the fresh URL + flashes "Copied!"** — both the /info `+` button and the group-page CTA. `lib/inviteCreation.ts: startInviteCreation(groupId)` is the single mint+copy implementation; `lib/clipboard.ts: copyTextFromPromise(textPromise)` is the clipboard primitive. **The iOS constraint that shapes it all: `navigator.clipboard.writeText` is rejected outside the tap's user-activation window, and the URL only exists after the mint API resolves.** `ClipboardItem` accepts promise-valued entries for exactly this — the write is REGISTERED synchronously in the tap handler and the browser fills the text in when the promise resolves. So `startInviteCreation` MUST be called synchronously inside the tap. Fallback routes through `copyTextToClipboard`'s layered ladder (writeText → hidden-textarea execCommand); resolves boolean, never rejects. Flash durations: manual Copy tap 1800ms, auto-copy 4000ms (visible after the page transition that precedes it).
