The template wrapper at `app/template.tsx` no longer adds a `paddingBottom: '0.5rem'` for `isGroupLikePage` — that buffer was for the OLD in-flow bubble bar and surfaced as an 8px gap below the last card once the bar floated. `slideOverlay`'s group-kind `innerStyle` and `GroupBackdropHost`'s wrapper similarly dropped their 0.5rem so the overlay → real-route handoff doesn't shift content. Historical: an earlier in-flow bubble bar set padding to 0.5rem flat; an even earlier iteration set it to 4.5rem (72px) as iOS Safari URL bar clearance but read as a layout bug on every other surface.
- **Per-second `setState` in a countdown component causes Firefox iOS scroll jitter at scroll edges.** When ~15+ countdown spans each re-render every second via `setTimeLeft(...)`, Firefox iOS momentum scrolling near the top edge compensates scrollY by +200-230px in a single frame (a single-frame snap, not a smooth bounce). The React reconciliation pass triggered by the setState — even if the DOM diff is just a text-node swap — trips a layout event that FxiOS treats as reason to adjust `scrollY`. Fix: update countdown text imperatively via a ref (`span.textContent = ...` inside `setInterval`) so React never re-renders. Both `components/GroupList.tsx` and the inner `SimpleCountdown` in `app/group/[groupId]/page.tsx` use this pattern. Safari iOS doesn't exhibit the bug, but the ref-based approach is also more efficient.
- **Diagnosing weird scroll behavior: instrument scrollY with a client-log tracer.** When user-reported "jitter" doesn't reproduce in Playwright (chromium + touch simulation can't replicate iOS momentum + URL-bar physics), add a temporary `window.addEventListener('scroll', () => console.log(...))` that records `scrollY`, `scrollHeight`, and `innerHeight` with timestamps via the existing client log forwarder. The user reproduces the issue once on their real device; the buffer captures the per-frame numbers. Finding a single-frame `dy > 100` with stable `scrollHeight`/`innerHeight` → something's programmatically adjusting scrollY (anchoring, max-clamp, browser compensation). `dy` tracking scrollHeight/innerHeight changes → layout-driven. This is how both the iOS Safari URL-bar bug and the FxiOS countdown-setState bug were nailed down — without the tracer, both looked identical visually.

### BubbleBarHost + BubbleBarPanel (Floating Bubble Bar)

> **UPDATE (June 2026, `claude/poll-ui-transition-timing-66Jdl`): the create-poll bar's `#draft-poll-portal` target moved BACK INTO the page content so the bar SLIDES WITH the page during transitions. `components/BubbleBarHost.tsx`, `components/BubbleBarPanel.tsx`, `lib/groupAccessState.ts`, and `lib/useGroupBackdropActive.ts` are DELETED; `PANEL_HEIGHT_VAR` moved to `lib/groupDomMarkers.ts`.** The layout-level `BubbleBarHost` (a single `position: fixed` element decoupled from page content) could only make the bar APPEAR/DISAPPEAR — a fixed element at the body level can't inherit a slide/swipe transform, so the bar popped in/out instantly instead of moving with the page. Fix: render `<div id={DRAFT_POLL_PORTAL_ID} className="relative z-40" />` INSIDE `GroupContent`'s loaded-with-access main return (`app/g/[groupShortId]/GroupPage.tsx`) and inside `EmptyPlaceholder` (`app/g/page.tsx`), as a body-level sibling of the swipe wrapper. The portaled bar is `position: fixed`, so its containing block is whatever box the page is moving in — exactly the fixed-`GroupHeader` mechanism:
>   - **Slide overlay (home→group, back-to-group via button):** the overlay's GroupContent renders the portal inside the overlay's `contain: strict` box, so the fixed bar resolves to the overlay and SLIDES IN with the group content. No manual transform/z-elevation — the bar inherits the overlay's z-60 + transform.
>   - **Poll→group swipe-back:** `GroupBackdropHost`'s GroupContent renders the portal inside the z-0 `contain: strict` backdrop, so the bar paints at the backdrop's z-0 level (BELOW the z-1 sliding poll page) and is REVEALED under it as the poll slides off.
>   - **Group→home swipe:** the portal node is body-level (a sibling of the swipe wrapper, NOT inside it — so the focused full-screen picker still layers above the z-20 header / z-30 commit badge in the viewport context). To make it slide off WITH the page it's added to the swipe gesture's `extraTargets` (`barPortalRef`), alongside the header. **PITFALL (the load-bearing `relative z-40`):** applying a `transform` to a STATIC div makes it a new stacking context at z-auto, which lands BELOW the z-0 home backdrop that mounts at swipe start — so the bar painted behind the opaque backdrop and VANISHED the instant the drag began. Giving the portal div `position: relative; z-40` makes it a stacking context ABOVE the backdrop in every state (incl. while transformed); `position: relative` doesn't trap the fixed bar (only transform/contain do), so the overlay-slide + backdrop-reveal cases are unchanged. `useSwipeBackGesture`'s `extraTargets` JSDoc now documents this requirement for any future body-portaled target. Verified via droplet Playwright by DOM-containment (bar `input` is a descendant of the z-60 overlay during the slide; inside the z-0 backdrop and NOT the z-1 poll wrapper during swipe-back; and during a simulated group→home swipe its `left` translates with the applied transform AND `elementFromPoint` still returns the bar, not the backdrop).
>   - **Access gating is now structural** — no more `groupAccessState` module store / `setGroupAccessGranted` effect / `useGroupAccessGranted`. GroupContent's loading spinner + no-access `GroupNotFoundFallback` early-returns simply OMIT the portal, so the bar can't appear on the wall; only the loaded-with-access main return renders it.
>   - **Multi-portal coincidence is unchanged:** CreateQuestionContent still renders the bar into EVERY `#draft-poll-portal` (the existing MutationObserver + `entriesShallowEqual` + WeakMap-keyed list). During the post-overlay-commit overlap two identical fixed pills coincide; during the slide proper only one portal exists (the source route has none). The `draftPollPortals` dep on the height-measuring ResizeObserver effect is LOAD-BEARING (re-attaches the observer when the bar remounts into a fresh portal node on a route change) — don't drop it.
>   - **The bullets below about `BubbleBarHost`/`BubbleBarPanel`, "no bar DURING the transition / withheld then slide-up", `slideActive`/`homeBackdrop` visibility gating, and the layout-level single instance are HISTORICAL — superseded by this note.**

> **UPDATE (June 2026, `claude/poll-creation-interface-8x3rD`): the bubble-button bar was REPLACED by a search-box poll creator.** The horizontally-scrolling category buttons (`BUBBLE_BUTTON_CLASS` / `BUBBLE_BUTTON_STYLE` / `BUBBLE_ICON_STYLE` / `BUBBLE_TITLE_STYLE` / `BUBBLE_*_PX` constants) are DELETED; the rounded-rectangle bubble description below is HISTORICAL. The bar is now an always-visible pill **text input** (`pollSearchBar` in `app/create-poll/page.tsx`) pinned to the bottom (NO hide-on-scroll — the panel's scroll-hide subsystem was removed). Tapping it raises the keyboard and expands a full-screen, keyboard-aware suggestion list ABOVE the bar:
>   - **Keyboard-aware layout**: the focused container is `position: fixed` pinned to the visual viewport (`top: vv.offsetTop; height: vv.height`) so its bottom edge lands flush on the keyboard's top and the input bar (its last child) sits just above it. ONE `<input>` (never reparented across the focus toggle) so focus/keyboard survive. `searchVv` tracked via a `visualViewport` resize/scroll listener that's attached **only while focused** (the component is layout-persistent, so an always-on listener would re-render on every scroll on every page). The unfocused container is bottom-anchored `{bottom:0}` auto-height (just the pill).
>   - **Bottom-anchored, best match nearest the bar**: the list `flex-col` + an inner `mt-auto` wrapper stacks rows UP from just above the bar (collapses to 0 on overflow so the top stays scrollable); an effect re-pins scroll to the bottom on focus / suggestion change / vv change. Order top→bottom: **Yes/No** (frames the literal text as a yes/no question), **filtered categories** (built-ins filtered by typed-token prefix match, reversed so the best match is at the bottom of the group), **Options** (when the text parses into ≥2 comma/"or"-delimited options → fixed-options poll), **Context** (`{category:'custom', forField:<typed text>}` → "Options for &lt;text&gt;?", interpreting the input as the poll's CONTEXT rather than its category; shown ONLY when the raw text has no standalone "for" yet — guard is `!/\bfor\b/i.test(raw)`, NOT `!context`, since "Birthday for" yields empty context but must still suppress the row; sits just ABOVE Custom), **Custom** (bottom, next to the bar — a custom-category poll named after the typed text, tagged "custom"). A trailing **"for …"** clause sets the context (`forField`) on every suggestion (shown as a muted "for …" suffix); the yes/no row keeps the literal text as its prompt. Helpers `parseForContext` / `parseOptionsFromText` (module scope) + the `searchSuggestions` useMemo.
>   - **Category matching + suggestion ordering were reworked into three pure modules (`claude/poll-textbox-ux-review-64wfgf`).** The OLD matcher required EVERY typed token to prefix-match a category word, so natural sentences ("movie night", "where should we eat") matched NO category and fell through to a generic poll; the generic "Context" row was the nearest-bar default for ~80% of natural input. Measured on a labeled corpus the box went from ~18% default-correct / ~42% recall to ~95% / ~98%.
>     - **`lib/categoryMatch.ts` is the SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH for category triggers** (pure, React-free, SSR-safe). `CATEGORY_DEFS` = the 6 searchable categories (restaurant, movie, video_game, time, location, showtime) in precedence order, each with `keywords`. Matching is **any-token, stop-word-filtered, ranked**: `tokenizeSubject` drops `STOP_WORDS` (generic fillers — a test asserts they're disjoint from every trigger), then `scoreCategory`/`scoreCategoryLabel`/`rankCategories`/`topCategory` score each category (label hit = 2, alias keyword = 1; label-score breaks ties so exact-label beats alias; `CATEGORY_ORDER` then recency break further ties). Hot-path detail: label words are precomputed in a module `LABEL_WORDS` Map and `scoreBoth` computes the full + label score in ONE pass (runs per keystroke). Adding a trigger is a one-line edit to `CATEGORY_DEFS.keywords`.
>     - **`detectCategory`/`decidePoll` (`lib/pollTextParse.ts`, the Siri-mirrored parser) now DELEGATE to `topCategory`** — unifying the box and Siri on one matcher. The existing JS↔Swift fixture (`poll-parse-cases.json`) stays green. **The Swift `PollTextParser` (now `ios/App/App/PollTextParser.swift`) was PORTED to mirror `categoryMatch.ts`** (`categoryDefs` / `stopWords` / `tokenizeSubject` / `singular` / `tokenHits` / `scoreBoth` + a ranked `detectCategory` = `topCategory`: score desc → label-score desc → precedence; recency tie-break is web-box-only and NOT mirrored). `decide()`'s precedence is unchanged — only the category lookup under it changed. The four natural-sentence wins (`dinner tonight`→restaurant, `movie night`→movie, `pick a game`→video_game, `where to eat`→restaurant) were added to `poll-parse-cases.json` once both sides agreed. Kept the Swift `stopWords` set byte-identical to `STOP_WORDS`; the JS test pins the no-trigger-overlap invariant on the JS side, and the Swift side is behaviorally pinned in CI by the shared-fixture harness `scripts/ios/test-parser.sh` (only on-device TestFlight verification of the spoken path remains — see `docs/poll-textbox-followups.md` TODO 1). `startsWithYesNoStem` is a new box-only export.
>     - **`lib/pollSuggestions.ts: planPollSuggestions` is the pure planner** that decides WHICH rows + their ORDER (single source of truth). The most-specific PRESENT interpretation is nearest the bar (precedence mirrors `decidePoll`: options ≥2 → yes/no stem → category → temporal-Time → custom; the temporal→Time step is **box-only** — Siri has no temporal capability, don't "align" it away or scheduling input regresses). Generic Custom/Context are the fallback floor, never the default. `app/create-poll/page.tsx: searchSuggestions` and the committed scoring harness BOTH consume this planner, so the box can't drift from what the tests measure. The box memo only maps each `PlannedRow` → display (icon + annotated segments + form overrides) and splices recents above the primary. Empty input returns the category-browse menu.
>     - **`components/TypeFieldInput.tsx: categoryMatchesQuery`/`categoryLabelMatchesQuery` now delegate to `scoreCategory`/`scoreCategoryLabel`** (kept for the dropdown + `category-search.test.ts`); the empty-tokens→true special case lives there. `BUILT_IN_TYPES` no longer carries a `keywords` field (matching data moved to `categoryMatch.ts`).
>     - **`tests/fixtures/poll-suggestion-corpus.ts` is a REUSABLE benchmark substrate** — a versioned labeled corpus (bucket-tagged) + classifier-agnostic scorers (`scoreTopChoice` default-correctness, `scoreRecall`, `intentMatches` with ambiguity-tolerant `accept` sets). `Prediction = {kind, category?}` so a PlannedRow, a decidePoll result, OR a future AI classifier's JSON all plug in. The CI gate (`poll-suggestion-scoring.test.ts`) consumes it; AI/prompt development grades against the heuristic baseline on the same data. Grow it with long-tail buckets (slang/typos/compound/multilingual).
>     - **Follow-ups: `docs/poll-textbox-followups.md`** — (1) mirror `categoryMatch.ts` into the Siri Swift parser; (2) augment the heuristic with a small on-device (iOS `NLEmbedding` / web `transformers.js`) or Mac-mini AI classifier (AUGMENT, never block — the deterministic planner stays the instant/offline default). The prod droplet (1 GB) can't host a model; on-device is the prod path, Mac mini is for prototyping/eval.
>     - **Embedding category ranker — PROTOTYPED + WIRED (dev/canary), `claude/poll-suggestion-embedding-ranker-l6wrx8`.** Follow-up (2) above, shipped behind a host gate. An on-device embedding classifier (`lib/aiCategoryClassify.ts`) embeds the typed subject and cosine-ranks it against prototype phrases per category (`lib/categoryPrototypes.ts`) so slang/typos/novel phrasings surface a built-in category the keyword matcher (`lib/categoryMatch.ts`) has no trigger word for. **Augment, never block:** a confident hint feeds `planPollSuggestions(..., { aiCategory })` which ADDS a category row nearest the bar (just above the primary) and NEVER changes the heuristic's default; every failure path (SSR, disabled host, model/network error, timeout, still-loading) returns null → planner no-op → box behaves exactly as today. Key decisions/pitfalls:
>       - **Model = `Xenova/bge-small-en-v1.5` (q8, ~30 MB), English-only.** Benchmarked in `prototypes/poll-classify/` (the eval substrate: `bench.mts` imports the REAL planner as baseline, validated to reproduce on all 132 cases, and swaps only the category step). On the grown corpus (core 77 + long-tail 55): baseline 75.8% default / 76.5% recall → bge **83.3% (+7.6) / 92.4% (+15.9)**; lift concentrated in slang (29%→59%) + typo (33%→53%); **compound stays 100%** (structure-dominated — the embedder correctly never overrides options/yes-no/temporal). Multilingual needs a multilingual model (out of scope this rollout; `paraphrase-multilingual-MiniLM-L12` → 92% multilingual if added later). Latency p50 ~2 ms / p95 ~4 ms on a server CPU (more on real phones, still far under the 300 ms debounce).
>       - **transformers.js is loaded from a CDN ESM URL at RUNTIME, NOT an npm dep.** `import(/* webpackIgnore: true */ /* turbopackIgnore: true */ url)` with a VARIABLE arg (`importTransformers()` in `aiCategoryClassify.ts`) — TS types it `Promise<unknown>` (no TS2307, no `@ts-expect-error` that could become an unused-directive error), and the magic comments keep BOTH webpack + turbopack from bundling it (its Node-only `onnxruntime-node`/`sharp` deps would break the client build). Zero `package.json`/`next.config.ts` change → zero bundler-config risk (the reason this path was chosen over npm-bundling). Inference runs on-device (WASM/CPU); only the lib+model BYTES are fetched + Cache-API-cached; the typed text never leaves the device (privacy preserved). `env.backends.onnx.wasm.numThreads = 1` (iOS WebViews lack the cross-origin isolation threads need). No CSP in the app, so the native dynamic import is safe (no `unsafe-eval`).
>       - **`classifyCategory()` AWAITS the full model load, it does NOT probe-timeout.** The box consumes it fully async (300 ms debounce + a latest-wins `cancelled` guard), so awaiting the ~30 MB load never blocks the UI. **Pitfall fixed on-device:** an initial 60 ms load-probe returned null while the model was still downloading, and the box's effect fires ONCE per query change — so the single "frag night" call missed the not-yet-ready model and never retried for the unchanged query (the control "feed me" only worked because it ran 40 s later). A not-ready/timeout return is NOT cached, so the single per-query call resolves once the (focus-warmed) model is up. `warmAiCategoryClassifier()` fires on box focus so the model is usually ready by the time the user pauses.
>       - **Rollout gate `isAiCategoryClassifyEnabled()` = browser AND (localhost / `*.dev.whoeverwants.com` / `latest.whoeverwants.com` / `whoeverwants.com`) — NOW ON EVERYWHERE INCL. PROD** (enabled after the canary-validation follow-up; the prod host was the last to flip). To roll back, drop the `whoeverwants.com` clause — the feature is fail-safe so removing it just forgoes the extra suggestion row. **Canary-validated end-to-end** via droplet Chromium against live `latest.whoeverwants.com` (a fast-network FLOOR — real phones on cellular are slower): "frag night" → Video Game, "feed me" → Restaurant, "moive night" → Movie (all correct, keyword-matcher misses); cold first-use ~10.6 s for the AI row to appear (transformers.js CDN + ~30 MB model + WASM init + inference — the box is fully usable throughout, only the AI row is delayed), warm ~0.6–0.9 s/query incl. the 300 ms debounce; zero page errors; one benign `QuotaExceeded` Cache-API warning in the ephemeral headless context (caches fine on real devices, worst case re-downloads). **Fail-safe confirmed** by re-running with the model CDN/Hub blocked: the box renders identical heuristic rows, no AI row, no errors. The one real cost is the ~30 MB model download per device on first poll-box focus (`warmAiCategoryClassifier`); could not be measured on a real phone here.
>       - **Corpus growth (`tests/fixtures/poll-suggestion-corpus.ts`):** a SEPARATE non-CI-gated `POLL_SUGGESTION_LONGTAIL` (+`_FULL`) export of 55 hard cases (slang/typo/compound/multilingual) — the heuristic is EXPECTED to fail there, so it's kept out of the core CI gate (`POLL_SUGGESTION_CORPUS` unchanged). The planner-merge contract (adds-above-primary, never-overrides, dedup vs keyword, no-op when absent) is pinned PURELY (no model) in `tests/__tests__/poll-suggestions-aihint.test.ts`. To re-tune the threshold (`AI_CATEGORY_MIN_SCORE`, 0.45 for bge) or compare models, run `prototypes/poll-classify/bench.mts`.
>   - **Segment labelling (`claude/poll-suggestion-labels-jTjOh`)**: each suggestion's text is split into typed `SuggestionSegment[]` (module-level type + `contextSegments(context)` helper, both above `CreateQuestionContent`) so the words that get PREFILLED show a tiny uppercase label over the left edge + a matching-color underline (`border-b-2`). Fixed palette (module consts, literal Tailwind class strings so JIT keeps them): **green** `SEG_CATEGORY`, **purple** `SEG_CONTEXT`, and a cycling `OPTION_SEG_COLORS` (red/blue/gold/pink/teal/orange) one per option. Colors are softened (`text-{c}-500/80 dark:text-{c}-400/80` + `border-{c}-400/50`). Per type: yes/no → one plain unlabeled segment (the text becomes the TITLE, not a bucket); category → `[{label:Category}, ...contextSegments]`; options → one `{label:Option}` per parsed option (cycling color) interleaved with muted `·` + `...contextSegments`; custom → `[{label:Category subject}, ...contextSegments]` or a bare unlabeled `New Poll`. Connectives (`for`, `·`) are `muted` (gray, no label). Render: the segments `<span>` is `relative ... overflow-hidden whitespace-nowrap` with `pt-3` ONLY when a labeled segment is present; each labeled segment is `relative inline-block` with the label `absolute left-0 bottom-full` (sits inside the `pt-3` zone so overflow-hidden, which clips long text on the right, doesn't clip it). `segments` is the SOLE render source — the suggestion objects no longer carry `primary`/`context` (the aria-label derives from `segments.map(s=>s.text).join('')`). Palette is intentionally NOT lifted to `lib/` (single consumer; the codebase uses ad-hoc color strings everywhere).
>   - **Selecting a row** → `chooseSuggestion(overrides)` → name-gate (stashes a retry THUNK in `pendingSearchAction` for `AccountGateModal`, since suggestions carry a full draft prefill — title/options/context — not just a category) → `openModalWithDraft(overrides)` which layers the overrides over `emptyDraft()` and opens the new-poll modal prefilled. `openModalFor`/`handleBubbleClick`/`pendingBubbleCategory` (category-only) were retired in favor of these.
>   - **The ✕ on the left of the bar** (`dismissSearch`) collapses back to the bottom pill; tapping outside / blurring the input also collapses.
>   - **Body scroll is locked while the bar is focused (`useBodyScrollLock(isModalOpen || searchFocused, false)`).** Focusing the input raises the soft keyboard / shrinks the visual viewport, which drifts the underlying group page's document scroll even though the picker is `position: fixed` — so cancelling left the group scrolled away from where it was (reported bug). The lock snapshots `scrollY` on focus and restores it via `window.scrollTo` on dismiss. `locked` stays true across the focus → modal-open transition (`searchFocused` flips false as `isModalOpen` flips true in the same render batch), so picking a suggestion doesn't teardown/restore-flicker the scroll. Don't gate the lock on `isModalOpen` alone — the focused-but-no-modal picker state needs it too.
>   - **Input caret**: the input carries `style={{ lineHeight: 'normal' }}` (NOT Tailwind `text-base`'s 1.5rem, NOT `leading-none`) — iOS Safari draws the caret on the font's natural metrics, so a custom line-height desyncs the caret from the placeholder/text (caret sat too low). `normal` makes them share metrics (default-input behavior).
>   - **Access gating (`lib/groupAccessState.ts`)**: the bar must NOT appear on the "Private Group / no access" wall. A module store (subscribe/`useSyncExternalStore`, mirrors `useHomeBackdropActive`) records the route id of the most recently loaded-with-access group; `GroupContent` calls `setGroupAccessGranted(groupId)` once it's loaded WITHOUT error (the wall — `error || !group` — never reaches that). `BubbleBarHost` shows the bar on `/g/<id>` (id via `extractGroupRouteId`) only when access is granted for THAT id (default hidden-until-confirmed avoids a flash on walls + stale-grant leaks across groups); the bare `/g` placeholder (no id) is always allowed. No unmount cleanup of the store (same overlay-double-mount reasoning as `data-group-id`); staleness handled by the route-id match.
>   - `BubbleBarPanel` is now a trivial portal-target div (`<div id={DRAFT_POLL_PORTAL_ID}/>`, NO transform so the portaled `fixed` bar is viewport-relative); it still exports `PANEL_HEIGHT_VAR` (the cards-wrapper bottom padding) which `CreateQuestionContent` writes from the measured unfocused bar height. `PANEL_OFFSET_VAR` was deleted (nothing read it after the down scroll-arrow was retired).
>   - **UPDATE (June 2026, `claude/poll-suggestion-text-box-GpJHt`): suggestions render the REAL auto-title; the bottom bar is split into a circular +/✕ button + a separate text box; recently-posted poll titles are offered as reuse suggestions; the right-side pill chips and the empty "New Poll" row are GONE.** Specifics superseding the two bullets above:
>     - **Every row's text IS the title the poll would land on**, derived from the same `overrides` the row prefills — so the displayed text can never drift from the created poll. The single source of truth is `draftTitleSegments(d)` in `createPollHelpers.ts` (`deriveDraftTitle` is now literally `draftTitleSegments(d).map(s=>s.text).join('')`, proven byte-identical to the old generator across 1440 cases): it returns typed `TitleSegment[]` (`kind: 'plain'|'category'|'context'|'option'`, `muted?`, `optionIndex?`). `page.tsx: overridesToSegments(overrides)` fills a minimal `TitleDraft` from a row's overrides → `draftTitleSegments` → `annotateSegments` (maps `kind`→the `SEG_CATEGORY`/`SEG_CONTEXT`/`OPTION_SEG_COLORS` UI spans; muted glue greys out; plain prompt is bare). The old per-type inline segment building + `contextSegments` helper + the `· ` option separator are REPLACED — options now read as the actual or-list ("Dune, Oppenheimer, or Barbie for movie night?") via `orListSegments` (mirrors `buildOrList`'s truncation, minus the trailing "?"). When adding a category to `draftTitleSegments`, the suggestion rows pick it up automatically.
>     - **Recently-posted poll titles** (annotated) sit at the bottom of the list, just above the bar. `pollToRecentEntry(poll)` reconstructs `overrides` + the annotated `titleText` from a poll's question (SINGLE-question polls only — multi-question titles can't be cleanly annotated from one section; yes_no special-cased to its prompt; icon via the shared `getCategoryIcon(q)`). A `recentEntries` useState is snapshotted from `getCachedAccessiblePolls()` in an effect keyed on `[searchFocused]` (re-read on every open, e.g. after creating a poll; cleared on close), sorted newest-LAST so the most recent sits nearest the bar. The `searchSuggestions` memo filters them by the same `matchesTokens` token-prefix predicate as the category rows. Requires the viewer to be a member of a group whose polls are warm in the accessible cache (loaded by visiting the group). **`pollToRecentEntry` MUST branch on `q.question_type`, NOT `q.category`** (`claude/time-polls-suggestions-render-OfZRC`): `draftToQuestionParams` only sets `params.category` for `ranked_choice`/`showtime`, so a **time** (and **limited_supply**) poll stores `category='custom'`, and time/showtime polls carry finalized **slot / showtime keys in `options`** — so the generic `else` branch both lost the time-ness AND seeded those raw keys as ballot options. The dedicated branches reconstruct `{ category: 'time' | 'showtime', forField: details }` (dropping `options` — keys aren't ballot options to re-seed; showtime re-fetches its catalog) and `{ category: 'limited_supply', title: details }` (item name verbatim, like yes_no's prompt). This mirrors the `question_type`-detection convention already in `getCategoryIcon` / `getQuestionSectionTitle` — any new "reconstruct a draft from a poll" path should detect `question_type` for time/showtime/limited_supply rather than trusting the stored `category`.
>     - **Split bar**: a standalone circular `<button>` (the `+`/`✕`) + a separate text-box pill, no inner `+`. `+` (unfocused) opens a blank poll via `chooseSuggestion({ category: 'custom' })`; `✕` (focused) `dismissSearch`. **It MUST be ONE persistent button toggling its `onClick` on `searchFocused`, with `onMouseDown={(e)=>e.preventDefault()}` — NOT two conditionally-rendered buttons.** The preventDefault is load-bearing: without it, tapping `✕` blurs the input → `onBlur` flips `searchFocused` false → the click then runs the `+` branch (opens the modal instead of cancelling). preventDefault keeps focus stable so the ternary reads the correct state at click time (same pattern the suggestion rows use). Two separate buttons are WORSE — the focused `✕` would unmount mid-click when blur re-renders, dropping the click entirely.
>     - The empty-text "New Poll" suggestion row is removed (the `+` button replaces it); a custom-category row still appears only when a non-empty subject is typed. `SEARCH_ROW_CLASS` dropped its `border-b` dividers and halved the inter-row vertical padding (`py-3.5`→`py-[7px]`, `min-h-[3.5rem]`→`min-h-[1.75rem]`); the right-side `tag` pill field + its render were deleted.
>     - **UPDATE (`claude/poll-option-annotations-blue-NrU8C`): option annotations are all blue; yes/no + limited-supply rows are annotated with the category NAME; yes/no titles get a "?"; a Limited Supply suggestion row was added.** Specifics:
>       - **Options are a single blue palette, not a cycling set.** `OPTION_SEG_COLORS` (the 6-color red/blue/gold/pink/teal/orange array) was replaced by one `SEG_OPTION` const (blue). `TitleSegment.optionIndex` (the cycling index) went with it — every option annotation is now blue regardless of position.
>       - **UPDATE (`claude/poll-textbox-annotations-ZbuwX`): only the FIRST option carries the "Options" label; every option is still underlined individually.** `annotateSegments` (page.tsx) tracks an `optionLabelled` flag and tags only the first `kind: 'option'` segment with `label: 'Options'` (plural); 2nd+ options get the bare `SEG_OPTION` (blue `colorText`/`colorBorder`) with no label. The render branch in `searchSuggestions` now keys the labelled/underlined branch on **`seg.colorBorder`** (not `seg.label`), and renders the label span conditionally (`{seg.label && ...}`) — so an unlabelled-but-coloured segment still gets its `border-b-2` underline. The `pt-3` label-room reservation stays gated on `s.segments.some((seg) => seg.label)` (the first option + category/context still carry labels). When adding a future segment kind that should underline without a label, give it a `colorBorder` and omit `label`.
>       - **yes/no + limited_supply titles annotate the WHOLE title with the category.** `draftTitleSegments` emits the typed prompt/item as `kind: 'category'` with a per-segment `label` override (`TitleSegment.label?: string`), so the suggestion row shows a **YES/NO** / **LIMITED SUPPLY** label + underline over the whole title (like the custom-category row, but with the specific category name instead of the generic "CATEGORY"). **UPDATE (`claude/poll-annotation-styling-sfEgO`): these whole-title category-NAME annotations are FADED GREY, not green.** Only the rows whose label IS a specific category name spanning the entire typed title (yes/no, limited_supply) use the faded `SEG_WHOLE_TITLE` const (`text-gray-400/80 dark:text-gray-500/80` label + `border-gray-300/60 dark:border-gray-600/60` underline); the generic green `SEG_CATEGORY` is kept for bare built-in category words (Place, Movie, Restaurant, Time, ...) and the custom CATEGORY row. `annotateSegments` (page.tsx) keys on the override: `if (s.label) return {...SEG_WHOLE_TITLE, label: s.label}` else green `SEG_CATEGORY`. The label string comes from `labelForCategory('yes_no'|'limited_supply')` — NOT hardcoded — so it stays in lockstep with `_CATEGORY_LABELS`. When adding another category whose whole title should carry a specific (faded) label, set `kind:'category'` + `label: labelForCategory(cat)` in its `draftTitleSegments` branch. (A broader "fade ANY category that spans the whole line" rule — keyed on the absence of context/option segments — was tried and reverted: it also drained the built-in category rows' green, which the owner wanted kept.)
>       - **yes/no titles end with "?".** `yesNoTitleText(prompt)` / `yesNoNeedsQuestionMark(prompt)` append "?" unless the prompt already ends with terminal punctuation (`?`/`!`/`.`). Applied in ALL THREE yes/no title paths to preserve the "suggestion ≡ persisted title" invariant: `draftTitleSegments` (the "?" is a trailing MUTED plain segment, so it greys like other rows' "?"), `draftPollPreview` (string), and the submit handler's `wrapperTitle` (gated on `draftDbQuestionType === 'yes_no'`, so limited_supply item names stay "?"-free). limited_supply gets NO "?" (item name, not a question).
>       - **Limited Supply suggestion row** sits next to the Yes/No row at the top of `searchSuggestions` (`row('limited', '🎟️', { category: 'limited_supply', title: raw, isAutoTitle: false })`) — frames the typed text as the item name, mirroring how Yes/No frames it as the prompt.
>       - **Title-required categories (`yes_no`, `limited_supply`) are EXCLUDED from the default category-suggestion loop** (the `cats = orderedBubbleEntries.filter(...)` in `searchSuggestions`). A default category row renders the category NAME as the title via `draftTitleSegments`' whole-title annotation — which for these two categories is exactly what the user-typed title is supposed to override (so the row would show "Yes/No" / "Limited Supply" AS the title, the reported bug). They stay creatable via their dedicated top rows (the `row('yesno', ...)` / `row('limited', ...)` calls, which frame the TYPED TEXT as the title), and their RECENT polls still surface in the `recentEntries` loop (those carry real user-typed titles, so showing them is correct). When adding a future category whose title must be user-typed, add its `value` to this filter's exclusion list AND give it a dedicated top row.
>     - **UPDATE (`claude/poll-suggestions-emoji-issue-RuH7j`): the free-text CUSTOM-category suggestion row is annotated GOLD "Custom Category" and ranks ABOVE the matched built-in category row.** Before this, a typed word that's also a built-in (e.g. "Showtime") produced two visually-identical green "CATEGORY" rows — one the built-in poll type, one a free-text custom category. Now: (1) `SEG_CUSTOM_CATEGORY` (gold/amber `label:'Custom'`, `text-amber-500/80 dark:text-amber-400/80` + `border-amber-400/50`) joins the `SEG_*` palette; (2) `customCategorySegments(overrides)` = `overridesToSegments(overrides).map(s => s.label === SEG_CATEGORY.label ? {...s, ...SEG_CUSTOM_CATEGORY} : s)` recolours the green Category segment to gold; (3) the local `row()` helper gained an optional `segmentsOverride?: SuggestionSegment[]` param (defaults to `overridesToSegments(o)`); (4) the custom `row('custom', '✏️', o, customCategorySegments(o))` push was MOVED to immediately before the built-in `cats` loop, so in the bottom-anchored list it renders above the built-in match (which stays nearest the bar). Yes/No stays always above Limited Supply (unchanged — both under `if (raw)`, yes/no first). **Why a localized post-process and NOT a `draftTitleSegments`/`annotateSegments` change**: the custom row's `category` is the typed TEXT (e.g. "Showtime"), not literally `'custom'`, so a built-in-vs-custom distinction pushed down into the segment pipeline would need non-trivial "is this a known built-in value" detection AND would change OTHER consumers (recent-poll rows for custom polls, etc.) — out of scope. Keep the gold treatment as the call-site-only `customCategorySegments` wrapper; the `s.label === SEG_CATEGORY.label` key references the constant (not a hardcoded "Category" string), so a label rename stays in lockstep. When adding another call site that wants a recoloured category annotation, reuse `customCategorySegments`-style mapping rather than re-spelling the spread.
>     - **UPDATE (`claude/poll-suggestion-spacing-fUm7Q`): suggestion-row spacing + icon/text vertical alignment.** `SEARCH_ROW_CLASS` inter-row spacing was tightened to 25% of the prior baseline (`py-[7px]`→`py-[1.75px]`, `min-h-[1.75rem]`→`min-h-[0.4375rem]`); the icon's flanking spacing was cut 30% (`px-5`→`pl-[14px] pr-5` for the left, `gap-4`→`gap-[11.2px]` for the icon→text gap — the row's *right* edge padding stays `pr-5` since that's after the text, not beside the icon). The annotation label sits flush above its text via `bottom-full`; the small remaining gap is the text line-box half-leading (~3px measured) and is nudged via `translate-y-[…px]` on the label span (currently `1.368px`). **Icon/text vertical alignment is load-bearing and non-obvious:** the text wrapper carries `pt-3` (label-clearance room), so with the row's `items-center` the icon centered against the *taller padded box* and the visible text glyphs ended up ~6.5px lower. Fix = mirror the SAME conditional `pt-3` onto the icon span (gated on `hasLabel = s.segments.some(seg => seg.label)`, hoisted once per row) so icon and text boxes are equal height (both 36px) and their glyph lines align. **Don't measure alignment by the icon span's bounding-box center** — that box includes the empty `pt-3` label zone on top, so it reads ~6px high; the visible emoji sits in the box's bottom line region. Verify with a screenshot, not `getBoundingClientRect()` on the padded span. Sub-pixel `translate-y` / `py` tweaks below ~1px are imperceptible — the icon glyph height (`text-2xl`) floors the row pitch once padding shrinks past it.

The HISTORICAL bubble-button description follows.

The create-poll bubble bar is mounted **ONCE at the layout level** by `<BubbleBarHost>` (`components/BubbleBarHost.tsx`, rendered in `app/layout.tsx`). It is NOT rendered per-route inside `GroupContent` / `EmptyPlaceholder` anymore. The host decides *whether* the bar is shown; `components/BubbleBarPanel.tsx` is the fixed-bottom chrome + the `#draft-poll-portal` target + the scroll-hide logic. The bar's JSX (the buttons) is still owned by `CreateQuestionContent` (`PersistentCreatePollHost`, also layout-level), which portals into `#draft-poll-portal`. The panel auto-hides on scroll-down and reappears on scroll-up or at the document edges.

- **Why layout-level (the slide-flicker saga).** When the bar lived in `GroupContent` it was rendered in BOTH the slide overlay's GroupContent and the real route's during a poll→group back slide, so the two copies met at the slide seam with mismatched horizontal offsets — a jumbled/**doubled** bar (confirmed on prod: 14 buttons mid-slide instead of 7). Single-rendering it (only the real route) killed the doubling but then the bar had to wait for the heavy GroupContent to commit — **measured 1–3.5s on the dev server** — before appearing. Both are structurally impossible with a single layout-level instance that isn't gated on the group render.
- **Behavior: no bar DURING the transition, then slide it up AFTER.** `BubbleBarHost` shows the bar iff `isGroupRootView(pathname) && !slideActive && !homeBackdrop`. `slideActive` (`useIsSlideOverlayGroupActive()`) is true while a group-arrival slide overlay is mounted, so the bar is withheld for the whole transition and mounts only once the overlay unmounts on the settled group route. `BubbleBarPanel` mounts **hidden** (`visible` starts `false` → `translateY(100%)`) and slides up via the existing `transition-transform`. Net UX: the page transition plays with no bar, then the bar slides up from the bottom. Direct group-page loads (no slide, `slideActive` already false) mount + slide up immediately. This sidesteps the seam entirely — the bar is never on screen during the slide.
- **Slide-up entrance needs a DOUBLE rAF.** A dedicated mount effect flips `visible=true` after `requestAnimationFrame(() => requestAnimationFrame(...))`. A single rAF batches with the mount commit so the `translateY(100%)` frame never paints and the transition is optimized away (the bar pops in). Same lesson as the slide overlay's enter→shown handoff. The scroll-visibility effect deliberately does NOT call its initial `schedule()` anymore — that would set `visible=true` on a single rAF and pre-empt the entrance; scroll-restore / bottom-pin still drive `evaluate()` via the scroll events they fire, so the `isScrollRestoring()` force-visible path is unaffected.
- **`useHomeBackdropActive()` (`lib/useHomeBackdropActive.ts`) is the shared SHOW/HIDE_HOME_BACKDROP_EVENT listener.** During a group→home swipe-back the group route is still the current path (until the gesture commits), so `isGroupRootView` would keep the bar on — floating it over the revealed home page. `BubbleBarHost` gates on `!homeBackdrop` so the bar hides for the duration of that gesture. The hook is shared by all three components that react to the backdrop (`HomeBackdropHost`, `CreateGroupButtonHost`, `BubbleBarHost`) — don't re-inline the addEventListener/removeEventListener boilerplate.
- **Behavior change vs the old per-route bar (swipe-back-from-group):** the old bar was a `useSwipeBackGesture.extraTargets` member, so it translated horizontally WITH the page during a group→home swipe. The layout-level bar instead **hides** at swipe-start (via `homeBackdrop`) and is gone by commit. Accepted trade-off — a fixed bar that vanishes as you leave reads fine and avoids a bar floating over home; it's an unreported gesture and not the poll→group path that motivated the work. If you want slide-with-page back, you'd need to re-anchor the bar into the swipe transform, which the layout-level single-instance model can't do cleanly.
- **Visibility rule (scroll-hide):** `visible = atTop || atBottom || lastDirection === 'up' || !userInteracted`. The `atTop` clause is load-bearing on iOS: rubber-band at the top edge produces a synthetic positive scrollY delta on snap-back, which would otherwise flip the direction to `'down'` and hide the panel. The `!userInteracted` clause keeps programmatic scrolls (back-nav restore, bottom-pin, iOS scroll-anchoring) from hiding the bar — only a real pointer/wheel/keydown-driven scroll-down hides it.
- **Two-layer DOM structure** (outer **shell** `position: fixed z-30` + touch-stop handlers, inner **panel** with bg/border/safe-area-padding + the visibility `translateY`). The shell is no longer a swipe transform target (the bar isn't in `GroupContent`'s swipe wrapper), but the split is kept: the shell owns positioning + `onTouchStart/Move/End/Cancel` `stopPropagation` (so a horizontal drag on the bubble row can't initiate a page swipe), and the inner panel owns the visibility transform. `z-30` always — the bar is never on-screen during a slide so it no longer elevates above the overlay.
- **CSS vars `PANEL_HEIGHT_VAR` / `PANEL_OFFSET_VAR`** are exported from `components/BubbleBarPanel.tsx`. The cards-wrapper in `GroupContent` reserves `var(${PANEL_HEIGHT_VAR}, 12rem)` of `padding-bottom` so the last card sits flush against the panel at scroll-bottom; the down scroll-helper arrow uses `bottom: calc(... + var(${PANEL_OFFSET_VAR}, 0px))` so it floats above the panel when visible and reclaims the space when it auto-hides. Don't hand-write the CSS var names — import the constants so a rename is grep-safe.
- **Measurement via `useMeasuredHeight`** (`lib/useMeasuredHeight.ts`) on the inner panel. **No initial seed** — see "Empty-write guard" pitfall below for why. A `vvCounter` state is bumped on every `visualViewport.resize` and passed as the hook's deps — that re-attaches the ResizeObserver and reads a fresh `offsetHeight`. iOS browsers resolve `env(safe-area-inset-bottom)` differently depending on URL-bar / toolbar visibility, but the env-driven size change isn't always picked up by ResizeObserver on those browsers; the deps bump catches the shift without reimplementing measurement.
- **Empty-write guard + `:root` CSS defaults.** The bubble bar JSX is portaled in by `CreateQuestionContent` asynchronously (its MutationObserver fires after BubbleBarPanel mounts), so for a few rAF ticks after each BubbleBarPanel mount the `#draft-poll-portal` div is empty and the panel's `offsetHeight` is only its border + `env(safe-area-inset-bottom)` — 1px on browsers without safe-area, ~35px on iPhone X-class. Writing that small value to `--bubble-bar-panel-offset` pulls the down scroll-helper arrow to the viewport bottom; when content lands and the panel resizes to ~130px the arrow's `transition: bottom 200ms ease-out` then visibly animates it up — surfacing to users as "the arrow repositions itself after the slide completes". Two-part fix in this file + `app/globals.css`: (a) `:root { --bubble-bar-panel-height: 192px; --bubble-bar-panel-offset: 192px; }` so consumers (cards-wrapper padding-bottom, down arrow's `bottom`) have a sensible default before any panel mounts; (b) BubbleBarPanel's CSS var writer skips when `visible && heightPx < MIN_MEANINGFUL_PANEL_HEIGHT` (50px) — comfortably above max-empty (35px) and below min-populated (~88px on wide desktop, ~130px+ on phones). The seed value passed to `useMeasuredHeight` was also removed; with the seed at 192 (≥ threshold), Render 1's useEffect closure could fire with `panelHeight=192` and write 192 to the var, briefly snapping the arrow back to the default before the measurement-driven write of 129 — sequence visible in the trace as `192 → 129 → 192 → 129` during the slide. Default-0 panelHeight + threshold guard catches that path too. Trace evidence: with the fix, the var transitions `192px → 129px` exactly once per slide (matching ResizeObserver's first real measurement after content portal); without the fix, the var oscillates `(empty) → 1px → 129px → 1px → 129px` across the overlay→real-route handoff.
- **Cached scrollHeight.** Reading `document.documentElement.scrollHeight` on every scroll tick forces a synchronous layout flush — on long group pages that's the hottest cost in the visibility loop. The component caches it via a one-shot `ResizeObserver(documentElement)` that updates a ref; the scroll evaluator uses the cached value.
- **Change-detection guard on CSS-var writes.** `style.setProperty` on `:root` invalidates style on every element reading the var (the cards-wrapper and the down arrow here). The write effect compares the new `(height, visible)` tuple against `lastWrittenRef` and skips no-ops — important because `visualViewport.resize` can fire many times per URL-bar transition with the same `offsetHeight`.
- **Single instance — no dual-portal anymore.** With the bar at the layout level there is exactly one `BubbleBarPanel` / one `#draft-poll-portal` at any time. The old "two instances coexist during a slide-overlay handoff" concern is gone (it was the source of the doubling). The CSS vars are still deliberately NOT cleared on unmount — when the bar hides (leaving a group route) a sibling group route may be about to mount and the host padding shouldn't flash to 0.
- **Transitions: the bar does not slide WITH the page — it's withheld then slides up.** There is no per-transition "panel translates with the page" behavior to maintain anymore (that was the per-route + extraTarget model). Every group arrival = no bar during the slide, then a slide-up on the settled route (see the "no bar DURING the transition" bullet above). The group→home swipe-back hides the bar via `homeBackdrop`; the group→home View-Transitions back button removes it when `pathname` leaves the group route.
- **Pitfall: rubber-band at scroll-top can flip direction to `'down'`** on iOS. Without the `atTop` clause, the snap-back from a pull-down gesture (scrollY goes negative briefly, then snaps back to 0 with a positive delta > threshold) flips `lastDirection` to `'down'` and hides the panel even though the user is at the top. The `atTop = currentY <= 2` check defeats this.
- **Pitfall: env(safe-area-inset-bottom) changes can be silent to ResizeObserver on iOS.** The `vvCounter`-driven remeasure is what catches them. If you remove the `visualViewport.resize` listener, expect iOS Firefox / iOS Safari to ship a stale panel height on URL-bar toggles, sliding content under the panel at scroll-bottom.

### Voter Availability: Split Slots + Ghost Re-add (`DayTimeWindowsInput` voter form)

The voter availability form (rendered by `TimeBallotSection` → `TimeQuestionFields` → `DayTimeWindowsInput` with `questionWindows` set, i.e. `isVoterForm`) lets a voter carve a creator's day window into multiple disconnected segments. Model (replaces the old per-window enabled-checkbox toggle, which is gone):

- **All ballot slots are deletable** (delete `×` button), exactly like the creation form, and the `+` button (`pickVoterSplitWindow`) adds another — dropped into the largest free gap inside the creator's allowed windows, with a 15-min margin off any edge that abuts an existing slot so it isn't born orange. Voters may delete every slot in a day (the creation form keeps its "can't delete the last slot" rule; voter form does not — re-add via a ghost).
- **Ghost rows.** For each original (creator) window that NO current ballot slot overlaps (`windowsOverlap`), a muted row with an empty checkbox is rendered (`handleRestoreWindow` re-adds the full window on check). Ghosts are merged with real rows in start-time order, and **suppressed when `disabled`** (the read-only post-vote summary) — there they'd read as "options you forgot".
- **Soft validation, not a hard clamp.** The old per-index `constraintMin/Max` clamp on the edit `TimeGridModal` was REMOVED (it mapped windows to the wrong question window once a split re-sorted the list). A voter window is invalid when it's outside every creator window (`isWindowWithinQuestionWindows`) OR touches/overlaps its sorted predecessor (`win.min <= prev.max`); invalid slots get the orange `intersecting` pill variant AND block submit.
- **`hasInvalidVoterWindows(voterDays, questionDays)` (`lib/timeUtils.ts`) is the single submit-gate predicate**, mirroring the per-pill orange flags so the highlight and the block can't drift. It's checked in THREE places that must stay in lockstep: `buildVoteData` (`voteDataBuilders.ts`, returns `{ok:false}` — covers the batch/wrapper path), `QuestionBallot.handleVoteClick` (early-return BEFORE opening the confirm modal — the detail page's wrapper Submit routes through `handleVoteClick`, so without this a voter would open the confirm modal and only get bounced on confirm), and `TimeBallotSection` (disables its own Submit button + shows the amber notice).
- **Card-less day rows.** `TimeQuestionFields` renders the day list as borderless `DayTimeWindowsInput` rows inside a `divide-y divide-gray-200 dark:divide-gray-700` (no per-day card). Because the pills then sit directly on the availability card (`POLL_SUBCARD_CLASS` = `bg-gray-100/900`), the filled pill / `+` button backgrounds are `bg-gray-200 dark:bg-gray-700` (one step darker than the surface) — also legible on the create-poll white/`gray-800` card. Don't drop them back to `gray-100` or they vanish on the voter card in light mode.
- **`handleEditApply` spreads the existing window (`{...w, min, max}`)** so an edit preserves other fields rather than replacing the object. The vestigial `enabled` field is still defensively honored by the `voteDataBuilders` cleaning filter + the submit gate (`w.enabled !== false`), but nothing sets it false anymore.
- Composes cleanly with #515 (voters can't add/remove DAYS — no day-picker in the voter `TimeQuestionFields`) since this feature is about within-day SLOTS, and with #516's drag-select which lives in the preferences phase, not availability.

### Day/Time-Window Long-Press: Bulk-Edit + Copy-to-Days (`DayTimeWindowsList`)

The multi-day window list (create-poll Time Windows card AND the voter availability ballot) supports two cross-day long-press gestures. The state spans multiple days, so a coordinator component (`components/DayTimeWindowsList.tsx`) owns it and renders the `divide-y` row list both call sites used before; per-day `DayTimeWindowsInput` rows are leaves wired via an optional `selection` prop.

- **Coordinator vs leaf split.** `DayTimeWindowsList` takes the FULL `dayTimeWindows` + a full-list `onChange` (`setDayTimeWindows` on create-poll, `onDayTimeWindowsChange` on the ballot). Both call sites (`app/create-poll/page.tsx` Time Windows `<section>`, `components/TimeQuestionFields.tsx`) now render `<DayTimeWindowsList>` instead of mapping `DayTimeWindowsInput` directly — so the gestures work in both contexts from one implementation. `DayTimeWindowsInput` keeps its own per-day single-window edit (TimeGridModal) for the `'none'` mode; the `selection` prop (`DayTimeSelection` interface exported from `DayTimeWindowsInput`) carries the long-press/tap wiring + per-row copy-mode flags. `selectionEnabled = !disabled && length>0`, so the read-only post-vote summary (`disabled`) gets no selection.
- **Bulk edit ('windows' mode).** Long-press a time-slot pill → `enterWindows` (haptic, pre-selects it, blue `ring-2 ring-blue-500`). Tap pills to multi-select across ANY days (`selectedWindows: Set<"${day}#${index}">`). The portaled floating toolbar's **"Edit N"** opens ONE `TimeGridModal` (seeded from the first selected window); on apply, every selected pill is set to that window. Same-day duplicates are deduped, then `fitWindowsToBounds` runs (below).
- **Copy-to-days ('copy' mode).** Long-press a day → a portaled context menu ("Copy times to other days") positioned at the long-press `(x,y)`. Tapping it enters copy mode: the source day greys out + shows "Copying…" (excluded as a target, `isCopySource`), other days get a left checkbox (`isPasteTarget`) and the whole row toggles them. **"Paste N"** copies the source day's windows onto each selected day (then `fitWindowsToBounds`). Gated on `dayTimeWindows.length >= 2` (`canCopy`).
- **Autofix: `fitWindowsToBounds(windows, questionWindows)` (`lib/timeUtils.ts`)** is the post-op normalizer so a bulk-edit/paste result can NEVER trip `hasInvalidVoterWindows` ("outside allowed times / overlap" submit-blocker). It clamps each window into the allowed (creator) window it most overlaps, or — when it overlaps none — snaps it (preserving duration) to the nearest allowed window; then sorts + merges overlapping/touching windows. Empty `questionWindows` (creator form, no constraints) ⇒ sort + de-overlap only. Returns the SAME array ref + `changed:false` when nothing moved. Cross-midnight allowed windows are skipped for the fit math. Both ops funnel through `commitBulkOp(next, adjusted)` which does `haptic + onChange + exitSelection + (adjusted && flashNotice(...))` — the fit→notify contract in one place. A transient toast ("Adjusted slots to fit the allowed times", portaled, 3s) shows only when something was clamped. Tests: `tests/__tests__/time-poll/fit-windows-to-bounds.test.ts`.
- **Long-press mechanics (leaf).** `DayTimeWindowsInput` uses a non-hook `longPressHandlers(fire)` factory + a single shared `lpRef` (one press at a time) — NOT the `lib/useLongPress` hook, because pills are rendered in a loop and hooks can't be called per-pill. Pointer-events only (covers mouse + touch uniformly), 500ms timer, 10px move-cancel, and a `fired` flag the pill onClick consults to swallow the synthesized click after a long-press (reset on the next pointerdown). The pills (`PILL_BASE`) + the long-press day label carry `select-none [-webkit-touch-callout:none]` so a long press doesn't select the slot text / pop the iOS callout.
- **z-index (portaled chrome).** Toolbar `z-[65]` (clears the create-poll bottom sheet's `z-60`); context menu + toast `z-[66]`. **`TimeGridModal` (both the bulk-edit AND per-window instances) is portaled via `ModalPortal` at `z-[70]` and always viewport-centered (`items-center justify-center`, no bottom-anchored mobile layout).** It used to render inline (`z-50`); on the poll detail page the swipe wrapper's permanent `will-change: transform` made it the containing block for `position: fixed` descendants, so the inline modal's `inset-0` box spanned the full page-content height and the bottom-aligned (`items-end pb-[25vh]`) modal landed BELOW the visible screen once the voter availability form grew past one viewport (split slots) — and the body scroll-lock kept the user from scrolling to it. The portal escapes any transformed/contained/will-change ancestor; `z-[70]` clears the create-poll sheet (z-60) since the portal also leaves that stacking context. `ModalPortal` stays mounted across open/close inside `TimeGridModal` (children gated on `isOpen` INSIDE it) — ModalPortal defers children by one commit, so mounting it fresh on open would leave `backdropRef` null when the touchmove-prevent effect runs. The toolbar is hidden while the modal is open (`!editOpen`), so they never fight.
- **Stale-selection guard.** A `useEffect` keyed on `disabled` + `dayKeySig` (`days.join('|')`) drops any in-flight selection if the day SET changes underneath (e.g. the calendar adds/removes a day) — keys/indices would otherwise apply to the wrong windows. Window edits don't change `dayKeySig` (it's days-only), so selection survives a normal edit; bulk ops `exitSelection()` themselves.
- **NOT memoized by design.** `DayTimeWindowsInput` isn't `React.memo`'d, so the per-day `selection` object + per-pill `longPressHandlers` are rebuilt each render — accepted (a handful of day rows; memoizing props yields zero render savings without `memo` on the child). Don't add memoization here without first memoizing the child.


### Constrained Time Wheel Pickers (Voter Response)

- **(Historical for the day-window editor) Voter time ranges as strict subsets via clamping is RETIRED for `DayTimeWindowsInput`** — see "Voter Availability: Split Slots" above; containment is now soft-validated (orange + block), not clamped. The wheel-picker notes below still describe `TimeCounterInput` / `TimeGridModal` internals.
- **Voter time ranges must be strict subsets of the question creator's window.** For non-cross-midnight question windows (e.g., 9AM–5PM), enforce `voter_min < voter_max` — never allow cross-midnight voter ranges. For cross-midnight question windows, only exclude exact equality (`min !== max`, which would be 24h). The `isValidVsSibling()` function in `TimeCounterInput.tsx` implements this.
- **Filter wheel items, don't clamp after the fact.** Clamping after scroll causes visible snap-back. Instead, compute the valid hour/minute sets upfront so invalid values are never shown.
- **Each picker must know the other picker's value** (`siblingValue` prop) to dynamically filter its options. The min picker shows only times strictly less than the current max, and vice versa. Hours with no valid minutes are hidden entirely.
- **The AM/PM wheel in constrained mode is non-interactive** — wrapped in `pointerEvents: 'none'`, it auto-follows the selected hour via `selectedIndex`. This keeps hours in chronological order across AM/PM boundaries.
- **When an hour change invalidates the current minute**, auto-select the minute giving the smallest positive duration to/from the sibling (≈1 increment gap). This avoids jarring jumps to arbitrary times.

### Cross-Midnight Time Windows

- **Time windows where `max <= min` represent cross-midnight ranges** (e.g., 10 PM–2 AM). Equal start/end means a full 24-hour window. Use `<=` consistently in all cross-midnight detection — `<` misses the equal-times-as-24h case.
- **String comparison works for HH:MM cross-midnight detection** only because the format is always zero-padded. `"02:00" < "22:00"` is correct lexicographically. If the format ever loses zero-padding (e.g., `"2:00"`), all comparisons silently break.
- **`_window_effective_end()` in `time_slots.py`** is the canonical backend helper — it adds 1440 minutes when `w_end <= w_start`. The frontend has no shared utility yet; cross-midnight checks are inline in `DayTimeWindowsInput` and `TimeGridModal`.
- **Looping scroll wheels must scroll to the nearest occurrence** of the target index, not the center repetition. Otherwise wraparound (12→1) causes the wheel to scroll the long way around through all values.
- **ScrollWheel's `suppressScrollHandler` flag can get permanently stuck.** `recenterLoop()` sets the flag and schedules an rAF to clear it, but `correctPosition()` runs synchronously right after and bails out because the flag is still set. If a touch interaction then overwrites `scrollTimeout`, the clearing rAF/timeout is lost and the flag stays true forever — silencing all `onChange` calls. Fix: defer `correctPosition` via rAF when suppression is active, and add a safety timeout (500ms) that guarantees the flag gets cleared.
- **Use refs (not render-scope variables) for state that multiple scroll events may read/write within a single React render cycle.** `handleHourChange` in `TimeCounterInput` captured `periodIndex` from the render scope. When two scroll events crossed the AM/PM boundary before React re-rendered, the second event used the stale value and emitted the wrong time. Track such state in a `useRef` and update it immediately in the handler.

### Limited Supply Question Type

A first-come, first-served signup: the creator sets `supply_count` slots for something (tickets, leftover food, a carpool seat, a volunteer shift) and whoever **claims first** gets them. Later claimants are **waitlisted** in order. Shipped on migration 131 (type + `questions.supply_count`) + migration 132 (`questions.reveal_claimant_names`).

- **Vote shape: a claim carries NO payload.** `vote_type='limited_supply'` with `is_abstain=false` is a CLAIM, `is_abstain=true` is a DECLINE ("No thanks"). `yes_no_choice`/`ranked_choices`/`suggestions` are all NULL (enforced by the `vote_structure_valid` CHECK). No new vote columns — claim order is just `votes.created_at`.
- **Resolution is ordering, weighted by plus-ones** (`server/algorithms/limited_supply.py: calculate_limited_supply_result`): take non-abstain votes, sort by `created_at`, then **expand each claim into one entry per represented person** — `vote_weight(vote)` = `1 + len(plus_one_names)` (submitter first, then each named/unnamed plus-one, all sharing the claim's `created_at`). The first `supply_count` person-entries are **secured**, the rest **waitlisted**. Because slots are awarded per-head, a single claim can **straddle** the boundary (a "me + 2 friends" claim with 2 spots left secures 2, waitlists the 3rd — first-come per person, no wasted spots). Declines take no slot, so when a secured claimant edits to a decline the next waitlister is promoted automatically (results recompute from the ordered claim list — there's no stored "secured" flag). `claim_count` / `secured_count` / `waitlist_count` are now **people** counts (not vote-row counts). `_compute_results` returns `supply_count` / `secured_count` / `waitlist_count` / `claims[]` (`{name, secured, position, created_at}` — one per person). Empty-string plus-ones collapse to `name=None` (FE shows the "Spot taken"/"Waiting" placeholder). A pre-migration-130 row with no `plus_one_names` weighs exactly 1.
- **The title IS the item** ("2 spare tickets"), like a yes_no prompt: the create form shows the Title field + a "Number Available" `CompactNumberRow`, NOT options/category/context (`category` stays `custom`; the bubble sets `question_type=limited_supply`). `isAutoTitle` is false. `_category_for_title` / `getQuestionLabel` / `QUESTION_TYPE_SYMBOLS` special-case `limited_supply` → 🎟️, same convention as `time`.
- **Create-form bottom-card settings are gated so only the relevant ones show + use "claiming" wording.** The ranked-choice / suggestion settings (Suggestion Cutoff, Scoring Algorithm, Minimum Votes, Show-Results-After-Min-Votes, Allow-voting-before-options) are hidden because `isSuggestionMode` AND `inlineFormIsRankedChoice` both exclude `limited_supply` (alongside `yes_no` + `time`) — otherwise a limited-supply category slips through (it's `!== yes_no && !== time`) and surfaces all of them. So the bottom card shows only **Claiming Cutoff** + **Allow claiming for others (plus-ones)** + the (now-shown) emoji picker. The "claiming" relabel is driven by `pollIsLimitedSupply` (= every question is limited_supply; mixed polls keep "voting"): `VotingCutoffField` takes a `label` prop ("Claiming Cutoff" vs default "Voting Cutoff"), and the plus-ones row swaps "voting"→"claiming". When adding a new bottom-card setting that's ranked-choice/suggestion-specific, gate it on `pollHasRankedChoice`/`pollHasPrephase` (both already exclude limited_supply) — don't gate on a bare `category !== 'yes_no' && category !== 'time'`, which re-introduces the limited-supply leak.
- **Multi-slot claims ride the plus-ones mechanism — `allow_plus_ones` defaults ON for limited_supply.** A voter claims N spots by adding N-1 people in the poll-level `PlusOnesInput` (the same "respond for others" feature used by time/yes-no); the claim's `plus_one_names`/`plus_one_user_ids` ride the submit and the server weights it (above). The default is set in BOTH `routers/polls.py: create_poll` (server) and `app/create-poll/page.tsx: effectiveAllowPlusOnes` (the create-form toggle's displayed state) — keep them in sync, same as the time-question default. There is NO bespoke quantity-stepper / per-claim quantity column; plus-ones IS the multi-slot mechanism.
- **Two submit modes, picked by `PollDetailPage`:**
  - **Self-submit (no plus-ones added):** "Claim a spot" / "No thanks" post immediately via QuestionBallot's `submitVote` (speed matters — one tap), using the `pendingSupplySubmit` deferred-submit pattern (mirrors binary-RC tap). `wrapperHandlesSubmit` is false; it's in `SELF_SUBMIT_TYPES` and `prepareBatchVoteItem` returns `{skip}` so a multi-question wrapper Submit can't double-claim.
  - **Staged (≥1 plus-one added, single-question poll):** `limitedSupplyStaged` (in `PollDetailPage`) flips `useWrapperSubmit` true → `wrapperHandlesSubmit` true → QuestionBallot's `supplySelectionMode`. The tap now only SELECTS claim-vs-decline (no submit); the ballot renders the two as a selector (claim selected by default) and the wrapper renders an explicit **"Claim N spots"** / **"Decline"** Submit that commits with the plus-ones attached. Mirrors the yes/no-with-plus-ones staging precedent. `handleVoteClick` routes limited_supply to immediate `submitVote()` (no confirm modal — the wrapper Submit IS the confirmation, like time). The name gate runs at the wrapper Submit (`gateOnName`), so the selection tap doesn't gate. After committing, the ballot shows the outcome + a "Change my response" affordance (`onEdit` → `setIsEditingVote(true)`) that re-reveals the Submit. The count for the label comes from a reactive `plusOnesCount` prop (distinct from the `getPlusOnes` getter read at submit time); `wrapperSubmitLabel` bakes the head count in ("Claim 3 spots"), so the wrapper SKIPS its generic " for N" suffix for limited_supply.
  - **`getPlusOnes` is threaded into QuestionBallot's `submitVote`** so the self-submit/single-question-wrapper paths carry plus-ones on the atomic POST (multi-question polls carry them via the wrapper's own `confirmPollSubmit`). This also fixed a latent gap where single-question non-yes_no polls (e.g. a single time poll) dropped plus-ones on the wrapper-Submit path.
  - The ballot shows live "N of M spots left" / "Full · K waitlisted", the viewer's own secured/waitlist status (matched by their own vote's `created_at` against the roster), and the signup roster (one row per person, including plus-ones). The committed-status JSX is factored into one `committedStatus` const shared by the staged-post-vote, self-submit-claim, and self-submit-decline branches.
- **Name visibility is a per-question toggle** ("Show who claimed", `reveal_claimant_names`, default true). When OFF, only the creator sees names; other viewers get an anonymized roster (positions/counts, "Spot taken"/"Waiting", own row → "You"). Enforced **server-side**: `_compute_results(..., reveal_names=)` nulls each claim's `name` + sets `names_hidden=true`; `get_results` (per-question) and `polls_for_poll_ids` (bulk/warm-cache) both compute `reveal_names = reveal_claimant_names OR viewer_user_id == poll.creator_user_id`. `created_at` is kept even when hidden so the FE can still identify the viewer's OWN row. `_SELECT_QUESTION_FULL` surfaces `poll_creator_user_id` for this check.
- **Group-card pill** (`CompactSupplyPreview`): "N/M claimed" or "Full" (green when full/closed). Uses counts only — names are never on the card — so the name toggle doesn't affect it. Falls back to `question.supply_count` when no results have loaded so a fresh poll still reads "0/N".
- **Not auto-closed when full** (deliberate — the waitlist keeps collecting so drops can promote). Tests: `server/tests/test_limited_supply.py`.
- **OPEN QUESTIONS — RESOLVED (owner confirmed June 2026: keep current behavior on all of them):** (a) **keep collecting a waitlist when full** (does NOT auto-close — so dropped/declined claims promote the next waitlister); (b) **keep the explicit "No thanks" decline** (non-claim is not treated as an implicit decline); (d) **name-visibility toggle defaults to visible** (`reveal_claimant_names` default true). **(c) per-person multi-slot claims — RESOLVED earlier:** done via plus-ones (see the "Multi-slot claims" + "Two submit modes" bullets above); not a quantity stepper. The whole limited-supply spec is now locked — no open design questions remain.
- **Name gate (SHIPPED):** a nameless user tapping a self-submitting ballot (limited_supply Claim/Decline, OR a binary 2-option ranked-choice card) would otherwise hit the server's `400 "name is required"` (surfaced as a raw vote error). These taps auto-submit and bypass the wrapper-Submit name gate, so `QuestionBallot` takes an optional `onRequireName?: (retry) => boolean` prop that `handleSupplyClaim`/`handleSupplyDecline` AND `handleBinaryChoiceTap` (first-time auto-submit only — the edit case stages through the gated wrapper Submit) consult before firing the deferred submit; `PollDetailPage` passes its `gateOnName` to **every** ballot (not scoped to a type — the prop is only consumed by the self-submitting tap paths, so it's a no-op elsewhere; yes/no's self-submit is gated separately via `dispatchYesNoTap`). The gate stashes the tap as a retry and replays it once `AccountGateModal` saves a name — `submitVote`'s existing `getUserName()` fallback then picks up the freshly-saved name. The prop is the canonical hook for any future self-submitting ballot type that needs the name gate; don't re-inline a getUserName check in the handler, and pass `gateOnName` unconditionally rather than re-adding a per-type ternary. **Pitfall fixed (private-browser "Failed to submit vote"):** the binary RC tap path was originally missed — `handleBinaryChoiceTap` never called `onRequireName` and `PollDetailPage` scoped the prop to `limited_supply` only, so an anonymous viewer with no saved name (e.g. opening a shared link in a private browser) auto-submitted with `voter_name: null` and got the 400.

### Poll Recurrence (migration 141)

A poll can **repeat on a schedule**. The creator opts in via a "Repeat" row in the create-poll bottom settings card (→ `RecurrenceModal`: frequency Off/Daily/Weekly/Monthly, "every N" interval, weekly day-of-week chips, monthly day-of-month vs nth-weekday, end conditions never/after-N/until-date, live next-5 preview). The poll then becomes a **recurring anchor**: the cron tick materializes a fresh copy of its questions on each future occurrence date. A "Scheduled" link at the top of the group scroll opens `/g/<id>/scheduled` listing the upcoming auto-opening instances.

- **The rule shape** (mirrored in `lib/recurrence.ts` TS ⇄ `server/services/recurrence.py` Python — keep in lockstep, like every other JS↔Python mirror): `{frequency, interval, weekdays:[0..6 Sun=0], monthlyMode:'dayOfMonth'|'nthWeekday', end:{type:'never'|'after'|'on', count?, date?}, start:'YYYY-MM-DD'}`. Occurrence generation is FREQ/INTERVAL/BYDAY/COUNT/UNTIL-flavoured (close to iCalendar RRULE). The TS + Python generators are unit-tested against the SAME cases (`tests/__tests__/recurrence.test.ts` + `server/tests/test_recurrence.py`) — when changing a rule, update both + both test files.
- **Schema (migration 141, all on `polls`):** `recurrence JSONB` (the rule+start; NULL = one-off), `recurrence_skip_dates JSONB` (individually-cancelled dates), `recurrence_until DATE` (exclusive "cancel this + remainder" cutoff), `recurrence_last_run DATE` (watermark of the last materialized occurrence; starts at the rule's `start` so the anchor IS the first occurrence), `recurrence_anchor_id UUID` (set on materialized CHILD instances → the anchor; NULL on anchors + non-recurring polls). The anchor carries the schedule; children carry `recurrence_anchor_id` + NULL recurrence. Since every poll SELECT is `polls.*` (`_SELECT_POLLS_WITH_GROUP`), the columns flow to `_row_to_poll` automatically — surfaced on `PollResponse.recurrence` / `recurrence_skip_dates` / `recurrence_until` / `recurrence_anchor_id` (+ FE `Poll.*` via `toPoll`).
- **Create:** `CreatePollRequest.recurrence` (raw dict). `_insert_poll` stores it via `_validate_recurrence` (returns None for a `frequency:'none'`/malformed rule → a one-off poll, never a 500) + sets `recurrence_last_run = start`. The create-poll form sends `{...recurrence, start: recurrenceStart}` (start = the day the form was opened). A human `🔁 Repeats: …` line is ALSO folded into the poll's Notes as a display cue (`recurrenceNote`) — cosmetic; the structured column is the source of truth.
- **Materialization (cron tick pass 5):** `services/recurrence.py: materialize_due_instances(conn, today)`. For each anchor, `due_occurrences` = occurrences after `recurrence_last_run`, on/before today, not skipped, before `until`. Each due date → a fresh instance by **reconstructing a `CreatePollRequest`/`CreateQuestionRequest` from the anchor's stored question rows** and calling the existing `_insert_poll` + `_insert_question` (reuses tested insert paths rather than a raw SQL deep-copy). Per-instance resets: suggestion polls (`ranked_choice` + `suggestion_deadline_minutes`) re-open collecting from scratch (`options=None`); time-window `day` dates are shifted forward by `(occurrence − start)` days so a recurring time poll's availability advances; `response_deadline = occurrence + the anchor's voting-window length`; the child links back via `recurrence_anchor_id` and carries no rule. The watermark advances to the last successfully-materialized date (stops on a failure so it retries next tick). Idempotent via the watermark. **Demo caveat:** materialization only runs on tiers where the cron + `INTERNAL_TICK_SECRET` exist (canary/prod) — dev servers don't run the tick, so live materialization isn't demoable there (it's server-tested).
  - **Firing the tick on dev (option (b) SHIPPED): `POST /api/internal/dev/tick`.** Per-branch dev servers have no cron + no `INTERNAL_TICK_SECRET`, so they never fire the tick on their own. This non-prod endpoint runs the SAME tick body without the bearer, gated by request Origin via `services/fe_origin.is_prod_origin` (503 on prod, available on canary / `*.dev.whoeverwants.com` / localhost — same model as the dev instant-sign-in links). Use it to exercise the tick-only paths end-to-end on dev (recurrence **materialization**, deadline-driven **auto-close**, **phase-transition** pushes, cancelled-time-event auto-close, **vote reminders**, **auto-aging**): e.g. backdate an anchor's `recurrence_last_run`, `curl -X POST -H 'Origin: https://<slug>.dev.whoeverwants.com' https://<slug>.dev.whoeverwants.com/api/internal/dev/tick`, assert the materialized instance appears. Idempotent + safe to re-run. The authenticated `/tick` (cron, bearer) is unchanged; both call the shared `_run_tick(now)` in `routers/internal.py`. Tests: `server/tests/test_internal_tick.py`. (Not done: option (a), a per-container cron loop that makes dev behave fully like prod automatically — on-demand firing covers manual verification, so it's deferred.)
- **Cancel (creator-only):** `POST /api/polls/{id}/recurrence/cancel {scope:'occurrence'|'series', date}`. `occurrence` → append `date` to the anchor's skip list; `series` → set `recurrence_until = date`. Resolves a child instance to its anchor (`recurrence_anchor_id || id`). FE: `apiCancelRecurrence(pollId, scope, date)`.
- **FE cancel UI = `RecurrenceCancelSheet`** (shared action sheet: "cancel this" / "cancel this + remainder"). Two surfaces, two mechanisms behind one sheet: the **Scheduled page** long-press a future row → cancel that occurrence (skip date) / this + all later (until), optimistic via `extraSkip`/`extraUntil` local state + the API; the **group page** long-press an OPEN recurring poll → `FollowUpModal`'s "Cancel recurring…" → cancel this poll (close it, `close_reason='cancelled'`) / this + stop repeating (close + `series` cancel with `until=today`). The open-poll "cancel this" CLOSES the real instance; the scheduled "cancel this" SKIPS a not-yet-materialized date — same sheet, surface-appropriate wiring.
- **Scheduled page** (`/g/<id>/scheduled`, slide kind `groupScheduled` added per the documented overlay-extension pattern): reads each group poll's `recurrence` (anchors only — children have NULL), generates upcoming occurrences filtered by skip/until/today, renders rows that **mirror the group card layout** (`GroupCardItem`) — same edge-to-edge rectangle, title row, metadata row — swapping only the creation date → the **open date** ("Opens <date>") + the votes/countdown corner → the recurrence cadence ("Weekly on Tue"). Swipe-back to the group root mirrors the poll detail page (`useSwipeBackGesture` + `GroupBackdropHost`). The "Scheduled ›" link sits at the top of the group scroll in the muted section-header gray.

### Showtime Question Type (migration 140)

A `showtime` poll behaves like a `time` poll but the options are **concrete movie showtimes** (curated by the creator from real Alamo Drafthouse data) instead of generated 15-minute slots. Voters mark each curated showtime **want / neutral / can't-attend** (a time-preference ballot where red = "can't attend"); winner = **max attendance (fewest can't-attend) → max likes (want) → earliest**. Full spec: `docs/showtime-poll-plan.md`. Scope: Alamo only, no self-healing (v1).

- **Vote storage REUSES the time shape — NO new columns.** Migration 140 only adds `'showtime'` to the 3 CHECK constraints (`questions_question_type_check`, `votes_vote_type_check`, `vote_structure_valid` — the showtime vote branch is just `yes_no_choice IS NULL`, mirroring `time`). Reactions live in the existing `votes.liked_slots` ("want") / `disliked_slots` ("can't"). No availability phase, no slot generation, no min-participants/exclusion machinery — options arrive pre-finalized at create time. `_insert_question` already gates the time-only columns (`day_time_windows`, `min_availability_percent`, etc.) on `== time`, so showtime gets them None/0 for free; `_finalize_time_slots` in `create_poll` is gated on `== time` so showtime skips it. **`allow_plus_ones` defaults ON** for showtime (group outings), like time — set in BOTH `routers/polls.py: create_poll` AND `app/create-poll/page.tsx: pollHasPlusOneDefaultType` (keep in sync).
- **Winner reuses `_pick_winner_from_reactions` verbatim** — `algorithms/showtime.py: calculate_showtime_results(options, votes)` returns `{winner, like_counts, dislike_counts, attendance_counts}` where `attendance_counts[opt] = total preference-respondent weight − dislike weight` (attendance-max ≡ dislike-min). Plus-one weighting flows through `vote_weight`. `_compute_results` has a `showtime` branch; `vote_validation` accepts showtime like time. `QuestionResultsResponse.attendance_counts` is the only new results field.
- **Option-key format = the time slot key** `"YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM-HH:MM"` (end = start + runtime) so `parse_slot_key` + `lib/timeUtils.ts` parsers/sorters work unchanged. Rich data (cinema, format, seats, poster, session_id, sales_url) lives in `options_metadata[key]`.
