  - **Cross-cinema key collisions are real and MUST be disambiguated** — two cinemas showing the same movie at the same start time + runtime produce the identical key (the format omits cinema/format on purpose so the shared parsers work), which both crashes React (duplicate key) and collides in `options_metadata`/vote refs. `alamo.py: group_by_film` → `_disambiguate_keys` nudges the END minute (+1m, mod 24h) per collision: the START + date (which drive the chronological winner sort + the displayed time) stay exact; only the rarely-shown end/runtime label shifts a minute. `ShowtimeBubbles` also keys React on `${key}#${idx}` as defense-in-depth. Don't append a space/`@`/`-` suffix to the key — it breaks `parse_slot_key`'s `split(" ")` / `split("-")` / `_time_to_minutes`.
  - **Brand-prefix normalization (`The Big Show:` etc.) — SHIPPED.** Alamo merchandises the SAME film under branded "presentations" — premium auditoriums (`The Big Show`, `Crafthouse`) AND themed/repertory events (`Kids Camp`, `Movie Party`, `Guest Selects`, `Sad Girl Cinema Club`, ...). Each gets its OWN `FilmId` + `FilmName` with the brand baked in as a `"<Brand>: <MOVIE>"` prefix (the feed's structured `Series` field is **empty in practice** — verified against the live DFW feed, so it can't drive this). Unhandled, one movie fragmented into several picker rows ("DISCLOSURE DAY" + "The Big Show: DISCLOSURE DAY"), breaking search + apples-to-apples comparison. Fix in `alamo.py`: `_split_brand(name) → (canonical, brand|None)` strips a prefix ONLY when the text before the first `": "` is in the curated `_BRAND_PREFIXES` allowlist; `group_by_film` then merges variants on `_group_key(canonical)` (case/whitespace-insensitive) instead of `FilmId`, preferring an UNBRANDED variant for the representative `film_id`/`name`/`poster_url` (the `_unbranded` flag on the film dict, popped before return) so the plain film's catalog poster + clean name win; a brand-only film still displays its cleaned canonical name. **The allowlist is load-bearing** — a generic "split on first colon" would mangle real titles `JACKASS: BEST AND LAST` / `STAR WARS: THE MANDALORIAN AND GROGU` (both verified present in the live feed, both must pass through untouched). **Brand stays visible per-session** by folding into the `fmt` label via `_display_format(brand, fmt_name)` (brand-first; dedupes when Alamo repeats the brand AS the `FormatName`, which it usually does; appends a distinctive format like `70mm` after the brand) — so each showtime bubble reads `The Big Show · Richardson` in both the curate picker and the vote ballot, with NO FE change (the bubble already renders `format · cinema`). `Showtime` gained `brand` + `group_key` fields; `brand` is read in `group_by_film` (the unbranded-preference test), so it's not dead weight. **Adding a brand = one line in `_BRAND_PREFIXES`.** Tests: `test_showtimes_adapter.py` (`test_split_brand` incl. the no-strip cases, `test_display_format_dedupes_and_appends`, `test_brand_variants_merge_into_one_film`, `test_brand_only_film_shows_clean_name`).
- **Data layer = `server/services/showtimes/` + `server/data/alamo_cinemas.json`.** Alamo has no official API; two unauthenticated JSON feeds are joined + a static cinema directory (coords/tz — neither feed carries coordinates):
  - **Sessions (legacy)** `feeds.drafthouse.com/adcService/showtimes.svc/market/{marketId}/` (NOTE trailing slash — bare path 307-redirects) → `Market→Dates→Cinemas→Films→Series→Formats→Sessions`; carries runtime, seats, format names, cinema names. Forward window is ~60 days for populated markets (NOT the ~2 days the plan feared).
  - **Catalog (modern)** `drafthouse.com/s/mother/v2/schedule/market/{marketSlug}` → posters (joined on the film slug); catalog also carries sessions but we take posters only. **Ticketing link = the per-cinema MOVIE showpage `https://drafthouse.com/{marketSlug}/show/{filmSlug}?cinemaId={cinemaId}` (theater + movie), NOT a session deep link.** `drafthouse.com/ticketing/{SessionId}` returns a 200 SHELL to curl but 404s IN-BROWSER once the session expires/sells out (the checkout SPA can't resolve a stale id), and `SessionSalesURL` is empty in practice — so a session-specific link is unreliable. The showpage is stable and as specific as we can get without the gated checkout API. `normalize()` builds `sales_url` from `dir_cinema.market_slug` + the feed's `FilmSlug` + `CinemaId` (None when `FilmSlug` is absent).
  - **Directory** (`alamo_cinemas.json`): `{cinema_id, slug, name, market_id, market_slug, lat, lng, timezone}`. The `cinema_id` is `00xx` for Austin (market `0000`), `07xx` for DFW (market `0700`, slug `dfw`). **Market IDs are NOT sequential** — most numeric ids return `null`; enumerate by probing (the legacy feed's `Market.MarketName/MarketSlug` confirms). **Adding a metro = drop its cinemas into this JSON** (the legacy feed gives id/name/slug/tz; lat/lng are the only manual part). `ShowtimeSource` protocol (`alamo.py`) is the seam for other chains later — don't build them now.
  - `cache.py` — per-(market, fetch_date) TTL disk+memory cache mirroring `search.py`'s favicon cache (atomic `NamedTemporaryFile`+`os.replace`); `SHOWTIME_CACHE_PATH` env (defaults to `~/.cache/whoeverwants/showtimes_cache.json`, NOT `/app/cache` — the favicon cache uses the latter). ≤1 SUCCESSFUL upstream fetch/day/market. `_haversine_miles` (from `routers/search.py`) is the radius filter against the directory. **`get_market_showtimes` NEVER caches an empty normalized result, and RETRIES the fetch once after `_RETRY_DELAY_SECONDS` (3.0s) when it's empty.** Root cause it fixes (real prod incident, June 2026): a transient upstream blip made `fetch_market` return `({}, {})` → `normalize` returned `[]` → the empty list got cached, and the read path serves on `cached is not None`, so EVERY user in that market got `[]` for the rest of the day — surfacing as the FE's "No Alamo showtimes found near here. Try a wider radius." even though the feed was healthy on the next request. Diagnostic signature: prod `GET /api/showtimes/nearby` returns `films: 0` + `has_sessions: false` for cinemas that ARE in radius (the directory/radius works, only the sessions are empty), while canary returns films — same code, different cached state. The poisoned entry is `<market_id>:<today>` -> `[]` in the cache file. **Recovery for a live tier (the code fix won't reach prod until a release):** the empty entry is held in-memory per uvicorn worker (`--workers 2`), so deleting the disk cache file ALONE doesn't help — `rm` the file AND `docker compose restart api` to reload both workers' `_load()`. Empty == failure is an accepted conflation (Alamo markets always carry upcoming showtimes, so a genuinely-empty market is indistinguishable from a transient failure); the retry+no-cache is co-located in the cache layer because the user-visible symptom is "empty result" regardless of whether the cause was an HTTP error or an empty payload. Tests: `server/tests/test_showtimes_cache.py` (no-cache-on-empty, retry-recovers-within-one-request, no-retry-on-nonempty). **FIXED (June 2026): the `_showtime(...)` + `_DirectoryCinema(...)` fixtures in this file were stale — they predated the `brand`/`group_key` fields on `Showtime` (brand-prefix work) and the `address` field on `_DirectoryCinema` (theater directory), so all 3 tests failed with `TypeError: missing required positional arguments`. The fixtures now pass `brand=None, group_key="dune"` and `address=None`; the cache logic they cover was always correct, only the fixtures lagged. If you add another required field to either dataclass, update this fixture too.**
- **`GET /api/showtimes/nearby?lat&lng&radius&days=21`** (`routers/showtimes.py`, dedicated router — heavy cached catalog, not a per-keystroke proxy; identity-free like `/preview`). Resolves in-radius cinemas → fetches their distinct markets **concurrently** (`asyncio.gather`) → filters to in-radius + horizon → groups by film. Returns `{reference, horizon_days, cinemas:[{cinema_id,name,slug,distance_miles,has_sessions}] (distance-sorted), films:[{film_id,name,year,rating,runtime,poster_url,sessions:[{key,session_id,cinema_id,cinema_name,cinema_slug,date,time,datetime,format,seats_left,sales_url}]}]}`. The FE loads it ONCE then filters/curates client-side. Rewrites added in `next.config.ts`. `lib/api/showtimes.ts: apiShowtimesNearby`.
- **The theater directory carries a hand-entered `address`** (`server/data/alamo_cinemas.json`). Neither Alamo upstream feed (legacy sessions OR modern catalog) ships a street address — the directory JSON is the only place it lives, alongside the lat/lng coords. It threads through `_DirectoryCinema.address` (`alamo.py: load_directory` reads `c.get("address")`, nullable) → the `/api/showtimes/nearby` cinema dict → `ShowtimeCinema.address` (FE type). When adding a metro, fill in each cinema's `address` along with lat/lng (the feeds give id/name/slug/tz for free).
- **The theater-selection step is a centered, wrapping list of pill bubbles** (NOT a checkbox list). Each bubble: theater name on line 1 (`text-[12.8px]`), `distance (blue) · address` on line 2, with the **address truncating to the name's width** via the `w-0 min-w-full` trick (line 2 is `flex w-0 min-w-full`, so only the name contributes to the bubble's intrinsic width and the address `truncate`s to it instead of widening the bubble — the canonical "truncate to sibling width" CSS pattern). Selected = blue tint (no round checkbox). Container is `flex flex-wrap justify-center gap-[6.4px]`.
- **Create flow = `app/create-poll/ShowtimeCreateFlow.tsx`** (rendered in the create-poll modal top card when `category === 'showtime'`): reference location + radius gate → **theater search runs AUTOMATICALLY** (a 400ms-debounced `useEffect` on `[refLatitude, refLongitude, searchRadius]` calls `loadShowtimes()` whenever the location/radius is set or changes — there is NO manual "Find theaters" button; a centered "Finding theaters near …" line shows while `loading`. `loadShowtimes` resets the downstream theater/film/day selections on each run, so a radius change re-searches from scratch) → **theater-selection step** (the in-radius `cinemas` list, nearest-first, as centered bubbles — see the bullet above; ≥1 must be selected) → filterable movie picker (only films playing at selected theaters; counts update with selection) → `DaysSelector inline compact allowedDays` → per-day `ShowtimeBubbles` curation (filtered to selected theaters). Writes back `options` (curated keys) + `options_metadata` + `forField` (= film name → "Showtime for {Film}" auto-title). `selectedCinemas`/`selectedFilmId`/curated are ephemeral component state (catalog re-fetched on reopen; `QuestionDraft.showtimeFilmId` persists the pick). **The create-form inline gates exclude showtime** (`isSuggestionMode`, `inlineFormIsRankedChoice`, `showOptionsCard`, the Context/Collect-Suggestions rows) AND **include it** in `pollHasPlusOneDefaultType` — so the bottom card shows only Voting Cutoff + plus-ones (ON) + Emoji, like limited_supply. When adding a new bottom-card setting, gate it on `pollHasRankedChoice`/`pollHasPrephase` (both exclude showtime) — don't gate on a bare `category !== 'yes_no'` exclusion list.
- **Ballot = `components/QuestionBallot/ShowtimeBallotSection.tsx`** (early-return branch in `QuestionBallot`, like limited_supply): single-phase tri-state over `question.options` via `ShowtimeBubbles mode="vote"`, want/neutral/can't, wrapper Submit + name gate + plus-ones (showtime is NOT in `SELF_SUBMIT_TYPES`, so it's wrapper-owned like time/ranked_choice). `voteDataBuilders.ts` has a `showtime` case (liked/disliked, `vote_type:'showtime'`). `submitVote` commits immediately (no confirm modal, like time). Committed reactions restore on edit (the `showtime` branch in the vote-data restore effect). Results fetch gate includes showtime.
- **`ShowtimeBubbles` (`components/ShowtimeBubbles.tsx`)** — **one full-width ROW per showtime, day-grouped (`flex-col`, NOT flex-wrap pills)**, deliberately NOT routed through `expandHourRowsToQuarters` (that snaps to :00/:15/:30/:45; real showtimes aren't on 15-min boundaries). Two modes share the layout: `curate` (2-state include/exclude) + `vote` (3-state want/neutral/can't, with a legend). `disabled` = read-only. **Drag-to-select + bulk-mark IS now ported from `TimeSlotBubbles`** (see the dedicated bullet below) — the earlier "tap-cycling only" note is retired. `slotsFromOptions(options, metadata)` (exported here) is the shared builder for `options + options_metadata → ShowtimeSlot[]` used by the ballot + results.
  - **Single-line row styling.** Each showtime is a `ShowtimeBubbleButton` child = a flex row (`flex items-center gap-1`) of: the **selectable card** (`flex-1` bordered `rounded-md` button) holding `{12h time}{AM/PM} · {location name · address, truncated} · {format}` on ONE line, then the **seats "N left" as an underlined ticket LINK to the RIGHT, OUTSIDE the card** (content-width, flush-right, `gap-1` = minimal gap). The period is tinted via `periodColorClass` (orange AM / purple PM) from the local `fmt12Parts(time)` split; the location name is per-location color-tinted; the address truncates (`flex-1 min-w-0 truncate`); `"Digital"` (the default format) is omitted, only distinctive formats (70mm / The Big Show) show. The card is `text-sm` (the layout was deliberately backed off ~20% from an earlier ultra-dense multi-per-row version).
  - **Seats text IS the Alamo ticketing link (no separate icon).** The `"N left"` (or `"Tickets"` fallback when seat count is unknown) is an `<a href={sales_url} target=_blank>` with `underline`, a SIBLING of the card (not inside it), with `onClick stopPropagation` so tapping it opens tickets without toggling the vote/curate state. Works in EVERY mode (curate / vote / disabled). Rule-respecting handoff to Alamo's stable theater+movie showpage (live price + seat map live only in their Cloudflare-gated checkout SPA — scraping would be ToS circumvention). `sales_url` rides `options_metadata[key].sales_url` (surfaced by `slotsFromOptions`); the create flow's inline `curateSlots` map must include it too (it doesn't go through `slotsFromOptions`). `window.open(_blank)` returning null (sandboxed iframes) falls back to same-tab `window.location.href`. The earlier long-press-to-open gesture (arm timer + `armed` ring + `openedRef`) was REMOVED in favor of this explicit link. The results view keeps its winner-only "Get tickets →" button (`QuestionResults.tsx: ShowtimeResults`).
  - **Drag-to-select range + bulk-mark (ported from `TimeSlotBubbles`).** A plain tap cycles ONE row's state; a press-and-DRAG across rows lassoes a contiguous range (constrained to the day the drag started in, via `rangeBetween` + `parseSlotDate`) and raises a fixed bottom `ModalPortal` toolbar to bulk-apply: **vote mode → Want/Can't/Neutral (`applyVote`), curate mode → Include/Exclude (`applyCurate`)**, plus a × cancel; tapping outside the rows/toolbar also deselects. Mechanics mirror `TimeSlotBubbles` verbatim: each card carries `data-slot` + `data-slot-available="true"` + `touch-action:none` + `select-none` + `WebkitTouchCallout:'none'` (so a long press never selects the row text / pops iOS's copy magnifier, and a drag lassoes instead of scrolling/swiping), the gesture runs on window `pointermove`/`up` listeners with `document.elementFromPoint(...).closest('[data-slot-available="true"]')` mapping the finger to a row, an 8px `DRAG_THRESHOLD` distinguishes tap from drag, and `selection.size` gates the outside-tap-deselect effect (load-bearing — re-runs when 0→N so the listener attaches). **`React.memo` on the row is intentionally NOT used (matches the source — rows are ≤ a few dozen).**
  - **BULK-MARK STALE-STATE TRAP (`ShowtimeBallotSection.toggle`).** The toolbar fires `onToggle` once per selected key in a SYNCHRONOUS loop, so `toggle` MUST use functional `setLikedSlots(prev => …)` / `setDislikedSlots(prev => …)` updates — reading `likedSlots`/`dislikedSlots` out of the closure makes every call see the same stale array, so only the LAST key survives (the reported "only one of the selected gets marked" bug). The prop types are `Dispatch<SetStateAction<string[] | null>>` (widened from `(v) => void`); `QuestionBallot`'s `setLikedSlotsTouched` forwards updater functions through transparently. The curate path (`ShowtimeCreateFlow.toggleCurate`) already used `setCurated(prev => …)`, so it was unaffected. Mirrors `TimeBallotSection`'s onToggle.
  - **State is conveyed by border + background ONLY** (`classFor`: green/red/gray border + `bg-*-50`), never text color — so the AM/PM tint + the per-location color + the muted seats stay consistent across want/neutral/can't (same principle as the time-slot bubbles' always-colored period column). Three independent color channels: **state** (border/bg), **time-of-day** (AM/PM tint), **location** (cinema-name tint).
  - **Per-location colors + top distance legend.** `buildLocationMap(slots)` (memoized) assigns each distinct theater a stable color from `LOCATION_COLORS` (palette ordered blue/cyan/pink/teal first to dodge the green-want/red-can't/orange-purple-AMPM hues), **keyed on `cinema_id`** (canonical, survives renames + same-name theaters; falls back to the stripped display name only when an id is absent — `cinemaKeyOf`), ordered nearest-first by distance. A top legend renders one `dot + name + "{N} mi"` per theater; the cinema name in each pill is tinted to match. **`distance_miles` is persisted into `options_metadata` at create time** (`ShowtimeCreateFlow` builds a `distanceByCinema` map from `catalog.cinemas[].distance_miles`) and read back in `slotsFromOptions`, so the legend shows distances on the ballot + results, not just the create flow. Distances are from the CREATOR's reference location (immutable once curated). **`cinemaShortName` NO LONGER strips the "Alamo " prefix** — names show in full ("Alamo Richardson"); it's now just a trim/null-guard. The **street address is threaded exactly like `distance_miles`**: `catalog.cinemas[].address` → an `addressByCinema` map in `ShowtimeCreateFlow` → `options_metadata[key].address` → `slotsFromOptions` → `ShowtimeSlot.address`, rendered (truncated) on the row. Both create-flow metadata sites (the persisted `useEffect` + the `curateSlots` render map) must include it.
  - **Create flow renders ONE combined `ShowtimeBubbles` across all selected days** (it groups by day internally), so the legend + color assignment are computed once over the full curated set rather than per-day (which would repeat the legend + risk reassigning colors when a near theater is absent on a given day).
- **Results = `ShowtimeResults` (closed: winner showtime + cinema/format + "N can attend · M want" + ticketing link, then the disabled bubble grid) + `CompactShowtimePreview` (group-card winner-time pill, green closed / blue open)** in `QuestionResults.tsx`; wired into `GroupCardItem.plainResultForQuestion`. `🎬` icon via `QUESTION_TYPE_SYMBOLS.showtime` + `getQuestionLabel` ("Showtime") + `_QUESTION_TYPE_SYMBOLS`/`_CATEGORY_LABELS` (server). Showtime is a `BUILT_IN_TYPES` entry (🎬), gated OUT of `isAutocompleteCategory` (catalog-sourced picker, not per-option Nominatim).
- **Demo caveat:** a CLOSED showtime poll is hidden from a fresh viewer by the closed-before-join filter (a screenshot browser "joins now"), so demo with OPEN polls or a sign-in link to the creator. Push delivery / results-as-anonymous can't be screenshot; the winner math is covered by `test_showtime_poll.py`. Tests: `server/tests/test_showtimes_adapter.py` (adapter join/dedup/radius against a real-payload fixture, no live network) + `test_showtime_poll.py` (algorithm + create/vote/winner over the DB).

### Time Question Type

- **Two-phase flow**: availability phase (voters submit `voter_day_time_windows`) → preferences phase (voters submit `liked_slots`/`disliked_slots` after cutoff). When the poll wrapper has `allow_pre_ranking !== false` (the default), the preferences phase opens for each voter as soon as they've submitted availability — they don't wait for the cutoff. See "Pre-ranking for time polls" below.
- **Time ballots submit immediately on Submit — NO confirmation modal (both phases).** `QuestionBallot.handleVoteClick` short-circuits to `void submitVote()` for `question_type === 'time'` (after all validation, including the availability-window overlap check) instead of opening `showVoteConfirmModal`. Yes/No and ranked-choice keep their confirm modal. **Pitfall this exposed (fixed):** removing the modal removed the incidental render cycle that let the wrapper's `externalVoterName` prop catch up. The wrapper (`PollDetailPage`) computes `savedUserName = getUserName()` at render time and passes it as `externalVoterName`; when a nameless user submits, `AccountGateModal.onSubmit` `saveUserName`s then SYNCHRONOUSLY replays the submit via `triggerSubmit()` → `handleVoteClick` → `submitVote()` before React re-renders, so the prop is still the stale `""` → server 400 "Voter name is required". Fix: `submitVote`'s `trimmedVoterName` falls back to `getUserName()?.trim()` when the prop-derived `voterName` is empty (the same source the name-gate verified). Any future "submit synchronously right after a localStorage write" path that reads a render-time-snapshotted prop needs the same fresh-read fallback — the prop won't reflect the write until the next render.
- **Slot finalization at cutoff**: `_finalize_time_slots()` runs at availability cutoff, applies the **"Minimum Participants" viability gate** (`filter_slots_by_min_participants()` — keeps slots that at least `time_min_participants` people can attend, default 2), deduplicates via `_keep_longest_per_start_time()`, and writes the surviving slots to `question.options`. When availability WAS collected but no slot met the gate it instead sets `options='[]'` + `time_event_cancelled=true` ("event's off"). The shared `_compute_candidate_time_slots(question, votes) → (slots, has_availability)` (in `server/services/questions.py`) is used both here AND for the pre-ranking tentative-slots path; the returned `has_availability` bool is what lets `_finalize_time_slots` distinguish "no slot cleared the bar → cancel" from "nobody submitted availability yet → don't cancel, keep all creator slots" without re-scanning votes. See "Minimum Participants" below for the full model. (The legacy relative `filter_slots_by_min_availability()` / `min_availability_percent` column survive for back-compat but no longer drive finalization for new polls.)
- **Pre-ranking for time polls (`allow_pre_ranking` on `polls`).** When set (default true), voters can react to candidate slots before the availability cutoff. `_compute_results` returns the dynamically-computed candidate list as `options` with `options_are_tentative=true`; the FE `questionResults.options_are_tentative` flag drives the new `isAvailabilitySubmission` derived state in `QuestionBallot.tsx`. Voter UX is sequential: availability form → submit → bubble UI with tentative slots → submit preferences → summary. The slot list shifts as more voters submit availability; likes/dislikes the voter cast on slots that subsequently get eliminated remain in their vote row but are silently ignored by the winner algorithm (only slots in `question.options` after finalization count). To edit availability after submitting, voters Forget the question and redo (no separate "Edit availability" affordance in this iteration). Disable the toggle to restore the old "wait for cutoff" behavior. Three load-bearing pieces of plumbing:
  - **`include_tentative_time_options` kwarg on `_compute_results`** (`server/services/questions.py`) — defaults `True` (per-question results endpoint), but the bulk `polls_for_poll_ids` path (`/api/groups/mine` + `/by-route-id/{id}`, on a 5s page refresh tick) passes `False` to skip the slot-generation pass. Without this gate, every active group tab with N time polls triggers `_compute_candidate_time_slots` for each poll twice every 5s — the algorithm is O(days × windows × durations × starts × voters) and dominates server CPU on the hot loop. The per-question `/api/questions/<id>/results` call still populates tentative options for the ballot UI; `QuestionBallot`'s `apiGetQuestionResults` cache write merges in.
  - **`isAvailabilitySubmission` vs `inAvailabilityPhase`** (`QuestionBallot.tsx`) — pair of distinct concepts that USED to be one boolean. `inAvailabilityPhase` is the wrapper-level "slots aren't finalized yet" gate that drives the "Collecting Availability" status label and the tentative-slots amber notice. `isAvailabilitySubmission` is the per-voter active-form gate: true while filling the availability inputs, false while reacting to tentative bubbles. Both flow through to `TimeBallotSection`; the summary header label, the input-vs-bubble JSX fork, and `voteDataBuilders` all switch on `isAvailabilitySubmission`. `BallotInputs.isAvailabilitySubmission` in `voteDataBuilders.ts` is the renamed field (was `inAvailabilityPhase`) — keeps the wire-format decision local to the builder.
  - **Inline `setUserVoteData(v)` on first-time submits, not just edits** (`QuestionBallot.tsx: submitVote` + `prepareBatchVoteItem.commit`). Was `if (isEditing) setUserVoteData(v)`. Pre-ranking time flow reads `userVoteData.voter_day_time_windows` synchronously to derive `hasSubmittedAvailability`, which feeds `isAvailabilitySubmission`. Without the inline write, the first-time-submit path leaves `userVoteData` null until the loadVoteData useEffect re-fetches — causing a one-cycle flash through the "Your availability:" summary before the bubble UI lands. The companion `await fetchQuestionResults()` after submission (gated on `time + inAvailabilityPhase + allow_pre_ranking !== false`) lands the tentative options in the same paint cycle.
- **`components/TimeQuestionFields.tsx` is now exclusively voter-facing and renders NO day-picker.** The create-poll form inlines `DayTimeWindowsInput`/`DaysSelector` directly (the creator defines the days); `TimeQuestionFields` is only mounted by `QuestionBallot/TimeBallotSection.tsx` (active availability form + the read-only submitted-availability summary). A voter replies *within* the creator-defined days, so the old "Select Days / Add/Remove Days" button + its `DaysSelector` were removed — voters toggle availability via the per-window checkboxes (`isVoterForm` branch in `DayTimeWindowsInput`, gated on a truthy `questionWindows`) but can't add or remove days. The `highlightDaysButton` prop went with it; `renderDaysSection` still gates the label + day rows. Don't reintroduce a day-picker here — if a creator-side embedded use ever returns, render the picker at that call site, not inside the voter component.
- **`TimeWindow.enabled?: boolean`** in `lib/types.ts` is the per-window "on/off" toggle the voter form writes. **The availability algorithms (FE `isVoterAvailableForSlot` + server `_voter_available_at`) do NOT read it.** Disabled windows must be stripped before submission, AND the day must be dropped if all its windows are disabled — leaving an empty `windows` array on a day means "all day available" per the helper's contract, which silently inverts the voter's "I'm NOT available" intent. The `voteDataBuilders.ts` time-availability branch handles both filters; mirror the pattern in any future direct-submit path. Submit-button disabled-state gating also reads `w.enabled !== false` so an all-unchecked state surfaces as a disabled CTA rather than silently sending null availability.
- **"Minimum Participants" viability gate (migration 129) — RESOLVES the old min-availability-model TODO.** Replaces the relative `min_availability_percent` % filter with a single, intuitive per-slot **viability gate**: a time slot counts only if at least `time_min_participants` people are available for it (default **2**). At the availability cutoff, slots below the threshold are dropped; if NO slot clears the bar the poll resolves to an explicit **"event's off"** terminal state (`time_event_cancelled=true`, `options=[]`) rather than a thin/empty ballot. The owner converged on this (the per-slot viability-gate interpretation, plain absolute count, explicit cancel) after comparing three prototype variants on separate dev servers (split filter+gate / single headcount / min-max range — all retired). Wiring:
  - **Schema (migration 129):** `questions.time_min_participants INTEGER` + `questions.time_event_cancelled BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT false`. The old `min_availability_percent` column + `filter_slots_by_min_availability()` survive for back-compat but no longer drive new-poll finalization.
  - **Gate only applies once availability is collected.** `_compute_candidate_time_slots` skips the gate (keeps all creator slots) when no vote carries `voter_day_time_windows` — otherwise an availability-phase-off poll (finalized at create with zero votes) or a phase that closed empty would cancel everything (every count is 0 < 2). This rule lives in ONE place: the function returns `(slots, has_availability)` so `_finalize_time_slots` cancels iff `has_availability and not slots` without re-scanning.
  - **Display: results-level flag only.** `time_event_cancelled` rides `QuestionResultsResponse` (+ `toQuestionResults` + FE `QuestionResults`); `TimeResults` shows a "No time works — the event's off" banner and `CompactTimePreview` shows an "Event's off" pill. It is deliberately NOT surfaced on the per-question read path (`QuestionResponse`/`Question`) — nothing consumes it there; `time_min_participants` IS on the question read path (duplicate-restore). The results branch reads `time_event_cancelled` straight off the question DB row (`p.*`), independent of `QuestionResponse`.
  - **FE create form:** a single "Minimum Participants" row (`<CompactNumberRow>`) in the bottom settings card, in the same slot "Minimum Votes" occupies for ranked_choice, gated on `showTimeFields && collectAvailability`. Draft field `QuestionDraft.minParticipants` (default 2) → API `min_participants` (`server/models.py: CreateQuestionRequest.min_participants: int = 2`) → `questions.time_min_participants`. The old "Minimum Availability %" card + `MinimumParticipationModal` are deleted.
  - **`<CompactNumberRow>` (`components/CompactNumberRow.tsx`)** is the shared label-+-tap-to-edit-number settings row, used by BOTH "Minimum Votes" (`CompactMinResponsesField`, which adds the "Show Results After Min Votes" toggle below it) and "Minimum Participants". Reuse it for any new numeric settings row rather than re-inlining the isEditing/inputRef edit-toggle.
  - **Auto-closed on cancel (SHIPPED).** When a poll's ENTIRE content is a cancelled time event, it auto-closes (`close_reason='cancelled'`) so the closed-gated "event's off" banner (`TimeResults`) surfaces. `services/questions.py: _maybe_close_cancelled_event_poll(conn, poll_id, now)` is the shared primitive — it closes iff every question is a `time` question AND every one is `time_event_cancelled` (so a multi-question poll with any non-time or uncancelled-time section stays open; the single-question time poll, the common case, closes). Called from BOTH the inline `cutoff-availability` endpoint (fires the poll-CLOSED push inline, sets `close_notified=true`, instead of a "voting is open" transition push) AND the cron tick's transition pass (excludes the cancelled poll from the transition-push loop; its close push fires the NEXT tick via step 2's un-notified-close claim, since the cancel-close happens after step 2 already ran that tick). `close_reason='cancelled'` is a new value stored on `polls.close_reason` (plain TEXT, no CHECK constraint; the FE treats `close_reason` as `string | null` and only switches on `manual`/`max_capacity`/`deadline`, so `cancelled` falls through harmlessly). The legacy per-question GET-read finalize path (`routers/questions.py`) is deliberately NOT auto-closed — closing as a side effect of a read would be surprising + inconsistent (only the tick + cutoff endpoint are deadline actors). Tests: `test_notification_events.py::test_cutoff_availability_cancels_unviable_event` + `::test_tick_cancels_unviable_time_event`. (The compact "Event's off" pill shows regardless of closed.)
  - **Seeding a time-poll demo via API (lesson):** the availability phase needs BOTH the poll-level `prephase_deadline_minutes` (arms the clock) AND the question-level `suggestion_deadline_minutes` (marks the question as having a phase) — sending only the question-level one makes `cutoff-availability` 400 with "No availability phase to cut off" (mirrors `social_tests/conftest.py: create_poll`, which injects the poll-level value when a question carries the per-question one). To demonstrate "event's off": give the creator one wide window + duration, then have several voters each available at DISJOINT single slots so no slot reaches 2.
  - **Tests:** `server/tests/test_time_poll.py: TestFilterSlotsByMinParticipants`.
- **"Attendance Leeway" (exclusion tolerance, migration 138).** Creator-set integer (default **0**) controlling how many fewer attendees than the best-attended slot a time may have and still reach the preference phase. A slot survives iff `max_attendance − slot_attendance ≤ tolerance`. Tolerance 0 → only the best-attended slot(s) are offered for preference voting (so the flexible-majority's availability decides, and preferences just break ties among the best slots — this is what makes "everyone marks availability, nobody bothers with preferences" produce the best-attended time instead of the *earliest* one, which was the old `_pick_winner_from_reactions` failure mode). Raising it widens the field so a slightly-less-attended-but-otherwise-preferable slot can stay in the running. Wiring:
  - **Schema (migration 138):** `questions.exclusion_tolerance INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0`. Rides every question read path (`p.*` → `_row_to_question` → `QuestionResponse.exclusion_tolerance` → `toQuestion` → FE `Question.exclusion_tolerance`); set on INSERT in `routers/polls.py` (clamped `max(0, …)`, 0 for non-time questions to satisfy NOT NULL).
  - **Algorithm:** `filter_slots_by_exclusion_tolerance(slots, availability_counts, tolerance)` (`algorithms/time_question.py`), applied in `_compute_candidate_time_slots` **AFTER** `filter_slots_by_min_participants` — the absolute viability gate decides whether the event happens at all (and "event's off"); leeway only *narrows* the surviving field. The best slot always survives (`max − max = 0 ≤ tolerance`), so it never empties a non-empty list. Because `_compute_candidate_time_slots` feeds BOTH `_finalize_time_slots` (persists `options`) and the pre-ranking tentative-slots path, the filter applies live + at cutoff with no extra code.
  - **FE create form:** a second `<CompactNumberRow label="Attendance Leeway">` directly under "Minimum Participants", same `showTimeFields && collectAvailability` gate, `min={0}`. Draft field `QuestionDraft.exclusionTolerance` (default 0) → `draftToQuestionParams` → API `exclusion_tolerance`. Persisted in `questionFormState`; carried through the duplicate flow (`buildQuestionSnapshot` now also includes it AND `time_min_participants` — the latter was a pre-existing dead read fixed in passing).
  - **Inline ⓘ via `CompactNumberRow.labelInfo` (new prop).** `CompactNumberRow` gained an optional `labelInfo?: ReactNode` rendered as a **sibling of** the `<label>` (not a child — so the info button's own click never triggers the label→input association). The leeway row passes `<OutcomeInfoButton align="left" text="…" />` (the codebase's existing grey inline-info popover, reused rather than a bespoke modal).
  - **Display: orange "excludes N" badge is ABSOLUTE, not relative.** Per slot the badge shows how many availability respondents that slot leaves out — `availability_respondents − availability_counts[slot]` — so **even the best-attended slot shows its real exclusion** (the owner's explicit requirement: "indicate that the best slot still excludes n people"). Both the preference ballot (`TimeSlotBubbles.excludedCount`) and the results page (`QuestionResults: TimeResults`, per-cell `unavailable` + the "X of Y available" winner line) use the respondent total as the denominator. **`QuestionResults.availability_respondents`** (new field, migration-free) = the weighted headcount of everyone who submitted availability (submitter + plus-ones); computed in `calculate_time_question_results`, rides `QuestionResultsResponse` → `toQuestionResults` → FE `QuestionResults`. Both renderers fall back to `max_availability` (the old relative baseline) when the field is absent (pre-deploy cached results). NOTE: because the badge is absolute, it is NO LONGER equal to the configured leeway value — leeway gates which slots *survive* (relative, `max − count ≤ tolerance`), the badge *displays* absolute exclusion (`respondents − count`); the two are intentionally different numbers.
  - **Tests:** `server/tests/test_time_poll.py: TestFilterSlotsByExclusionTolerance`.
- **Per-voter "minimum participants" = conditional attendance (migration 130).** A time RESPONDENT can attach a personal turnout threshold to their availability ("only count me for a slot if at least N people total are available"). It's the per-voter mirror of the creator's gate above and composes with it. The owner chose the "conditional attendance" semantics over an aggregated-preference or informational-only reading.
  - **Algorithm: `compute_slot_availability` returns EFFECTIVE counts** (`server/algorithms/time_question.py`). For each slot it gathers the available voters' thresholds (`_voter_threshold`, default **1** = no constraint) and resolves a fixed point via **`_effective_attendance(thresholds)`** = the largest k such that ≥ k voters have a threshold ≤ k (sort ascending; max k where `s[k-1] <= k`). Dropping a voter from a slot lowers the count, which can push another voter below their threshold, cascading — this is the closed-form of the greedy-removal fixed point (verified against a brute-force reference over 20k random cases in `TestEffectiveAttendance`). All-default thresholds (1) reduce to the prior raw count, so existing polls are unaffected. The creator's viability gate (`filter_slots_by_min_participants`) then runs against these effective counts, so the two compose; and the displayed "X of Y available" + winner use effective counts. The like/dislike winner tiebreak is intentionally NOT changed (a dropped voter's preference on a surviving slot still counts — v1 scope; the threshold drives viability/availability, not the preference aggregation).
  - **Schema (migration 130):** `votes.voter_min_participants INTEGER` (nullable; NULL = no constraint). Threaded through `SubmitVoteRequest`/`EditVoteRequest`/`PollVoteItem`/`VoteResponse`, the insert/edit SQL (written directly, NOT COALESCEd — the preferences-phase edit re-sends it from `userVoteData`, same reasoning as `voter_day_time_windows`), `_row_to_vote`, the `_finalize_time_slots` votes SELECT (must list it explicitly — that query doesn't `SELECT *`), and the `_vote_item_to_{submit,edit}_req` converters. All time votes go through `apiSubmitPollVotes`, so the builders in `voteDataBuilders.ts` are the single FE emit point.
  - **FE: NO on/off toggle.** A `<CompactNumberRow>` "Minimum people for me to come" in the availability ballot (`TimeBallotSection`). The value itself encodes the constraint (the owner rejected a `SliderSwitch` as redundant). **Floored at the creator's poll minimum** (`time_min_participants`, "the original min") — a personal min below that is meaningless since the slot already requires it. `QuestionBallot` computes `pollMinParticipants = Math.max(1, question.time_min_participants ?? 2)`, seeds the field default to it, passes it as the field `min` (`voterMinParticipantsFloor`), and **sends `voter_min_participants` only when the value is strictly above the floor** (else null = no extra constraint). Persisted in the ballot draft (`voterMinParticipantsValue`) and restored from the committed vote (only when `> floor`).
- **`<CompactNumberRow>` can be backspaced to empty while editing.** The edit input holds a local string `draft` (seeded from `value` on edit-start) so it can sit empty mid-type without snapping back to the old number — valid in-range values live-commit via `setValue`, but an empty / below-`min` draft leaves the committed value alone until **`commit()` (blur / Enter) clamps it UP to `min`**. This is the canonical "clear-and-retype + auto-correct-to-floor" behavior and applies to every `CompactNumberRow` (creator Minimum Votes / Minimum Participants too). Don't reintroduce the old "controlled `value={value}` number input that ignores empty onChange" form — it made the field impossible to clear.
- **Preference-phase bubbles must be filtered per-voter by their availability.** A voter who said they can't attend a slot in the availability phase should not see (or be able to react to) that slot in the preferences phase. `preferenceSlotsForVoter` in `QuestionBallot.tsx` runs `isVoterAvailableForSlot()` (from `lib/timeUtils.ts`) against the loaded `userVoteData.voter_day_time_windows` and passes the filtered list to `TimeSlotBubbles`. Voters who never submitted availability see every finalized slot.
- **`null` vs `[]` semantics for liked/disliked slots**: `null` = voter hasn't submitted preferences yet; `[]` = submitted with all bubbles neutral. The frontend uses this distinction to show an implicit edit prompt (hasNotReactedYet).
- **Preferences-phase vote edits MUST re-send `voter_day_time_windows` + `voter_duration`.** `_edit_vote_on_question` writes both columns directly (not `COALESCE`d), because the same NULL-on-the-wire value carries two different meanings: in the availability phase it means "clear my availability" (abstain edit), but in the preferences phase the FE just leaves the field out of its UI. Symptom of forgetting: a voter who submits availability in phase 1, then submits preferences in phase 2, ends up with `voter_day_time_windows = NULL` because the phase-2 edit overwrote it — `compute_slot_availability` then returns 0 for every slot (winner is still picked from likes/dislikes, but the displayed "X of Y available" reads "0 of 0"). The fix lives in the FE: `voteDataBuilders.ts` preferences-phase branch reads `state.userVoteData?.voter_day_time_windows` / `voter_duration` and passes them through, so the SQL UPDATE writes the existing values back (effective no-op). Don't try to fix this with `COALESCE` server-side — that breaks the legitimate availability-phase abstain flow.
- **`TimeBallotSection` post-submit summary must skip the `hasVoted && !isEditingVote` branch when the voter has not reacted yet.** A voter who submitted availability in phase 1 transitions into phase 2 with `hasVoted=true` but `liked_slots/disliked_slots` still `null`. Without an explicit gate, the post-submit summary's ternary chain falls through to `null` — leaving `"Your preferences:"` sitting above the wrapper Submit with nothing in between. The gate is `const hasNotReactedYet = !inAvailabilityPhase && hasVoted && userVoteData?.liked_slots === null && userVoteData?.disliked_slots === null && !userVoteData?.is_abstain;` and `if (hasVoted && !isEditingVote && !hasNotReactedYet) { ...summary... }`. Falling through renders the active preferences form so the voter can mark slots. Mirrors the parent's `hasNotReactedYet` in `QuestionBallot.tsx:780` (which feeds `canImplicitlyEdit` for the wrapper Submit visibility).
- **Submitted availability is shown by re-rendering `TimeQuestionFields` with `disabled={true}`**, populated from `userVoteData.voter_day_time_windows` and `userVoteData.voter_duration` rather than the active form state (which may have been reset post-submit). Pattern used in the post-submit summary in `components/QuestionBallot/TimeBallotSection.tsx`. Duration callbacks are spread conditionally on `userVoteData.voter_duration` truthiness — when the voter didn't constrain duration, omit all four `onDurationXxx` handlers so `TimeQuestionFields`'s "render the duration block only when all callbacks are provided" gate hides it instead of showing `0–0` defaults.
- **Submitted PREFERENCES are shown by re-rendering `<TimeSlotBubbles disabled={true}>`, NOT a text list.** The post-vote "Your preferences:" summary (the `hasVoted && !isEditingVote && !hasNotReactedYet` block in `TimeBallotSection.tsx`) renders the same bubble grid the voter used, greyed out, so they see their actual ballot (green/red bubbles + legend) instead of a "Liked: … / Disliked: …" sentence. Tapping the existing `editVoteButton` flips `isEditingVote` → falls through to the interactive instance below. Props mirror the active form EXCEPT: `onToggle={() => {}}` (no-op), `disabled={true}`, and liked/disliked are sourced from the COMMITTED `userVoteData.liked_slots`/`disliked_slots` (the active form sources from in-flight `likedSlots`/`dislikedSlots` state) — that divergence is intentional, the two instances render in mutually-exclusive view states. `disabled` mode in `TimeSlotBubbles` already gates every interactive bit (pointer-down returns early, no window listeners, the range-toolbar `ModalPortal` is gated on `!disabled`), so the heavier component is cheap to render read-only. This deliberately parallels the availability-summary pattern above (re-render the real ballot disabled, don't paraphrase it as text). The two `<TimeSlotBubbles>` render sites (disabled summary / enabled form) are kept separate, NOT unified behind a computed `disabled` prop — they sit in structurally different branches with different surrounding chrome (header + edit button vs "Mark Your Preferences" + abstain + submit).
- **Closed-state `TimeBallotSection` returns `null` unless the voter abstained.** The parent's closed-state block in `QuestionBallot.tsx:1244-1261` already renders `QuestionResultsDisplay` (the candidate slots) plus loading + "unable to load" fallbacks — so `TimeBallotSection`'s own closed branch only needs to add the "You Abstained" badge. An earlier version wrapped its (mostly empty) inner content in `py-6`, leaving ~48px of dead space below the candidate slots whenever the voter wasn't abstaining. When adding new closed-state content for a question type, audit whether the parent already handles it before adding a wrapping section.
- **Time results: heading is "Start Options" (no count) and the slot list is collapsed-by-default with fade + chevron.** `components/QuestionResults.tsx: TimeResults` wraps the day-rows divider div in `<CollapsibleStartOptions>`, which delegates to the shared `<CollapsibleFadeSection>` primitive (collapsed at 80px, 28px fade band). The legend (liked / disliked / unavail.) renders in the always-visible header next to the heading; only the rows themselves collapse. Same UX as the Notes field on a poll card.
- **Winner algorithm**: fewest dislikes → most likes → earliest slot key (chronological tiebreak). Implemented in `_pick_winner_from_reactions()` in `server/algorithms/time_question.py`.
- **Category "Time" in create form**: selecting it from the category dropdown keeps the standard form and injects `ParticipationConditions` + threshold slider + availability cutoff in place of options. Uses a single `{(questionType === 'time' || (questionType === 'question' && category === 'time'))}` condition — do NOT add a separate duplicate block for each case.
- **`formatDayLabel(dateStr)`** is the canonical day-label formatter in `lib/timeUtils.ts`. Use it in all time-related components instead of local copies.
- **Shared time-slot helpers** in `lib/timeUtils.ts`: `parseSlotStart`, `parseSlotDate`, `groupSlotsByDay`, `getBubbleLabel` (predecessor-aware compact label returning `{time, period}` — see "Time bubble grid layout" below for the case table), `expandHourRowsToQuarters` (pads each hour-row to four `:00/:15/:30/:45` `SlotCell { slot, available }` entries — synthetic ghost cells for missing positions use a slot-key WITHOUT an end time, e.g. `${date} 10:00`, distinct from real keys so liked/disliked Set lookups never collide), `periodColorClass(period)` (Tailwind class for AM=orange / PM=purple, used by `TimeSlotBubbles`, `QuestionResults`, AND `DayTimeWindowsInput`'s time-window pills — extract before introducing a 5th call site), `formatStackedDayLabel` (stacked weekday / month+day for the bubble grid row label), and `formatTimeSlot` (full "Mon, Apr 28 • 10:00 AM – 10:30 AM (30m)" label). `TimeSlotBubbles.tsx` (voting ballot) and `QuestionResults.tsx` (results view) both use these — never re-implement slot formatting locally.
- **Time bubble grid layout** (`components/TimeSlotBubbles.tsx` + the `QuestionResults` results-view start-options panel). Each day renders three columns: stacked day label (left, `w-12`) | period column (`w-7`, AM/PM badge, orange/purple via `periodColorClass`, empty when this row continues the previous period) | bubble grid (a 2-col CSS grid where col 1 holds the hour-anchor bubble and col 2 holds a `flex flex-wrap` of the minute bubbles, so col-2 wraps stay indented past col 1). One row per hour. Every row carries all four quarter-hour positions; missing positions render at the same `min-w-12 h-8 px-2` footprint but with no border/background + faded text (`text-gray-300 dark:text-gray-600`, `select-none`, `aria-hidden`) — ghost cells, so the columns line up vertically across rows. Bubble text uses Geist Mono (`font-mono` Tailwind class → `--font-geist-mono`); single-digit hours are NBSP-padded in `getBubbleLabel` (e.g. `  1:00`) so the digit/colon/minute aligns column-by-column with `10:00`/`11:00`/`12:00`. **NBSP, not a regular space**: `white-space: nowrap` still collapses leading regular spaces; NBSP survives. `getBubbleLabel` cases: `:MM` for same-hour-different-minute, `H:MM` for same-period-different-hour, `H:MM` + non-null `period` for first-of-row / period-change. Hour-anchor labels always include `:00` (no bare-hour form) so the format is uniform across the grid. Row-level `period` is read off the first cell's `getBubbleLabel(firstSlot, prevSlot).period`, where `prevSlot` is the previous hour-row's last cell's slot key (use `hourRows[rowIdx-1].at(-1).slot`, NOT a flat-array index — the old `hourRows.flat() + flatIdx + cellsWithPrev` orchestration was retired). The shared `SLOT_CELL_SIZE` string lives at module scope inside each consumer so the ghost branch + real branch agree on dimensions. **Per-day `useMemo`** wraps the `groupSlotsByDay + formatStackedDayLabel + expandHourRowsToQuarters` chain keyed on `[options]` — keep new transformations inside the same memo rather than re-running per render tick.
- **Slot keys `"YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM-HH:MM"` arrive from the backend already in chronological order.** Consumers that just group by day (`groupSlotsByDay`) do NOT need to re-sort the list first; the old list view only sorted because it reordered by dislikes/likes.
- **Drag-to-select range voting (preferences phase, `components/TimeSlotBubbles.tsx`).** A plain tap still cycles ONE bubble (neutral → liked → disliked → neutral); a DRAG across bubbles selects a contiguous **chronological** range (`options.slice(lo, hi+1)` via an `optionIndex` Map — the visual 2D grid is irrelevant, range is by slot order) **constrained to the day the drag STARTED in** (`rangeBetween` filters the slice to `parseSlotDate(slot) === parseSlotDate(anchor)`). Without the day clamp the chronological slice spills into the tail of the day-row above and the head of the day-row below the anchor — their bubbles line up in the same columns, so a horizontal drag in the middle row silently rings slots in the adjacent days the user never dragged over (reported bug). A drag is distinguished from a tap by an 8px `DRAG_THRESHOLD`. While ≥1 slot is selected, a fixed bottom-center toolbar (portaled via `ModalPortal`, so it never reflows the page) offers Like / Dislike / Neutral + a cancel ×; pressing one applies that state to every selected slot (loops the existing `onToggle`) and clears the selection. Selected bubbles get a flush `ring-2 ring-blue-500` (NO `ring-offset` — the outline hugs the bubble edge). Key mechanics:
  - **Gesture runs on POINTER events via window listeners, not React handlers.** `onPointerDown` on a bubble records an anchor + adds `window` `pointermove`/`pointerup`/`pointercancel` listeners; touch implicitly captures the pointer to the bubble but the events still bubble to `window`, and `document.elementFromPoint(x,y).closest('[data-slot-available="true"]')` maps the finger position to the hovered bubble (no `setPointerCapture` needed — works for mouse + touch). `onClick` was REMOVED; a no-drag `pointerup` calls `handleTap` (keyboard handled separately via `onKeyDown` Enter/Space) — so a drag never also fires a stray tap-cycle.
  - **Tap vs drag selection: `setSelection((prev) => prev.size === 0 ? prev : new Set())` on pointerdown** clears any prior selection without a redundant re-render when already empty.
  - **`effectiveSelection = options.filter(s => selection.has(s))`** intersects the live options so a stale selection (slots that vanished as tentative availability shifted) silently drops out; the toolbar gate + apply both read it. After dropping the "N selected" count text, `effectiveSelection` is still load-bearing (gate + apply) — don't delete it.
  - **Tap-outside-to-deselect (NOT drag-outside).** While a selection is active, a window-level capture-phase `pointerdown`+`pointerup` effect clears it when the user TAPS (sub-`DRAG_THRESHOLD` travel) outside both the selectable bubbles AND the floating toolbar — but a DRAG outside (scroll/swipe with a selection active) leaves it intact, so the page stays scrollable. "Inside" is matched via `target.closest('[data-slot-available="true"],[data-slot-toolbar="true"]')` — the data-attribute selector is portal-safe (the toolbar is `ModalPortal`'d to body, so `closest()` walks its real DOM parent chain, not the component tree). Capture phase so a bubble's own `stopPropagation` (touch-only) can't hide the event. A new drag begun ON a bubble starts inside → this never fights `handleBubblePointerDown`; a bubble tap already starts a fresh gesture. **Gated + dep'd on `selection.size`, NOT `effectiveSelection.length`** — the latter is a fresh array derived from `options`, so it would tear down + re-attach the window listeners on every `options` shift; `selection.size` only changes when the user acts. The tap-vs-drag check is the shared module-level `withinTapThreshold(x1,y1,x2,y2)` helper, reused by the in-bubble gesture's `onMove` too.
  - **Scroll + swipe-back blocking is scoped to the AVAILABLE bubbles only, not the grid/labels/ghosts.** Each available bubble `<button>` carries `touch-none` (`touch-action: none` → browser won't pan/scroll) AND `onTouchStart/Move/End/Cancel={e => e.stopPropagation()}` (keeps the ancestor `useSwipeBackGesture` React `onTouch*` handlers on PollDetail's `.touch-pan-y` wrapper from seeing the drag → no page-transition). Touch events target the element where the touch STARTED for the whole gesture, so blocking only on the bubble fully covers any drag begun there. Day labels, gaps, the grid container, and greyed-out ghost cells keep `touch-action: auto` so they scroll/swipe normally — verified via computed `touch-action` (`none` on bubbles, `auto` elsewhere) + a CDP touch test (rightward drag on a bubble leaves the swipe wrapper `transform: none`; the same drag on a ghost cell produces `translate3d(...)`). `touch-action` alone can't stop the JS swipe handler (it only governs the browser's native pan); the `stopPropagation` is what blocks the gesture, so BOTH are required.
- **Cap-height text centering for bubble labels**: time-slot bubble labels are pure cap-height text (digits, uppercase letters, colons — no descenders like g/j/y). `flex items-center` on a `leading-none` line box positions the **line box** at the bubble center, but the visible glyphs sit in the UPPER half of that line box because the space below the baseline is reserved for descenders that never appear — so the text looks "too high". Fix: use the modern CSS properties `text-box-trim: trim-both` + `text-box-edge: cap alphabetic` to shrink the text box to exactly the cap-height range, so flex centering aligns the visible glyphs instead of the padded line box. The shared `.cap-height-text` utility class in `app/globals.css` encapsulates the rule; use it on any `<span>` wrapping single-line, descender-free labels inside a centered container. Supported in Chromium 133+ / Safari 18.2+.
- **Availability cutoff requires `suggestion_deadline_minutes` to be set** on the question — the endpoint enforces `suggestion_deadline IS NULL AND suggestion_deadline_minutes IS NOT NULL`. Questions created without this field will fail the cutoff endpoint with 400.
- **`ChunkLoadError` after new builds**: the browser has stale cached chunks from the previous build. The lazy `CreateQuestionContent` import and the global `unhandledrejection` handler in `template.tsx` both auto-reload the page when this happens. The service worker uses network-first for JS chunks so new builds take effect immediately.
- **Autotitle convention**: time questions use `"Time?"` as the autotitle (matching the `BUILT_IN_TYPES` label), not a bespoke prompt like "When works?". Every branch of `generateTitle()` in `app/create-question/page.tsx` must call `appendFor(...)` on its return value so the "for X" suffix gets appended — the standalone `questionType === 'time'` fallback originally returned a raw string and silently dropped `forSuffix`.

### Day-Time-Windows Input (Creator Form)

- **The `+` button does NOT open the time-grid modal**; it appends a slot in place via `pickNextTimeWindow(targetDay, allDays)` (in `lib/timeUtils.ts`). The algorithm walks chronologically-previous days first (each day's slots tried latest-start first; the first one that doesn't overlap any existing slot on the target day wins), then chronologically-following days under the same rule, then falls back to `DEFAULT_TIME_WINDOW` (`08:00–17:00`). The `TimeGridModal` is now an **edit-only** surface — `handleEditApply` only handles the `editingIndex !== null` path. The `+ Time` amber-pill fallback for empty days is also gone since new days always inherit windows; if an empty-windows day ever happens, the always-on `+` column adds one.
- **`useDayTimeWindowsState.onWindowsChange` always sorts by start time.** Sorting is the single source of truth for "slots are in chronological order"; every consumer (the per-pill `intersectsPrev` validation, the `+ button`'s smart pick) trusts this. Don't store slots out-of-order anywhere — the sort lives in the hook so any new mutation that funnels through `onChange` is covered.
- **`useDayTimeWindowsState.onDaysSelected` inheritance**: a newly-added day's windows come from (1) the in-memory removed-day cache (re-adding a day restores its previous windows), else (2) the chronologically-previous day in the working list (deep-copied so edits don't propagate backward), else (3) `DEFAULT_TIME_WINDOW`. The auto-added "today" entry from `emptyDraft({category: 'time'})` and the parallel path in `app/create-poll/page.tsx` also seed with `DEFAULT_TIME_WINDOW` so the "first day" rule lands even when the day was auto-added.
- **`intersectsPrev` is per-pill validation, not a global error.** The styling is a soft orange outline (`border-orange-400 dark:border-orange-500`) on an otherwise-normal pill — read as a hint, not a blocker. Submission is not blocked. The duration-too-short check is a separate, harder error state (`bg-red-50` + red border + red text). Two separate flags fold into a single `pillVariant` selector keyed against `PILL_STATE_CLASSES` (`disabled | tooShort | intersecting | normal`).
- **Layout-stable pill borders: `PILL_BASE` always sets `border` (1 px reserved) + exactly one border-color class appended per pill via `pillBorderClass(variant, isVoterForm)`.** `PILL_STATE_CLASSES` carries ONLY bg/text/hover now — NO border-color — because the border color is form-aware: `tooShort`/`intersecting` keep their warning colors (red/orange) regardless of form; neutral pills (`normal`/`disabled`) are `border-transparent` in the **creator** form (borderless, composes into the create-poll `divide-y` card) but `border-gray-300 dark:border-gray-600` (a thin outline) in the **voter** form. The voter outline replaces the removed "Select time slots to fine-tune" hint — it signals the time-slot bubbles are tappable. **Don't fold the border color back into `PILL_STATE_CLASSES`**: a class string containing both `border-transparent` and `border-gray-300` hits Tailwind's source-order precedence (later-generated class wins, NOT class-attribute order), so the override is unreliable — exactly-one-border-color-per-pill is the invariant that avoids it. `pillVariant` only ever returns `normal`/`tooShort`/`intersecting`; the `disabled` variant is applied directly (not via `pillVariant`) by the voter form's **ghost re-add rows** (`PILL_STATE_CLASSES.disabled` + `pillBorderClass('disabled', isVoterForm)`), so its gray outline + `opacity-50` reads as a faded "tap to re-add this removed window" affordance. Switching states never shifts layout by ±1 px because the `border` width is constant.
- **Date column is fixed at `w-[88px]`** so the `+` button column to its right lands at the same X position on every row, regardless of how short any individual row's date / relative-day label is. The width is sized to the upper bound of label widths in Geist Sans: longest date line "Wed, Sep 30" ≈ 81 px, longest abbreviated relative-day "23mo away" ≈ 50 px. If labels ever exceed 88 px, either widen the column or compact the labels further.
- **Relative-day labels use abbreviated units with no space between number and unit**: `Today`, `Tomorrow`, `5d away`, `2w away`, `3mo away`, `1y away`. Matches the `compactDurationSince` convention in `lib/questionListUtils.ts`. Bucket thresholds: <14 days → `Nd`, <8 weeks → `Nw`, <24 months → `Nmo`, else `Ny`.
- **The `+ button` is in its own flex column, `self-start` aligned**, so its center sits at the same Y as the topmost pill's center regardless of how many pills the day has. `shrink-0` keeps it a perfect 34 px circle even when delete controls appear in the slot column and pressure the row. Diameter 34 px = pill height (text-sm line + py-1.5 + 1 px border each side).
- **Delete control hides when only one slot remains.** Two-or-more-slot days show a red `×` icon (no trash can) to the left of each pill; one-slot days show no `×` so the day can never drop to zero windows. To remove the entire day, use the day picker — that's the only path.
- **`DEFAULT_TIME_WINDOW`** in `lib/timeUtils.ts` is the canonical "first window" — `{ min: '08:00', max: '17:00' }`. Imported by `useDayTimeWindowsState.ts`, `createPollHelpers.ts: emptyDraft`, and `app/create-poll/page.tsx`'s today-init path. Don't re-inline the times.
- **Slot add/remove animates its height (creator form ONLY).** Each time slot is wrapped in `AnimatedSlotRow` — a `grid-template-rows: 0fr ↔ 1fr` + opacity clip (300ms) — so adding a slot grows it in and removing one collapses it out, with everything below (Duration card, cutoffs) sliding to make room via normal document flow. The render forks `isVoterForm ? (existing instant ghost-row block) : (animated block)`; the voter form is deliberately NOT animated (its ghost-row + split-window semantics differ, and the feature was scoped to creation). Mechanics, all in `components/DayTimeWindowsInput.tsx`:
  - **Stable-id reconciler `reconcileRows(prev, windows, allocId)` is what makes EDITS not animate.** Time windows are plain `{min,max}` with no identity, and `useDayTimeWindowsState.onWindowsChange` re-sorts on every change, so naive value- or index-keys would make an edit read as remove+add. The reconciler assigns persistent ids: exact value-match first (unchanged slots keep their id), then positional pairing for in-place edits (id kept → no animation), then leftover prop windows become `'enter'` rows (inserted at sorted position) and unmatched existing rows become `'leave'` rows (kept in place to collapse where they sat). Returns the SAME array ref when nothing changed so the reconcile `useEffect([windows, isVoterForm])` can't loop. Deps are reference-stable per day (the hook only news the changed day's `windows` array), so unrelated parent re-renders (typing elsewhere) don't re-run it.
  - **Delete/add handlers are unchanged — animation is purely reactive to props.** `handleDeleteWindow` still calls `onChange(filter)` immediately; the reconciler then sees the row missing from `windows` and flips it to `'leave'`. The `AnimatedSlotRow` collapses and fires `onLeaveDone` on the `grid-template-rows` `transitionEnd`, which removes it from `animRows`. No delayed-onChange or imperative DOM collapse needed.
  - **`AnimatedSlotRow` `open` init is `phase !== 'enter'` (NOT `=== 'shown'`).** At mount phase is only ever `'enter'` or `'shown'` (leave rows are always derived from an already-mounted shown row), so the two are equivalent at mount — but the `setOpen(true)` fall-through after the enter/leave branches is **load-bearing, not dead code**: it handles leave→shown *revival* (a slot deleted then re-added/re-matched before its collapse completes) by reopening the still-mounted row. Don't "simplify" it away.
  - **`overflow-hidden` is applied only WHILE animating, not at rest (the `clip` state).** The grid-rows collapse needs `overflow: hidden` to mask the height transition, but leaving it on at rest CLIPPED the pill's **selection ring** (`ring-2 ring-blue-500` in `renderWindowButton`, drawn 2px OUTSIDE the pill box) at top/bottom — the reported "blue outline cut off, too big for container" bug (creator form only; the voter form has no `AnimatedSlotRow`, so its outset ring always showed). Fix: a `clip` boolean (init `phase !== 'shown'`) set true on enter/leave, flipped false on the `grid-template-rows` `transitionEnd` when settled `open` (and false immediately in the `'shown'` fall-through); BOTH the outer grid div and inner `min-h-0` div toggle `overflow: clip ? 'hidden' : 'visible'` in lockstep (mirrors the original dual `overflow-hidden`). `clip` is NOT derivable from `open` — it must stay true through the enter-open transition and only release on transitionEnd, so the outset ring is masked during the height animation but paints fully at rest. **Don't switch the ring to `ring-inset` instead** — outset rings hugging the edge are the app-wide selection-bubble convention (TimeSlotBubbles / ShowtimeBubbles / RankableOptions); fixing the wrapper to not clip settled content is the right altitude (any outset decoration, not just this ring, would otherwise clip).
  - **Rows absent from `windows` render a neutral non-interactive `<span>` (no trash, `aria-hidden`).** Covers both leaving rows and the one-frame gap between a `windows` change and the post-render reconcile effect. `windows.indexOf(row.window) === -1` is the "not present" signal; gate trash + edit + validation flags on `isPresent`.
  - **Inter-row gap is `pt-2` skipped on the first row (`idx > 0`), INSIDE the clip.** It must live inside the `AnimatedSlotRow` clip so it collapses with the row (no 8px gap snap on enter/leave). It must be `pt-2`-not-first (NOT `pb-2`-on-all, the first shipped form) or the last slot carries 8px of trailing space against the day's hairline divider — the reported regression. So: spacing only ever appears *between* visible slots, none leading or trailing.
- **`allDays` prop on `DayTimeWindowsInput`** is the full day list across the form, used only by the `+ button`'s smart-pick. Optional with a sensible fallback (`[{day, windows}]`) so the component still works standalone if a caller forgets to pass it; the smart-pick just degenerates to the default-window branch in that case.

### Service Worker Caching Strategy

- **Never use `url.pathname.startsWith('/')` in service worker URL matching** — it matches ALL paths. Use exact equality (`===`) or more specific prefixes like `/create-question`.
- **Use network-first for HTML navigation, cache-first only for immutable assets.** Cache-first for navigation causes the PWA to serve stale HTML that references old JS bundles (also cached), making it impossible for users to get new code. Network-first ensures fresh HTML on every load; cache is only a fallback for offline.
- **Skip API requests in the service worker** — let them go directly to the network. Caching API responses causes stale question data with no visible error.
- **Bump `CACHE_NAME` version when changing caching strategy** to force old caches to be deleted on activation. Without this, users keep stale cached content indefinitely.
- **JS chunks need network-first too** — even with content-hash filenames, the old manifest chunk references old chunk names. After a new build, the manifest is cached with old chunk references; network-first for `/_next/static/chunks/` ensures the manifest is always fresh.

### iOS PWA Safe Area Positioning

- **`position: fixed; top: 0` goes behind the notch** in iOS PWA with `viewport-fit: cover` and `black-translucent` status bar. Either push content down via `padding-top: env(safe-area-inset-top)` on the fixed element (so its background fills the notch zone), or anchor the element at `top: env(safe-area-inset-top)` (so it sits below the notch). The group header uses the first pattern; the commit badge uses the second via `.pwa-badge-top`.
- **Body gets horizontal safe-area padding** (`padding-left/right: env(safe-area-inset-left/right)`); vertical safe-area insets are handled per-element by whatever sits at the top/bottom (fixed group header, home/settings titles via `.page-title-safe-top`, the new group button via its flat `bottom` offset (`1.9rem` web / `2.65rem` Capacitor native) — see "iOS PWA layout viewport vs physical screen" below for why we don't add `env(safe-area-inset-bottom)` here).
- **Use CSS media queries, not JS state, for PWA safe-area layout.** React state (`isStandalone`) starts `false` and only updates after `useEffect`, causing a visible jump on first render. `@media (display-mode: standalone)` applies instantly before any JS runs. Reserve `isStandalone` state for conditional rendering (e.g., back button visibility) where a one-frame flash is acceptable.
- **To position at the true screen edge**, render via a portal to `document.body` (outside the `.responsive-scaling-container`). From there, `fixed top: 0` = the safe area boundary (notch bottom) in PWA standalone mode.
- **Fixed header bars need to cover the notch zone, not just sit below it.** A header anchored at `top: env(safe-area-inset-top)` leaves the area above it (the notch zone) uncovered, showing scrolling content through it. Instead, anchor the bar at `top: 0` and push its content down with `padding-top: env(safe-area-inset-top, 0px)` so the background fills from the physical screen top. **The measurement ref (for computing a sibling's `padding-top`) must be on the OUTER fixed div, not the inner content div** — `offsetHeight` includes the element's own padding, so measuring the outer div picks up `env(safe-area-inset-top)` automatically (in iOS PWA where it's ~47-59px notch; in browser/desktop where env resolves to 0, the outer and inner heights match). An earlier iteration kept the ref on the inner content div with the rationale "stays content-only", but the consumer's `paddingTop: ${headerHeight}px + 0.5rem` then under-reserved by the safe-area amount and content sat behind the bottom of the header in iOS PWA. Most visible on the empty group placeholder (`/g/`) where a centered "Create a question…" caption was clipped by the header; on regular group cards the same bug just consumed the first ~47px of dead space and went unnoticed. Pattern used in `components/GroupHeader.tsx`.
- **iOS PWA layout viewport vs physical screen.** On iPhone X-class devices in PWA standalone mode (e.g., iOS 18.7 / Safari 26.4), `window.innerHeight` (= the layout viewport) can be smaller than `window.screen.height` (= the physical screen). One observed pairing: `innerH=812`, `screenH=874` — 62 logical points of physical screen sit BELOW the layout viewport. This is separate from `env(safe-area-inset-bottom)`, which reports the home-indicator zone WITHIN the layout viewport (typically 34px on iPhone X-class). Two consequences:
  - **Fixed-positioned DOM elements are clipped to the layout viewport boundary.** Anything at `top: ${innerH}px` or `bottom: -<n>px` simply doesn't render. Only `body`/`html` background paints into the strip below the layout viewport (verified by setting `--background: #ffff00` and observing the entire physical screen turn yellow). Therefore, the only knob on this strip is its bg color — you cannot put a button, text, or any DOM there.
  - **iOS PWA screenshots crop at the layout viewport bottom**, NOT the full physical screen. A `position: fixed; bottom: 0` element appears flush with the screenshot's bottom edge while sitting well above the device's actual screen edge. This burned multiple debugging cycles in the original session — markers that looked correct in screenshots were physically displaced on-device. When debugging iOS PWA layout from screenshots, mentally extend the screenshot's bottom edge by `screen.height - innerHeight` to get the true physical bottom. Or just trust the readout values, not the eye.
  - **The new group button sits at a flat `bottom` offset (`1.9rem` web / `2.65rem` Capacitor native, NOT `max(..., env(safe-area-inset-bottom))`)** because adding the env value pushes it another `~34px` above the layout viewport bottom (which already sits well above the physical screen edge), producing a visibly far-from-edge button on iPhone PWA. The flat value keeps it a fixed distance above the layout viewport bottom; the home-indicator gesture pill is centered horizontally at the bottom and doesn't reach the new group button's right-edge position, so iOS's reserved-zone overlap there is fine. The 62px physical strip below remains body-bg-painted "wasted" space that we currently leave matching the page background. The offset (`components/CreateGroupButtonHost.tsx`) is the original baseline (`1rem` / `1.75rem`) raised by `0.9rem` = 30% of the 48px (`h-12`) button height — a 20%-of-`1rem` bump was tried first but ~3px is imperceptible, so the lift was re-anchored to button-height.

### Navigation Layout

- **No bottom bar. No home button.** The old three-button bottom bar (Home / + / Profile) was removed. Navigation is:
  - **New group button on home only**: a single rounded blue "+ Group" pill pinned bottom-right via `position: fixed` + `max(1.5rem, env(safe-area-inset-right, 0px))` / `max(1rem, env(safe-area-inset-bottom, 0px))`. Tapping it navigates to `/g/` (the empty placeholder), where the category bubble bar lets the user pick a category to start a new poll. Home does NOT show the bubble bar — choosing a category is a per-group decision, not a "starting fresh" decision. Sizing tuned empirically: `px-[16.56px]` horizontal padding (1.38× the original `px-3` to give the "+" + "Group" content room to breathe at the pill ends) and `text-[28.8px]` for the "+" glyph (0.8× the original `text-4xl` so the plus reads closer in visual weight to the `text-lg` "Group" label rather than dominating it). Half-pixel arbitrary Tailwind values are house style throughout the codebase (see other examples in `GroupListItem.tsx`, `DayTimeWindowsInput.tsx`); don't "tidy" them to the nearest standard size — they encode exact percentage-tuned design specs.
  - **Home empty-state is session-aware** (`app/page.tsx`, shown when `polls.length === 0 && emptyGroups.length === 0`): always renders "You don't have access to any groups"; signed-OUT users additionally get a blue "Sign In" button below it that opens the shared `<SignInModal>`. Session state seeds from `getCachedSessionUser()` after mount (null on first render for SSR/hydration parity) and tracks `SESSION_CHANGED_EVENT`, mirroring the `GroupNotFound` session-tracking pattern — so signing in via the modal swaps the button away without a navigation. Note: anonymous auto-minted accounts (the create-poll auto-account) issue no session, so they read as signed-out here and still see the Sign In button — intended.
  - **Category bubble bar on group-like pages**: portaled into `#draft-poll-portal`, which lives inside the floating `BubbleBarPanel` mounted once at the layout level by `BubbleBarHost` (`components/BubbleBarHost.tsx`) and shown on `/g/<id>` and the `/g/` empty placeholder — see the "BubbleBarHost + BubbleBarPanel (Floating Bubble Bar)" section above for the panel's structure, visibility rules, and the withheld-during-slide-then-slide-up behavior. The row starts with a bold "+ New Poll" button (catch-all opening the modal with the default `custom` category) followed by one button per `BUILT_IN_TYPES` entry — see `BUBBLE_ENTRIES` in `app/create-poll/page.tsx`. Each entry calls `openModalFor(category)` which seeds a fresh draft via `emptyDraft({category})` and opens the new-question modal. There is no separate draft poll card or always-on creation form. All buttons share the module-scope `BUBBLE_BUTTON_CLASS` constant; the retired trailing "Other" entry (also `custom`-category) was folded into "New Poll" to avoid two buttons doing the same thing. **Each bubble is a rounded rectangle (`rounded-2xl`), not a pill** — `BUBBLE_HEIGHT_PX = 112` (fixed across all bubbles for vertical uniformity), icon stacked above word-wrapped title. The icon is `BUBBLE_ICON_PX = 32` (2× the previous `text-base` glyph); the `+` on the New Poll bubble is an SVG cross sized to the same 32×32 footprint because a text `+` at the same `font-size` only occupies ~18px of its em-box and read visually smaller than the 40px-wide emoji glyphs on the neighbour bubbles. Title uses `-webkit-line-clamp: 3` for the cap and `wordBreak: normal` + `overflowWrap: normal` so words stay intact and wrap by spaces. **Width auto-sizes via `width: min-content`** floored at `BUBBLE_MIN_WIDTH_PX = round(32 × 1.75) = 56px` (2× icon would be 64, but 1.75× felt visually balanced and packs more bubbles into the viewport — "Restaurant" lands at ~90px because its single word doesn't fit 56). Vertical balance: the button uses `pt-3` only (no `pb-*`), so the title wrapper's `flex-1 + items-center` produces equal slack between (icon-bottom → title-top) and (title-bottom → rectangle-bottom); adding bottom padding would tilt that balance toward the bottom. Bg is the standard "off-page-bg" `bg-gray-50 dark:bg-gray-800` + a 1px `border-gray-200 dark:border-gray-700` outline so the bubbles read as quiet containers rather than primary affordances. The panel uses `bg-background` so it blends with the page bg and has no top divider — the rounded rectangles are sufficient visual separation. Earlier iterations: single-line pill chips with blue fill and inline icon+label (`rounded-full` + `px-[13.5px] py-[6.75px]`); before that an underlined sticky "New" label with bordered bubbles + a top border on the panel; before that a 3-row wrapped grid with a centered "Create a New Poll" h2 above. (Earlier iterations had a visible draft card hosting staged questions + a Submit button; that's gone — the modal now submits the entire poll directly. Even earlier iterations had a floating What/When/Where 3-bubble bar with `?mode=time` / `?category=restaurant` URL preselection; also gone — see the historical note in the Active Plan section above. An intermediate iteration kept the bar in-flow at the bottom of the cards-wrapper; the current floating panel replaces that with hide-on-scroll-down behavior.)
