- **Block autocomplete search for location-like categories until a reference location is set.** Proximity-bounded searches are useless without a reference point — Nominatim returns geographically random hits. `OptionsInput` computes `needsReferenceLocation = isLocationLikeCategory(category) && (refLat === undefined || refLng === undefined)` and shows an orange warning above the options while passing `searchDisabled={true}` to `AutocompleteInput`. `AutocompleteInput`'s `searchDisabled` prop is the single gate: early-return in `handleChange` (skip debounce/doSearch), guarded `setSuggestions` / `setShowSuggestions` in a `useEffect(() => ..., [searchDisabled])` that clears any previously cached results, and a check in `onFocus` so a cached list can't resurface.
  - **Suppress the warning in voter-facing contexts via `hideReferenceLocationWarning`.** `OptionsInput` is reused inside `SuggestionVotingInterface` for voter suggestion entry; if the creator never set a reference location, the "Choose a reference location above to enable search" message is un-actionable for voters (no field "above" — only the creator could have set it). The `SuggestionVotingInterface` callsite passes `hideReferenceLocationWarning` to suppress just the message; `searchDisabled` is still derived from `needsReferenceLocation` so Nominatim still gets blocked and voters can still type free-form options. The prop is presentation-only and orthogonal to `searchDisabled` — don't conflate them. If `OptionsInput` ever lands in a third context where the caller can't fix the missing reference location, pass `hideReferenceLocationWarning` rather than inventing an `isVotingContext`-style flag — visible-effect naming (`hideLoser`, `hideRespondents`) travels better than context naming and matches existing prop conventions in the codebase.
- **Document `mousedown` (outside-click) dismissal does NOT cover iOS keyboard accessory buttons.** `AutocompleteInput`'s dropdown was originally closed via a document-level `mousedown` listener + `Escape` keydown. iOS keyboard's Done checkmark and the form-navigation up/down arrows blur the input but don't fire any document-level pointer event or keydown that JS can observe — so the dropdown stayed open after focus moved away. Always pair outside-click dismissal with an `onBlur` close on the input itself. Tapping a dropdown item still works because each `<li>` uses `onMouseDown` with `e.preventDefault()` (prevents the input from blurring before `selectSuggestion` runs). Same gotcha applies to any other popup/menu/tooltip anchored to an input — outside-click alone is insufficient on iOS.

### Create-Poll: Form Fields & Helpers

- **`CreateQuestionContent` is exported** from `app/create-poll/page.tsx` and lazy-loaded via `React.lazy` in `template.tsx`. The `/create-poll` URL is a redirect stub that bounces to `/g/` (where the bubble bar lives).
- **`draftPollPreview(drafts, pollContext)` mirrors `server/algorithms/poll_title.generate_poll_title`** so the placeholder card's title matches what the server picks on submit. The duplication is intentional and called out in a header comment; both sides have to evolve together when adding a new category to `BUILT_IN_TYPES`. The auto-title rules (matched on both sides):
  - **1 question**: title equals the question's own auto-title (category + its `forField` as "for X" suffix). E.g. `[Restaurant + forField=Tonight]` → "Restaurant for Tonight". Yes_no questions whose title was user-typed (`!isAutoTitle`) keep the typed title.
  - **N questions sharing the SAME context** (poll-level `context` OR every question's `forField` matches case-insensitively): comma-join the categories + " for X". E.g. `[Restaurant, Movie, all forField=Tonight]` → "Restaurant, Movie for Tonight".
  - **N questions, distinct per-question contexts**: greedy "Cat for Ctx" pairs joined with commas, ending in ", etc." once the next pair would overflow. Falls back to "Questions" if not even one pair fits.
  - **N questions, no context anywhere**: comma-join the category labels (no "for X" suffix, no "and").
  - **Length cap**: `_TITLE_CHAR_LIMIT = 40` on the server, `TITLE_LIMIT = 40` on the FE (the same constant `buildOrList` already uses for question-level "or"-list titles). When the comma-joined "Cat1, Cat2 for X" form exceeds the cap, fall back to "Questions for X". Tune both constants in lockstep if mobile rendering changes.
  - **Per-question contexts source on the server**: `questions.details` (mapped via the `_contexts_for_title()` helper in `routers/polls.py`). Don't confuse with `polls.details` (the multi-line Notes field).
- **The FE is the single source of title truth for the create-poll flow — it SENDS its computed title, never `null` (the server's `generate_poll_title` is bypassed for web creates).** Earlier the FE sent `title: null` for auto-titled polls and let the server regenerate; that diverged from what the user previewed/tapped. Concrete bug it fixed: a custom-category suggestion poll (`{category:'custom', forField:'Bog'}`, no options) previewed + suggested as **"Options for Bog?"** but posted as **"Ranked Choice for Bog"** — because `_categories_for_title` (`routers/polls.py`) falls back to `sp.question_type.value` ("ranked_choice") for a category-less question, and `generate_poll_title`'s single-question path has no "Options for X" special case (the FE does). Three coupled pieces enforce the guarantee, all using the SAME generator (`draftTitleSegments`):
  - **One FE generator.** `generateTitle` (the live form preview in `app/create-poll/page.tsx`) delegates to `deriveDraftTitle(...)` (= `draftTitleSegments(...).map(s=>s.text).join('')`) instead of re-implementing its own title-building — so the preview can't drift from the suggestion rows (which also go through `draftTitleSegments` via `overridesToSegments`). The lone exception is a blank-custom draft (no category/options/context) → `generateTitle` returns `''` so the form shows the placeholder HINT rather than the generic "Suggestions" segment text.
  - **`deriveDraftTitle(d: TitleDraft)`** takes the narrow 6-field `TitleDraft` (widened from `QuestionDraft`), so callers that only have form fields (not a full draft) can call it directly.
  - **Submit sends the previewed title.** `handleSubmitClick`'s `wrapperTitle` is ALWAYS non-null now: single-question auto → `onlyDraft.title.trim() || deriveDraftTitle(onlyDraft)` (the `||` covers the blank-custom case where the preview was the hint); single-question typed (yes_no/limited_supply) → the typed title (yes_no gets `yesNoTitleText`); multi-question → `draftPollPreview(effectiveDrafts, '').title`. Per-arity sources mirror the optimistic placeholder (`synthesizePlaceholderPoll`), so no title-morph on hydration.
  - **Verified end-to-end**: suggestion text = form preview = POST body `title` = displayed poll header, all `"Options for Bog?"`.
  - **Latent divergence still exists for RAW-API callers** that omit `req.title` — the server's `generate_poll_title` is still reachable and could drift from the FE's `draftPollPreview`/`draftTitleSegments` over future category-label edits. Acceptable scope (the web flow is fixed); if it matters later, either require `title` on every `POST /api/polls` or add a test pairing `draftPollPreview` outputs against `generate_poll_title` on shared inputs. (The `draftPollPreview` mirror bullet above stays the source of the MULTI-question wrapper title + the placeholder card.)
- **`sharedDraftContext(drafts)` exported from `createPollHelpers.ts`** returns the case-insensitive-shared `forField` across every staged draft, or null when one is missing or values diverge. Used by `openModalFor` in `app/create-poll/page.tsx` to pre-fill the new question form's `forField` when restoring multi-question staging — a 2nd "Restaurant for Tonight" added to a "Movie for Tonight" poll inherits "Tonight" without retyping. Don't double-apply: `emptyDraft({ forField: ... })` plumbs the value into a fresh draft only.
- **Poll-level Context vs Notes** are two separate fields. Context is a short single-line input that drives the auto-title's "for X" suffix (maps to `polls.context`); Notes is a multi-line textarea with link support (maps to `polls.details` — wrapper column added in migration 103, wired through `CreatePollRequest` / `PollResponse` / `_insert_poll` / `_row_to_poll`). Don't confuse with `questions.details` (per-question context, added by migration 068 to the OLD polls table that 097 renamed to `questions`) — the two `details` columns live on different tables and carry different meaning. **Notes never feed the autotitle.** The FE-only `Context` field doesn't exist yet, so submit always sends `context: null` and `draftPollPreview(drafts, '').title` mirrors that — passing the Notes textarea (`details` state) into `draftPollPreview` would make the preview show a "Cat for <notes-text>" title that the server would never produce, divorcing preview from reality. If a Context UI field is added later, it goes into the `pollContext` arg of `draftPollPreview`, not Notes. **Poll-level notes render in the expanded group card** via `<QuestionDetails details={wrapper.details} label="Notes: " />` at the top of the expand-clip wrapper in `app/g/[groupShortId]/GroupCardItem.tsx` — same component used for `question.details` (per-question context inside `QuestionBallot`, no `label` passed), so multi-line text + link rendering + truncation/expand chevron come for free. The optimistic placeholder (`synthesizePlaceholderPoll`) carries `details` through too so the user sees their typed Notes immediately on submit, before the API resolves.
- **Pitfall: wiring a column through API code without a migration causes silent prod-only drift.** Migration 068's `details` column lives on `questions` after the 097 rename — adding `polls.details` to `_insert_poll`, `_row_to_poll`, `CreatePollRequest`, and `PollResponse` without a fresh migration left every `POST /api/polls` 500ing on prod (`column "details" of relation "polls" does not exist`). Dev DBs created post-097 from scratch happen to ALSO be missing the column, but the failure manifested only on prod because dev wrappers were created against a checkout that pre-dated the wire-through. When adding a new column-shaped field to a wrapper / table, audit `\d <table>` on prod (or grep `database/migrations/*.sql` for an `ALTER TABLE <table> ADD COLUMN <field>` since the table was last renamed) before wiring it through. The fix here was migration 103.
- **No duplicate/follow-up confirmation card.** When the modal is opened with `?duplicate=<id>` or `?followUpTo=<id>`, the underlying form logic still wires the new question up correctly — but there's no visual "this is a follow-up to X" header card. If you need a "remove association" UX in the future, also re-introduce a way to reset the related state.
- **Category and Context are stacked standard form fields inside the top card, with a live auto-generated title preview rendered ABOVE the card (outside it).** The title preview uses bold `text-xl` text in `text-blue-600 dark:text-blue-400` with `fontFamily: "'M PLUS 1 Code', monospace"` (the project's title-style monospace blue font, also used by `components/AnimatedTitle.tsx` and `app/page.tsx`'s home title); empty-state placeholder `‹title›` keeps the slot visible before the user picks a category. Category field uses the shared `TypeFieldInput` dropdown (same `BUILT_IN_TYPES` + custom-text fallback). Context is a normal `<input>` with `FOR_FIELD_PLACEHOLDERS[category]` as the category-aware placeholder (Restaurant→"Dinner, Lunch, etc.", etc.), falling back to "Context" when no entry. The Context field is hidden when `category === 'yes_no'` (yes/no has no "for X" suffix; the title IS the prompt and renders via the existing `titleField` input below). An earlier iteration used a custom `CategoryForLine.tsx` component that put both fields on one auto-sizing line in the title's place — that's been deleted; the new layout is just two stacked label/input pairs that get the same job done without the binary-search font-fitting, mirror-sizer width tracking, or auto-completion-of-options-as-italic-placeholder machinery.
- **The empty-state title-preview hint (shown in the blue-title slot before any title is typed) is category-aware via `titlePreviewHint`.** `yes_no` and `limited_supply` forms expose ONLY a Title field (no Category/Context/Options rows), so they show `"Enter a title"`; every other category keeps `"Enter a Category, Context, and/or Options"` (accurate for custom + ranked-choice; `time`/ranked categories auto-title immediately so the empty hint is effectively unreachable for them). Keep the hint matched to the fields the current category actually renders — don't reference Context/Options for a title-only form. Separately, the `titleField` input placeholder is `"auto"` when `isAutoTitle`, `"What's available?"` for `limited_supply` (the title IS the item being handed out), else `"Enter your title..."`.
- **`ConfirmationModal` z-index must be above z-60** (the create-poll bottom sheet). Currently at `z-[70]`. Any new modal that needs to appear over the create flow must exceed z-60.

### Create-Poll Bottom Sheet — Current Architecture

The flow has gone through four iterations: docked-panel (top + bottom sheets), always-visible inline form inside the draft card, then a rounded-corner modal that staged drafts into a visible draft poll card, and now (current) a single bottom-sheet modal that submits the entire poll directly. There is no longer a separate "draft" stage in single-question mode — tapping the modal's check button submits the poll immediately.

- **Bubble bar at the bottom of the scroll** (floating fixed via `BubbleBarPanel`) is a single horizontally-scrollable row: a leading bold "+ New" button (catch-all `custom` category) followed by one button per `BUILT_IN_TYPES` entry. Tapping any bubble calls `openModalFor(category)` which seeds a fresh `emptyDraft({ category })` and opens the bottom-sheet. The bubble bar always renders on group-like pages; constants `BUBBLE_ENTRIES` and `BUBBLE_BUTTON_CLASS` live at module scope (don't recreate per render). Order matches the `TypeFieldInput` dropdown so muscle memory carries over.
- **Bottom sheet, not draft card.** The modal is a slide-up sheet anchored to the bottom edge with rounded top corners (`rounded-t-3xl` + `animate-slide-up`). The previous architecture rendered an inline draft poll card in `#draft-poll-portal` showing staged questions + a separate Submit button; that card is GONE in single-question mode. The portal still exists (the constant `DRAFT_POLL_PORTAL_ID` is still mounted by group routes) and hosts only the bubble bar. Don't reintroduce a visible draft card without first restoring the multi-question staging flow.
- **Sheet height is fixed (`height: calc(100dvh - 70px)`), not content-driven (`maxHeight`).** Using `maxHeight` made the panel size to its content, so a short form (e.g. yes_no with no options yet) only slid up partway — the slide-up animation ended at a visually arbitrary height that depended on how many fields were rendered. Fixed height pins the top edge to a consistent location regardless of form state; the inner `overflow-y-auto` already handles tall content, and short content just gets empty space below. Don't switch back to `maxHeight` to "tighten" the visual — the inconsistent top edge is more disorienting than the empty space. **The `70px` constant is the top-edge clearance below the iOS Dynamic Island**: the sheet is bottom-anchored (`flex items-end`), so its top edge sits at `100dvh - height` = a flat `70px` from the physical top. `100dvh` on iOS includes the area behind the notch (`viewport-fit=cover`), so an earlier flat `3rem` (48px) put the top edge right where the Dynamic Island / notch lives on iPhone X-class devices (`safe-area-inset-top` ≈ 47–59px) and the rounded top corners intersected it. A device-adaptive `- env(safe-area-inset-top)` term was tried (top edge = `3rem + inset` ≈ 107px on a 59px-inset device) but read as too much gap; the owner asked for a flat `70px`, which clears the ~59px island on every current iPhone with ~11px to spare. If a future device ships a larger top inset, bump this constant rather than reintroducing the env term.
- **Two stacked borderless cards inside the sheet** — top card holds the question form (category + options + per-question fields), bottom card holds poll-level settings (voting cutoff, prephase cutoff, notes, voter name). Each card is `rounded-3xl bg-white dark:bg-gray-800 px-4 py-4` with no border. The sheet itself uses `bg-gray-100 dark:bg-gray-900` so the inner cards read as raised panels on a slightly darker surface. The cards' `space-y-3` gap visually separates the two responsibilities.
- **The sheet body opens pinned to scroll-top (`setSheetScrollerRef` in `app/create-poll/page.tsx`).** On a real iOS device, picking a suggestion from the focused poll search box opens the sheet WHILE the soft keyboard is collapsing, and WebKit scrolls the freshly-mounted sheet scroller during that settle window — reported first as "the form opens with the Options card at the top" and later as "scrolled way past the bottom" (device-only; doesn't reproduce in headless Chromium OR headless WebKit, like the other keyboard-settle races). Fix: a callback ref on the `overflow-y-auto` sheet body zeroes `scrollTop` on attach, re-zeroes on each `scroll` event, AND re-asserts `scrollTop = 0` every frame via a `requestAnimationFrame` loop for `SHEET_SCROLL_PIN_MS` (800ms — outlasts keyboard collapse + the 300ms slide-up). **The per-frame rAF reassert is load-bearing on top of the `scroll`-event handler**: setting `scrollTop` on the single mid-animation `scroll` event iOS fires is sometimes IGNORED, so the rAF re-asserts the next frame; keep both (the `scroll` handler is the synchronous fast path, the rAF the insurance — don't "simplify" one away). The user's first `touchstart`/`wheel`/`pointerdown` disarms it immediately (cancels the rAF + timer) so a real scroll gesture is never fought. A callback ref, NOT an `isModalOpen`-keyed effect — the scroller mounts inside `<ModalPortal>`'s deferred commit, so the effect would fire while the node is still null (same pitfall as `setTitleInputRef`). Deliberately NOT hoisted into `ModalPortal` — auto-pinning every modal's scroller to top would fight `KeyboardSuggestionPicker`'s bottom-anchored scroll, and only this sheet opens from a focused-keyboard state; extract a shared `useScrollPin` hook if a 3rd modal needs it.
- **`resetFreshPollFields()` is the canonical "start a brand-new poll" reset for POLL-LEVEL fields (`app/create-poll/page.tsx`).** `applyDraftToState` resets only the per-question `QuestionDraft`; poll-level state (`details`/Notes, `recurrence`, `allowPlusOnes`) lives outside the draft and would otherwise leak across polls — a prior session's Notes restored by `loadFormState` showed up as "random text in the notes field", and a stale active `recurrence` would silently make the next poll repeat. `resetFreshPollFields()` (details `""` + recurrence `DEFAULT_RECURRENCE` + allowPlusOnes `null`) is called by `discardAndClose` (the explicit X-confirm reset) AND by `openModalWithDraft` (the search-box / bubble-bar fresh-poll entry) — **but in `openModalWithDraft` it's gated on `drafts.length === 0`**, because when staged drafts exist the user is ADDING a question to a multi-question poll and the poll-level config they already set must be preserved. It deliberately does NOT reset the carried-over "remembered" fields (voting/suggestion cutoffs, min votes, show-results-after-min, allow-pre-ranking) — those persist across polls by design. When adding a new poll-level field, decide: fresh-each-poll → add it to `resetFreshPollFields`; remembered → leave it out.
- **The check button submits the entire poll immediately.** Tapping the right-aligned check in the sheet header calls `handleSubmitClick` directly — no intermediate "draft" stage. On success the sheet closes (`setIsModalOpen(false)`) and the optimistic placeholder card is already in place on the destination group. On validation/API failure the sheet stays open with form values intact. Backdrop click + Escape dismiss without submitting.
- **Form state reset is on the success path ONLY.** A previous version called `applyDraftToState(emptyDraft())` BEFORE the poll-level validation step in `handleSubmitClick`. If validation failed (or the API rejected), the user saw an error in a now-empty modal and lost everything. The reset must run after `apiCreatePoll` resolves successfully, never before. Same applies to the duplicate-redirect branch (see next bullet) — clear state inline before `router.replace`. **Pitfall**: with the visible draft card gone, there is no fallback UI for staged drafts — once the form is wiped without a successful submit, the user can't recover their typing. Never insert a "reset early to keep the JSX simpler" change here.
- **Duplicate-redirect rule: same-creator + 30s window only.** `apiFindDuplicateQuestion` exists to catch accidental double-submits, NOT to forbid duplicate titles in general. The redirect only fires when both: (a) the current browser holds a `creator_secret` for the existing question (i.e. it's the creator), and (b) the existing question was created less than `DUPLICATE_REDIRECT_WINDOW_MS = 30_000` ago. Otherwise the create-poll flow proceeds and a real duplicate is created. The earlier "any matching title in the group → redirect" rule blocked legitimate duplicates (e.g. a fresh `Movie?` suggestion round in the same group). The `getCreatorSecret(existing.id)` check in `app/create-poll/page.tsx` is the canonical way to ask "am I the creator?" — there's no persistent user identity across polls, so it's the only signal available.
- **The duplicate-redirect branch must tear down submit state BEFORE `router.replace`.** Without it, `isLoading` stays true, the modal stays open, and the spinning check button covers the destination group forever. Fix is: `isSubmittingRef.current = false; setIsLoading(false); setIsModalOpen(false); applyDraftToState(emptyDraft()); setError(null);` before the redirect call. Symptom of regression: tapping the same built-in suggestion-mode bubble (e.g. Movie) twice in the same group within the 30s window hangs the submit forever — the auto-title `Movie?` matches and the duplicate-redirect fires but never closes the modal.
- **Auto-open the modal when URL params (`?duplicate=`, `?voteFromSuggestion=`) prefill the form.** Without this the prefill is invisible because the modal opens via explicit user action only. One-line `setIsModalOpen(true)` after the prefill effects.
- **Per-category defaults live in `emptyDraft`, not `openModalFor`.** `emptyDraft({ category })` initializes:
  - `dayTimeWindows: [{ today, [] }]` when category is `'time'` (or legacy `mode: 'time'`)
  - `isAutoTitle: false` when category is `'yes_no'` (the title IS the prompt)
  - everything else default
  Don't add per-category branches to `openModalFor` — the helper is a single `applyDraftToState(emptyDraft({category}))` call. Other entry points (auto-stage on Submit, post-submit reset) call `emptyDraft()` with no opts and get the same defaults.
- **Time category uses `questionType: 'question'` (not `'time'`).** All bubbles, including Time, set `questionType='question'` so the standard Category + Context fields render. The `questionFormBody` branches on `category === 'time'` to render `TimeQuestionFields`. **The legacy `questionType='time'` WRITERS are gone** (June 2026): the `?duplicate=` handler's time branch now sets the canonical `questionType='question'` + `category='time'` pair (the old `setQuestionType('time')` hid the entire top card — Category/Context/availability-toggle rows gate on `questionType === 'question'`; this was the "copied a time poll, no category/context card" bug), and `loadFormState` converts a legacy saved `questionType='time'` into the same pair on restore. The `questionType === 'time'` READS (`showTimeFields`, `draftDbQuestionType`, etc.) stay as defensive back-compat for stale localStorage drafts — don't write the value anywhere new. The handler's category restore is a SINGLE late-site writer (`restoredCategory = question_type === 'time' ? 'time' : duplicateData.category`) because the snapshot stores `category='custom'` for time polls — don't reintroduce a per-branch `setCategory` + skip-guard pair. Related: `duplicateData.details` is the PER-QUESTION context (`questions.details`) — the handler restores it into `forField` (Context field) for context-bearing types so the copy auto-titles identically; the old `setDetails(...)` mis-mapped it into Notes (`polls.details`, never snapshotted). yes_no/limited_supply drop it (details = the typed prompt/item name, intentionally not copied, like the title — the shared `detailsIsTypedPrompt` predicate in `createPollHelpers.ts` encodes that type set for both this restore and `draftToQuestionParams`'s title→context forward mapping). TODO (pre-existing, inline in the handler's else branch): limited_supply duplicates lose their type entirely — snapshot has `category='custom'` and no `supply_count`/`reveal_claimant_names`.
- **Body-scroll-lock is `lib/useBodyScrollLock.ts: useBodyScrollLock(locked, { overscroll? })`** — the shared hook for the `position: fixed` + saved-scrollY pattern (`overflow: hidden` alone doesn't block iOS PTR). It saves/restores the prior inline-style values (so nested locks compose) and optionally pins `overscroll-behavior: none` on `<body>`+`<html>` (default true). Used by `TimeGridModal`, `DaysSelector`, `ImageCropModal`, and the create-poll bottom sheet; each keeps its own modal-specific touchmove/Escape handler as a separate effect. **`RankableOptions` is the deliberate exception** — its lock is a drag-gesture lock entangled with the pointer-event lifecycle and uses different styles (`touchAction`/`overflow`/`left`/`right`), so it stays inline rather than forcing extra options onto the hook. When adding a new modal, call this hook; don't re-inline the `position:fixed; top:-scrollY` effect.
- **MutationObserver for the `#draft-poll-portal` target stays armed for the component's lifetime, not self-disconnecting.** The portal target is rendered inside `GroupContent`'s main return — but `GroupContent` has loading + error early-returns that don't render it. When the page transitions through one of those states (or after a route change to a fresh group), a self-disconnecting observer keeps a stale reference to a detached node and the bubble bar portals into invisibility. Always-armed + rAF coalescing keeps the cost negligible.
- **DOM markers are named constants, not magic strings.** `lib/groupDomMarkers.ts` exports `GROUP_LATEST_QUESTION_ID_ATTR`, `GROUP_ID_ATTR`, and `DRAFT_POLL_PORTAL_ID`. The group page (writer) and create-poll (reader) both import them. Hardcoded strings on either side would silently break the integration without the constant.
- **Implicit follow-up via `<body data-group-id>`.** The submit handler reads the body attribute directly to determine the group to attach the new poll to. Skipped when on `/g/` (empty placeholder) — by construction the user is starting a new group, and the body attribute can be stale (the group route's cleanup is a useEffect return that React/HMR/view-transitions can delay). `EmptyPlaceholder` also clears the attribute on mount as belt-and-braces. The on-empty-group regex `^\/t\/?$` is computed once at the top of `handleSubmitClick` — used both for the body-attribute gate and the post-success `router.replace` decision.
- **No DOM-mutation form-disable.** The submit handler used to imperatively walk `document.querySelector('form').querySelectorAll(...)` and set `.disabled = true`. Two failure modes: it only matched the FIRST form on the page (potentially the wrong one), and the captured form ref went stale across `router.replace` so `reEnableForm` operated on a detached tree — manifesting as "submit button unresponsive after first submit". Every input/select/button already takes `disabled={isLoading}` from React state; that's the single source of truth.
- **Empty-group submits stay on `/g/` until the API resolves.** Eagerly redirecting to `/g/<placeholderPoll.id>/` (the previous flow) loses, because `pending-...` ids don't resolve as UUIDs or short_ids — the destination renders "Group Not Found" and that view doesn't include `#draft-poll-portal`, so a subsequent API failure has no UI to surface the error. Stay on `/g/`; success branch redirects to the real short_id, failure branch leaves the user in place with the modal open + form intact.
- **Same-kind / distinct-context pre-validation.** Mirrors `server/routers/polls.py: _validate_request` client-side: groups drafts by `(question_type, category)` and rejects when group contexts collide (case-insensitive, empties collide). Surfaces inline in the modal's red error box. Currently dormant for single-question polls (one draft can't conflict with itself) but kept around for the multi-question staging flow that will return.
- **Inline-form contributions to poll-level predicates must gate on `isModalOpen`.** `pollHasPrephase` / `pollHasRankedChoice` are the union of "any staged draft matches" + "the in-progress inline form matches". After dismiss, `applyDraftToState(emptyDraft())` resets the inline form to defaults — `questionType='question'`, `category='custom'`, no options — which incidentally satisfy `isSuggestionMode` and `inlineFormIsRankedChoice`. Without an `isModalOpen` gate, those defaults wrongly light up the prephase predicates after every dismiss, surfacing "Suggestion/Availability Cutoff" + "allow voting before options are finalized" in Settings even when no question has a prephase. Don't try to fix this by tweaking `emptyDraft()`'s defaults (they're load-bearing for the open-modal first-paint UX) and don't reach for `inlineFormHasContent()` — that helper checks "has the user typed anything?" (used for auto-stage on Submit), which is a stricter gate than needed. The right gate is exactly "is the user editing right now?" which is `isModalOpen`. Same fix applies to any future poll-level predicate that mixes staged-draft state with inline-form state.
- **The Suggestion/Availability cutoff label is unified, not type-conditional.** Earlier the cutoff field flipped between "Suggestions Cutoff" (any non-time prephase) and "Availability Cutoff" (any time-question draft). The label is now fixed as "Suggestion/Availability Cutoff" because (a) a multi-question poll can have BOTH a suggestion-mode question and a time question sharing the same cutoff, and (b) the toggle's gate is `pollHasPrephase` regardless of which kind. Don't reintroduce a conditional label — the unified form is correct for every combination.
- **Modal close has two paths with different state semantics.** Header is upper-left **X button** + centered title + upper-right blue checkmark (submit). Backdrop-click and Escape route through `closeKeepState()` — close-without-reset, so the React form state + the `questionFormState` localStorage auto-save are both preserved (subject to `openModalFor` reseeding the form on the next bubble tap). The X button routes through `handleCloseClick`: when `inlineFormHasContent() || drafts.length > 0`, it opens a destructive-red `ConfirmationModal` whose confirm calls `discardAndClose` (resets form + clears drafts); when there's nothing to lose, it calls `closeKeepState` directly. The submit-success + duplicate-redirect callsites still inline the same reset operations as `discardAndClose` (not refactored to call it because they need to slot specific ordering with `router.replace` etc.).
- **Stacked-modal Escape double-fire pitfall.** When the inner `ConfirmationModal` is open, BOTH listeners are registered on `document` — `ConfirmationModal`'s own keydown handler AND the sheet's body-lock effect's keydown handler. A single Escape press fires both, dismissing both modals if the outer doesn't gate itself. Fix: the sheet's `handleEsc` reads `showDiscardConfirmRef.current` and bails when the inner modal is open. **Use a ref (not direct state in the effect's deps)** so toggling the confirm doesn't tear down + rebuild the body `position: fixed` lock on every open/close — the cleanup runs `window.scrollTo(0, scrollY)` and on iOS the brief unlock between cleanup and re-effect could let PTR engage. The pattern: `const showDiscardConfirmRef = useRef(showDiscardConfirm); useEffect(() => { ref.current = showDiscardConfirm; }, [showDiscardConfirm]);` then read `ref.current` inside the listener. Apply this any time you stack a modal over the create-poll sheet (or any other modal with its own document-level keydown).
- **`ConfirmationModal` renders message + a single confirm button only.** No title, no cancel button — `title` and `cancelText` props are accepted but unused (kept for callsite compatibility, both optional). Cancel is via backdrop click or Escape (the component installs its own document-level keydown listener for Escape → `onCancel`). When stacking it over another modal with a document keydown listener, gate the outer listener — see the stacked-modal Escape pitfall above.
- **Multi-question staging is currently dormant.** `drafts` state and the auto-stage path in `handleSubmitClick` are kept around so the multi-question flow can plug back in cleanly. Today they only ever hold a single auto-staged draft transiently mid-submit. The `editingDraftIndex` state was removed entirely (it was truly dead — nothing wrote it after the staged-draft list UI came out). When restoring multi-question staging, that's the state to add back along with a list UI somewhere — the previous draft poll card is gone.

### Submit Morph (Optimistic Placeholder + Fade-In)

Submitting a draft used to navigate to `/p/<newPollShortId>`. The user described the route change as a "page reload" feeling, so for a while the flow stayed on the current page and the placeholder fade-in was the only feedback. **As of the auto-navigate change, submitting ALWAYS lands on the new poll's detail page** (`getGroupHrefForPoll(createdPoll)` = `/g/<group>/p/<pollShort>`): `router.replace` from the empty `/g/` placeholder (so it doesn't linger in history), `router.push` from an existing group (so the group page stays the back target — the poll detail's back button uses `slideToGroupRoot` which `router.back()`s when `hasAppHistory()`). The optimistic placeholder + hydration flow below is STILL used (not for the user to watch the card appear — they navigate away — but to warm `accessiblePollsCache` + the per-poll cache so the poll detail page mounts instantly, and to keep the group list correct on back-nav). Both navigation branches run after the `POLL_HYDRATED_EVENT` dispatch + `cachePoll(createdPoll)`, so the destination cache is hot. The placeholder mounts in its sorted slot and fades in via a CSS keyframe (visible only in the brief window before navigation, and on the group list when the user returns).

- **`POLL_PENDING_EVENT` + `POLL_HYDRATED_EVENT`** in `lib/eventChannels.ts` are the cross-component event channels. On Submit, `CreateQuestionContent` (a) synthesizes an optimistic placeholder `Poll` from the draft state via `synthesizePlaceholderPoll` (id prefixed `pending-`), (b) caches it, (c) dispatches `POLL_PENDING_EVENT` with `{ poll }`, (d) clears draft state, and (e) starts `apiCreatePoll` in parallel. On API success, `POLL_HYDRATED_EVENT` swaps the placeholder fields for the real Poll. (The event's old `fromBbox` field — captured for the retired FLIP morph — was dropped along with the `data-draft-poll-card` attribute once nothing consumed it.)
- **GroupContent's POLL_PENDING listener** rebuilds the group state to include the placeholder. The placeholder card mounts with `pendingPollFirstQuestionId === question.id` so the status row / voter circles / countdown are suppressed until hydration — only the title shows. The cardFrame's className picks up `card-pending-enter` (defined in `app/globals.css`) which fades it in from `translateY(8px)/opacity:0` to natural over 300ms.
- **Why CSS keyframe instead of FLIP.** Earlier the morph was a JS FLIP that animated the cardFrame's width/height from the draft card's captured bbox to its natural slot. The cardFrame is a CSS Grid item, and Grid items default to `min-height: auto`/`min-width: auto`, both of which resolve to **min-content** — that clamps the cardFrame to its intrinsic content size and prevents the height transition from interpolating below it. Even after pinning `min-height: 0` and switching to a double-rAF dance + ease-out, the morph was inconsistent: `getComputedStyle().height` reported the from-value for ~80% of the animation duration, with an abrupt snap to the final value near the end. The CSS keyframe is fully declarative, no min-height edge case, and reads as "the new card just appeared".
- **`rebuildGroupFromCacheOrPrev(prev, mutate?)` is the shared rebuild for POLL_PENDING / POLL_HYDRATED / POLL_FAILED.** Source-of-truth merge: `byId = prev.polls + cached + (mutate.add) - (mutate.remove)`. **Always merging `prev.polls`** is the resilience fallback for stale `accessiblePollsCache` — the cache has a 60s TTL, and the submit handler's `cacheAccessiblePolls([...getCached() ?? [], newPoll])` pattern wipes every other poll out of the cache when the cache happens to be stale (idle >60s). Without `prev.polls` in the merge, `buildGroupFromPollDown(rootPollId, ...)` can't find rootPollId and bails, leaving the group frozen. Earlier the success path was an in-place `prev.polls.map(p => p.id === placeholderId ? realPoll : p)`, with a fallback for "POLL_PENDING bailed"; the hand-rolled swap was fragile across browsers (Firefox iOS hit a listener-registration race that left the swap a no-op). Both POLL_HYDRATED and POLL_FAILED route through this rebuild now and return `prev` when the rebuilt poll-id sequence matches (so identity-based memos stay stable). The submit handler ALSO uses `updateAccessiblePollsIfFresh(merge)` (`lib/questionCache.ts`) instead of the raw `cacheAccessiblePolls(...getCached() ?? [])` pattern — the helper's null-guard prevents wiping the cache to just `[newPoll]` when the cache was stale.
- **`mutate.add` / `mutate.remove` are required so swap is explicit.** Without them, leaving the placeholder AND the real poll both in the rebuild source yields a group containing both as children of the same parent. POLL_PENDING passes `{ add: placeholder }`, POLL_HYDRATED passes `{ add: realPoll, remove: placeholderId }`, POLL_FAILED passes `{ remove: placeholderId }`.
- **Anchor selection in the rebuild MUST validate `prev.rootPollId` against the post-mutate polls.** The expression is `prev.rootPollId && polls.some(p => p.id === prev.rootPollId) ? prev.rootPollId : polls[0].id`. The naive `prev.rootPollId ?? polls[0].id` form ships the empty→first-poll path correctly (rootPollId is null then) but breaks the placeholder-swap path on a 1-poll group: after POLL_PENDING, `prev.rootPollId = placeholder.id`; POLL_HYDRATED passes `mutate.remove = placeholderId`; the post-mutate `polls` is `[realPoll]`; the `??` falls through to the truthy-but-stale placeholder id; `buildGroupFromPollDown` returns null; the rebuild bails to prev — leaving the placeholder in `group.polls`. Meanwhile `mountedGroupKeys` advances to `realPoll.id`, so the slot keyed on the placeholder id renders as the virtualization gray spacer div. Symptom: "first poll in a new group → a space appears but the poll doesn't appear until I refresh." Same trap applies to any future mutate where the removed id IS the prior anchor.
- **`isPendingPollId(id)` (`lib/groupUtils.ts`) is the canonical check** for placeholder polls. Used by the group page's `maybeFetch` and POLL_FAILED-state guard, and by `GroupList`'s viewport prefetch — all to skip API calls on `pending-...` ids that 500 server-side (`psycopg.errors.InvalidTextRepresentation` because they're not valid UUIDs). Don't inline `id.startsWith('pending-')`.
- **POLL_PENDING / POLL_HYDRATED also setMountedGroupKeys directly.** GroupCardItem rendering is virtualized: groups not in `mountedGroupKeys` render as a fixed-height gray placeholder div, with progressive-fill walking outward from the URL anchor in idle-time batches of 4. When a brand-new poll lands, the validation effect (`mountedGroupKeys = prev ∩ validKeys + anchor`) doesn't add the new key — it was never in `prev` and isn't the URL anchor — so the new card renders as a gray div until progressive-fill reaches it. On a 50+ poll group anchored mid-group, that's ~17 batches (≈270ms) for a chronologically-last new poll. Fix: each handler does its own `setMountedGroupKeys((prev) => { if (no-change) return prev; const next = new Set(prev); next.add/delete; return next; })` alongside the setGroup, mounting the new card on the same render tick. The validation effect's intersection preserves these keys on subsequent runs since they end up in `validKeys` once the rebuild lands them in group state. Symptom of forgetting this: card animates in via POLL_PENDING (it happened to be in the queue from prior progressive-fill), then disappears into a gray div on POLL_HYDRATED.
- **POLL_HYDRATED async-refresh fallback for missing-ancestor case.** When the new poll's parent isn't in `prev.polls` (e.g. the parent was discovered AFTER group state was built — accessiblePollsCache was invalidated by discovery, so the cache fallback can't fill it in either), the in-place rebuild leaves the new poll out of the chain. After the optimistic setGroup, async `await getAccessiblePolls()` (which fetches based on localStorage's full accessibleIds, including newly-discovered ancestors) and re-runs the rebuild. **Skip the async refresh when the optimistic already added the realPoll** (`optimisticWillAdd = hasPlaceholder || isFollowUp || isOwnRoot`, computed from `groupRef.current` BEFORE setGroup because setState is async). On the happy path this saves a cache-fetch + redundant setState per submit. The async path remains the only fix for the missing-ancestor case where the optimistic bails to prev.
- **Submit handler always caches the real poll (or evicts the placeholder on failure) before dispatching, so the cache is the source of truth.** POLL_FAILED additionally early-returns when no `pending-...` poll is in group state — the failure typically fires while the user is on a different group.
- **Home page listens to POLL_HYDRATED only.** Newly-created polls auto-appear in the home list without refresh. POLL_FAILED is intentionally not listened to (placeholder polls never reach the home cache, so failure can't change the home list). The handler diffs poll-id sequences before calling `setPolls` — `getAccessiblePolls()` returns a new array on every call, so a content-equality check prevents re-rendering every `GroupList` row when nothing changed.
- **Per-question titles match the combined poll title.** The server gives every question of a multi-question poll the SAME title (e.g., "Movie and Video Game"), so `synthesizePlaceholderPoll` does the same — every placeholder question's title = the combined `draftPollPreview(drafts, '').title`. Earlier the placeholder used `deriveDraftTitle(d)` per-question, producing single-question titles like "Movie?" that morphed into "Movie and Video Game" on hydration. Server `_CATEGORY_LABELS` (`server/algorithms/poll_title.py`) now uses "Place" for `location` (matches the FE dropdown label in `TypeFieldInput.tsx`); the previous "Location" label caused a flicker on hydration.
- **Server `_CATEGORY_LABELS` keys must match `BUILT_IN_TYPES` `value` exactly.** The FE's `components/TypeFieldInput.tsx: BUILT_IN_TYPES` defines the wire-format value for each category (e.g. `value: "video_game"` with underscore). The server map keys must include that exact form, or the lookup misses and the fallback `_label_for(category)` runs — which only gets it right by accident. Symptom: a poll auto-title rendered as "Video_game" instead of "Video Game". Defense in depth: `_label_for`'s fallback now does `.replace("_", " ").split()` so any underscored future category title-cases correctly without a dedicated map entry, and the FE's `app/create-poll/createPollHelpers.ts: _CATEGORY_LABELS` carries BOTH spellings (`video_game` AND `videogame`) for the same reason. When adding a new built-in: add the entry to `BUILT_IN_TYPES`, mirror it in BOTH `_CATEGORY_LABELS` maps, and add a `test_<category>_label` regression test in `server/tests/test_poll_title.py` that asserts the wire-format value resolves.
- **View Transitions API was tried first but didn't fit.** The browser's `::view-transition-old(name)` / `::view-transition-new(name)` are bitmap snapshots — even with `width: 100%; height: 100%; object-fit: fill` they stretch as the group resizes (so the title squashes/elongates with it). For in-place content morphs, neither view transitions nor JS FLIP work cleanly; the simple fade-in is what shipped.
- **Hot-path event listener pattern: state-via-ref + empty deps.** GroupContent's POLL_PENDING listener used to depend on `[group]`, which tore the listener down + re-added on every vote / hydration / cache refresh. Now `groupRef.current = group` is updated in a separate effect and the listener reads from the ref with empty deps. Same pattern as the existing POLL_HYDRATED listener. Apply this any time a long-lived window listener needs to read state that changes frequently.

### Within-Group Poll Sort

- **The group-PAGE display order is reverse-chronological (`created_at` newest first, latest on top), for ALL tabs.** The `groupQuestions` memo in `app/g/[groupShortId]/GroupPage.tsx` re-sorts `group.questions` DESCENDING by `created_at`; it's the single source feeding `groupedGroupQuestions` → `visibleGroupedQuestions`, and the To Do · New · Old tabs all filter that list while preserving its order — so one comparator flip reverses every tab. Sub-questions within a multi-question poll still order by `question_index`. NOTE this is independent of the `lib/groupUtils.ts: sortByCreatedAt` order below: `buildGroups`/`buildGroupFromPolls` still build `group.questions` ASCENDING, but the group page overrides it on render. The virtualization/scroll anchor (`anchorGroupKey`) still picks the LAST visible group (now the oldest poll), so the default bottom-pin lands toward the bottom on long groups — flip the anchor + initial-scroll target if "land on the latest" is wanted.
- **`lib/groupUtils.ts` builds `group.questions` chronological ascending (`created_at` oldest first).** `sortByCreatedAt` (used by `buildGroups`/`buildGroupFromPolls`) is just `new Date(a.created_at) - new Date(b.created_at)`. The group page re-sorts this descending for display (see above); other consumers (e.g. `latestActivityMs` = `max(created_at)`, the home cross-group "by recency" sort) read the list independently of the in-group display order.

### Adding New Question Categories

- **Built-in categories** are defined in `TypeFieldInput.tsx: BUILT_IN_TYPES`. Add new entries there.
- **`isLocationLikeCategory()`** in `TypeFieldInput.tsx` controls which categories show reference location input and use proximity search. Update it when adding location-aware categories.
- **`isAutocompleteCategory()`** in `TypeFieldInput.tsx` controls which categories use the autocomplete dropdown (derived from `BUILT_IN_TYPES`).
- **Search dispatch** is in `AutocompleteInput.tsx: doSearch()` — add a new branch for each category's API endpoint.
- **Metadata rendering** is in `OptionLabel.tsx` — add detection function (like `isRestaurantEntry()`) and inline/stacked layout branches.
- **Place detail modal**: Tapping a restaurant/location name opens `PlaceDetailModal` (map embed + metadata). Tapping the address opens an iOS-style action sheet (`AddressActionsModal`) with "Open in Maps" (Apple Maps), "Open in Google Maps", and "Copy Address". Don't use `geo:` URIs on iOS — they're unreliable (may open Google Earth or other random apps). Don't include the business name in maps queries — it triggers a search for multiple branches instead of navigating to the specific address.
- **`line-clamp-2` breaks flex layouts**: Don't apply `line-clamp-*` to containers with flex children (like `OptionLabel`). The CSS treats flex items as flowing text and truncates unexpectedly. Use `overflow-hidden` instead and let inner components handle their own truncation.
- **Voting Cutoff field is a shared component**: `components/VotingCutoffField.tsx` renders the inline colored-value dropdown + conditional custom date/time inputs used by every question category in `app/create-question/page.tsx`. Reuse it when adding new categories — don't copy-paste the JSX. The custom date/time inputs inside use ids `customDate` and `customTime`; the component assumes only one instance is rendered at a time (enforced by the mutually exclusive `category === 'time'` vs `category !== 'time'` branches).
- **Three places to mirror new built-in labels**: `components/TypeFieldInput.tsx: BUILT_IN_TYPES`, `app/create-poll/createPollHelpers.ts: _CATEGORY_LABELS` (used by `labelForCategory`, the canonical FE label resolver), and `server/algorithms/poll_title.py: _CATEGORY_LABELS`. (The iMessage extension's Swift `categoryLabels` mirror was RETIRED in extension Phase 2 — the bubble's question labels now arrive server-rendered from `GET /api/polls/{short}/summary`, whose `_summary_question_label` reuses the poll_title map.) The two label maps must stay in lockstep — the server drives auto-generated wrapper titles; the FE drives draft preview + per-question section headers. `getQuestionSectionTitle` in `lib/questionListUtils.ts` follows this convention (special-cases `time` → `"Time"`, returns null for `yes_no` per the "yes_no is a category, not display text" rule, falls through to `getBuiltInType(category)?.label` for everything else). **yes_no is filtered out of auto-titles on both sides** (see the "'Yes/No' is a CATEGORY, not display text" bullet in the Poll Cards section) — both `draftPollPreview` (FE) and `generate_poll_title` (server) skip yes_no when composing wrapper titles. **`BUILT_IN_TYPES.label` and `_CATEGORY_LABELS` are NOT 1:1** for non-yes_no categories with formatting variants (e.g. the "Yes / No" UI label with spaces vs the historical "Yes/No" auto-title label without spaces) — when you need the auto-title-aligned label string, route through `labelForCategory`, don't read `BUILT_IN_TYPES.label` directly.
- **Time questions need a `question_type === 'time'` short-circuit before reading `category`**, mirroring `_category_for_title` in `server/routers/polls.py`. The Time bubble in `app/create-poll/page.tsx` sets `question_type=time` but leaves `category="custom"` (the form's default). Reading the category alone gives "Custom" everywhere this matters — auto-title generation, section headers, label lookups, AND category-icon lookups (e.g. compact preview pills): `getBuiltInCategoryIcon(sp.category)` returns `undefined` for time questions because "custom" isn't a built-in, so the pill rendered iconless. The `CompactTimePreview` callsite in `app/g/[groupShortId]/GroupCardItem.tsx` hard-codes `getBuiltInCategoryIcon("time")` since it's already inside a `question_type === "time"` branch — same fix idiom. Both `getQuestionSectionTitle` (FE) and `_category_for_title` (server) implement the short-circuit; if you write a third helper that maps a question to its display label, icon, or any other category-driven attribute, it must too.

### Custom-Category Emoji (per-question icon)

- **Any category (built-in OR custom) can carry a creator-chosen emoji, stored on `questions.category_icon` (migration 127, nullable TEXT).** When no emoji is chosen, the icon falls back to the built-in's `BUILT_IN_TYPES.icon` (built-in categories) or the generic question-type glyph (🗳️, custom categories). `category_icon` is the override and beats the default for either kind. (Originally the picker was a custom-only form row; it's now an inline tappable chip in front of the title preview that opens a modal — see the `EmojiPickerModal` bullet — and it's shown for every category except `yes_no`.)
- **`getCategoryIcon(question)` (`lib/questionListUtils.ts`) prefers `question.category_icon`** over the built-in icon and the type-symbol fallback. Two display sites: the group-card title glyph (`GroupCardItem`) and the poll-detail `InlineCategoryIcon`. The server mirror `_question_icon` (`server/routers/polls.py`) prefers it too for push-notification glyphs. Full flow: `category_icon` rides every read path automatically (question SELECTs are `SELECT *` → `_row_to_question` surfaces it → `toQuestion` maps it → `Question.category_icon`); on create it threads `CreateQuestionRequest` → `_insert_poll` INSERT (column sits between `category` and `options_metadata` — keep column/value/param counts aligned).
- **The emoji is an inline chip in front of the title preview, NOT a form row.** In `app/create-poll/page.tsx`, the title-preview slot at the top of the create-poll sheet renders a circular off-bg button (`w-8 h-8 rounded-full bg-gray-200 dark:bg-gray-700`) before the title/hint, **shown for every category except `yes_no`** (whose 👍 is conceptually fixed; gate is `category !== 'yes_no'`). The button glyph is `categoryEmoji.trim() || getBuiltInType(category)?.icon || '🗳️'`; **only the glyph `<span>` carries `opacity-40` in the auto/default state — the chip background stays solid** (so the faded look is "no explicit choice yet", and an explicit pick reads as solid). Tapping it opens `<EmojiPickerModal>`. `limited_supply` shows it too (default placeholder = 🎟️). The old `CategoryEmojiField` form-row component is **deleted**.
- **`components/EmojiPickerModal.tsx`** is the picker modal (`ModalPortal`, `z-[80]` so it sits above the create-poll sheet's `z-60` and `ConfirmationModal`'s `z-[70]`). Same UI the old field had: a single-emoji-validated text input above the relevance-sorted preset grid. **The modal box uses `w-fit max-w-[calc(100vw-2rem)]` and the grid is a FIXED-column CSS grid (`grid-cols-[repeat(7,2.25rem)]`)** so the box hugs the grid exactly — left/right margins are symmetric and the header text input's right edge (the header is `justify-between`) lands flush with the grid's right edge below. The clear ✕ sits to the LEFT of the input (so the input stays rightmost = aligned). Grid selection commits + closes; typing empty / tapping ✕ clears back to "no explicit emoji" (the chip reverts to the faded default). Props: `placeholder = getBuiltInType(category)?.icon` (component defaults to 🗳️ when undefined — don't re-pass `?? '🗳️'` at the callsite). 7 fixed columns is sized for mobile overlay scrollbars (width 0); on a desktop browser with classic scrollbars the last column can overflow the grid edge by ~15px while scrolling — harmless on the mobile target. Switching category resets the chosen emoji (`handleCategoryChange` clears `categoryEmoji`) so the chip reflects the new category's default until overridden. Free-text validated by `isEmoji()`; the grid is **sorted, not filtered**, by `rankEmojiOptions(categoryWord)`.
- **`lib/emojiData.ts` owns the matching logic; the dataset is curated + comprehensive.** `EMOJI_OPTIONS` is a curated `{emoji, keywords[]}` list (~146 emojis) that defines the picker grid's hand-ordered no-query default AND a small set of intentional, high-priority keywords. MATCHING is backed by BOTH that list AND a **comprehensive auto-generated keyword index** (`lib/emojiKeywords.generated.ts`, ~1592 emojis sourced from the Unicode CLDR `en` annotations — the same keyword set iOS emoji search uses). This is why typing "pie" surfaces 🥧 even though 🥧 isn't curated (the original bug: the curated list had no pie + no "pie" keyword, so `bestEmojiMatch` returned null and the new-poll suggestion rows fell back to the generic ✏️/👍/🎟️ glyph while the iOS keyboard — which has the full CLDR set — suggested 🥧).
  - **Generated index = `scripts/gen-emoji-keywords.mjs` → `lib/emojiKeywords.generated.ts`** (committed, ~90KB, lazy-loaded in the create-poll chunk — NOT in the initial bundle; no runtime network/dependency). Each entry is `[emoji, "space joined keywords"]` (CLDR `tts` name words first, then the `default` keyword list, lowercased/deduped/≤12 tokens). The generator **FE0F-qualifies** single-scalar text-default emojis (✂ → ✂️, 🏖 → 🏖️) via `\p{Emoji} && !\p{Emoji_Presentation}`, so the bundled forms render as emoji (not text) AND match the curated list's FE0F forms exactly — otherwise 🏖️ (curated) and bare 🏖 (CLDR) are distinct strings → duplicate picker buttons + bare-form returns. Regenerate against a newer CLDR release with `node scripts/gen-emoji-keywords.mjs`.
  - **`matchCandidates()` (lazy, memoized) pools curated + comprehensive but does NOT merge.** The curated list OWNS its emojis: comprehensive entries are skipped when their VS16-stripped form already exists in the curated set, so curated keywords + tie-break order stay byte-identical to the pre-CLDR behavior (every existing curated case — movie → 🍿, beach trip → ✈️, etc. — is unchanged). The comprehensive index ONLY fills emojis the curated list lacks. (An earlier attempt MERGED CLDR keywords onto curated entries; that shifted curated tie-breaks — e.g. 📷 stole "trip" and beat 🏖️ — so merging was dropped. Don't reintroduce it.)
  - **`rankedMatches(tokens)` is the single ordering** both `rankEmojiOptions` and `bestEmojiMatch` consume (so the auto-picked icon can't drift from the picker's top suggestion). Sort: score desc → curated-before-comprehensive → in-pool order. `scoreEmojiOption` is exact(3) > prefix(2) > length-≥4 substring(1); the length-4 floor stops short keywords like "pet" matching "competition". `tokenizeEmojiQuery` drops <2-char tokens + the noise word "custom".
  - `rankEmojiOptions(query)`: no query → returns `EMOJI_OPTIONS` unchanged (curated default grid); with a query → matches first (across curated + comprehensive, best first), then the curated remainder so the grid stays browseable — bounded to ~(matches + 146) buttons, not all 1592. `isEmoji(s)` accepts a single emoji incl. ZWJ/flag/keycap/skin-tone (whole-string emoji-only regex + `Intl.Segmenter` grapheme-count === 1, module-singleton segmenter; rejects letters/words/multi-emoji). When `Intl.Segmenter` is absent (very old engine) the multi-emoji check is skipped — the server length cap is the backstop.
- **`bestEmojiMatch(query): string | null` is the auto-pick variant** — `rankedMatches(tokens)[0]?.cand.emoji ?? null`, so it always equals `rankEmojiOptions(query)[0]` when a match exists. Returns null (not a default) when nothing matched anywhere, keeping the fallback choice a UI concern at the call site. Used by the new-poll search box (`app/create-poll/page.tsx: searchSuggestions`) to put a matching emoji on the FREE-TEXT suggestion rows instead of their generic fallback icon: yes/no + limited-supply share one `bestEmojiMatch(raw)` call (→ `?? '👍'` / `?? '🎟️'`), and the custom-category row uses `bestEmojiMatch(\`${subject} ${context}\`)` (→ `?? '✏️'`) so a match on the typed category OR its context surfaces an emoji. Built-in category rows are NOT touched — they keep their own `BUILT_IN_TYPES` icon (overriding a built-in's icon from its context would read as confusing). Tests: `tests/__tests__/emoji-match.test.ts` (curated invariants + comprehensive coverage + FE0F no-bare-twin).
- **A suggestion row's DISPLAYED emoji MUST equal the emoji it prefills onto the form's chip — they share one source via the `row()` helper.** `row(key, icon, overrides)` (in `searchSuggestions`, `app/create-poll/page.tsx`) computes the displayed glyph as `leadingIcon ?? o.categoryIcon ?? icon` and the prefill as `o.categoryIcon` (or `leadingIcon` when a leading emoji was typed). So a row that wants a content-matched emoji (`bestEmojiMatch` for the yes-no / limited-supply / custom-category rows) passes it as `overrides.categoryIcon` (NOT as the `icon` fallback arg) — the `?? undefined` after `bestEmojiMatch(...)` is load-bearing because it returns `string | null` while `Partial<QuestionDraft>.categoryIcon` is `string | undefined` (assigning `null` is a TS error). Rows that want a faded CATEGORY DEFAULT (built-in category rows, the generic 🗳️ options/context rows) pass NO `categoryIcon`, so the chip stays a faded default rather than a solid override — don't add `categoryIcon` to those. `pollToRecentEntry` mirrors this: it copies only an EXPLICIT `q.category_icon` into `overrides.categoryIcon` (never the built-in / type-symbol fallback). **The bug this fixed (`claude/poll-emoji-mismatch-ZrbNa`):** the content-matched emoji was passed only as the displayed `icon` arg, never into `overrides`, so tapping a "birthday party" suggestion showing 🎂 prefilled the form's chip with the custom default 🗳️ instead. When adding a new suggestion row, decide per-row: content-matched/explicit emoji → into `overrides.categoryIcon`; category default → omit it.
- **`splitLeadingEmoji(s): { emoji, rest }` (`lib/emojiData.ts`) peels a LEADING emoji off the new-poll search text.** Grapheme-segmenter based (reuses the module-private `graphemeSegmenter()` + `isEmoji()`), so multi-codepoint emoji (ZWJ family, flags, keycaps like `5️⃣`) split off as one unit; trims the gap after the emoji; degrades to `{ emoji: null, rest: s }` when the segmenter is unavailable. `searchSuggestions` (`app/create-poll/page.tsx`) calls it FIRST: when a leading emoji is present it (a) parses the REST (not the raw text) for category / options / context, (b) overrides EVERY suggestion row's icon with that emoji, and (c) threads `categoryIcon: leadingIcon` into each row's `overrides` so picking a row PREFILLS the chosen-emoji chip (the one `EmojiPickerModal` edits). A typed leading emoji takes PRIORITY over a per-row `bestEmojiMatch` result (it's the first term in `row()`'s `leadingIcon ?? o.categoryIcon ?? icon`). The user's input box keeps the emoji visible — it's stripped only for parsing/icon. Emoji-only input (no rest) → all category rows show, each wearing the emoji. Tests: `tests/__tests__/emoji-match.test.ts` (`splitLeadingEmoji` suite).
- **`effectiveCategoryIcon(draft)` (`app/create-poll/createPollHelpers.ts`) is the single source of truth** for "what icon does this draft persist" — the trimmed chosen emoji, or null when empty (the read path then falls back to the category's default icon). It does NOT restrict to custom categories anymore (`return (d.categoryIcon ?? '').trim() || null`), so a chosen emoji overrides the default for ANY category. Used by BOTH `draftToQuestionParams` and `synthesizePlaceholderPoll` so the API payload + optimistic placeholder can't diverge. **The `?? ''` null-guard is load-bearing, not defensive paranoia:** a draft restored from a localStorage `questionFormState` (or a staged `drafts[]` entry, both reloaded verbatim at `app/create-poll/page.tsx`) saved BEFORE the `categoryIcon` field shipped (migration 127) has no `categoryIcon`, so a bare `d.categoryIcon.trim()` threw `TypeError: undefined is not an object` mid-submit and the poll never posted — a real prod-Safari crash for returning users. **General rule for any draft-field chokepoint helper: read newer `QuestionDraft` fields null-safely** (`d.field ?? default`), because the localStorage form-state + staged-drafts restore paths replay old-shaped objects that predate the field. The per-key restore in `page.tsx` already guards scalar fields with `!== undefined` checks, but `formState.drafts` and `formState.options`/etc. are spread/restored wholesale, so any helper consuming a draft must tolerate a missing newer key on its own.
- **`validate_category_icon` (`server/services/validation.py`) is the backstop**, called per-question in `_validate_request` AND at insert. Lenient on emoji shape (the FE owns that) but caps length (≤64) and rejects ASCII letters / control chars — so a raw-API caller can't store arbitrary text/huge strings that would render verbatim as the icon (incl. in notifications). It never rejects a value the FE's `isEmoji` accepts (emoji carry no ASCII letters; keycap digits like "5️⃣" are allowed).

### Rich Selection Styling (Autocomplete Options)

- **Autocomplete is a FULL-SCREEN keyboard-aware overlay, NOT a dropdown popup — it reuses the new-poll search-box chrome.** Tapping a category option/suggestion field (`AutocompleteInput.tsx`, used by `OptionsInput` → create-poll Options, voter `SuggestionVotingInterface`, `LocationTimeFieldConfig`) fills the screen above with search results and floats the input just above the keyboard, identical to the new-poll search bar. Architecture:
  - **`components/KeyboardSuggestionPicker.tsx`** is the SHARED picker chrome (the generalization of the old `pollSearchBar`): visual-viewport tracking (`{top: vv.offsetTop, height: vv.height}`), the fixed `flex-col` container, a bottom-anchored scrollable list (`flex-1 min-h-0 overflow-y-auto` + inner `mt-auto`, so the best match sits nearest the bar), an always-rendered input-bar slot (`children`), and auto-scroll-to-bottom (bump `scrollSignal` when rows change). Props: `focused`, `rows`, `children` (input bar), `zClassName` (default `z-40`; the autocomplete overlay passes `z-[85]` so it covers the create-poll modal z-60), `barRef`, `scrollSignal`. **Body-scroll-lock is NOT done here** — each callsite owns its own `useBodyScrollLock` so the lock snapshot can't thrash across the focus→modal-open handoff. The new-poll bar (`app/create-poll/page.tsx`) renders through it with `focused={searchFocused}` (input always mounted = never reparented); the autocomplete renders it body-portalled only while `overlayOpen` (always `focused`). **The picker blocks unconsumable touch drags itself (iOS visual-viewport pan stutter fix).** On iOS Safari with the keyboard open, a drag the suggestion list can't consume (too short to scroll, or already at the edge in the drag direction) pans the VISUAL VIEWPORT within the layout viewport — neither the callsite's body lock (`position: fixed`) nor `overscroll-contain` governs that keyboard-driven pan, so the locked page behind visibly scrolls by AND the pan's `visualViewport` scroll events make the vv-pinned container chase `vv.offsetTop` through async re-renders (a visible up/down stutter). Fix lives in the picker (the only layer that knows the list's scroll geometry): a non-passive `touchmove` on the root `preventDefault`s drags the list can't consume; drags it CAN consume return early (real scrolling untouched — this is the sanctioned exception to the "never preventDefault in touchmove on a scrollable element" rule), and caret-drag/text-selection on `input, textarea` targets is exempt. Per-gesture state (input-target exemption, list scrollability, `maxScroll`) is classified ONCE at `touchstart` — touch events keep targeting the touchstart element all gesture — so the 60Hz move handler only reads `scrollTop`. Device-only bug (headless can't reproduce keyboard viewport panning); regressions to tap-select/scroll are headless-testable.
  - **`lib/useKeyboardPrimer.ts`** is the extracted iOS keyboard primer (`{prime, focusOnMount, cancel}`). The autocomplete inline field is a `<button>` styled like the input (preserving the rich-selection underline + favicon); tapping it `prime()`s the keyboard synchronously, opens the overlay, and the real input's `focusOnMount` callback ref transfers focus once it mounts (matches the create-poll title primer). `searchDisabled` (e.g. location category with no reference location) → plain inline `<input>`, no overlay (a full-screen picker with no suggestions is pointless) — **UNLESS `priorOptions` is non-empty** (see "Previously-Referenced Category Options" below), in which case the trigger+overlay still renders so the prior options can be shown + filtered with live search off.
  - **Pitfall: don't `.blur()` the overlay input in `selectSuggestion`** — it fires `onBlur→closeOverlay` synchronously, which trims the STALE `value` prop (the just-`onChange`'d label hasn't re-rendered) and can clobber the selection. Rely on unmount to dismiss the keyboard. **Compact-variant trigger alignment:** the inline button takes `className` verbatim (so `text-right` survives), and the value sits in a `block truncate` span that INHERITS the alignment — don't force `text-left`/`flex` on the button (the Tailwind source-order footgun would override `text-right`).
  - Verifying: the overlay open/full-screen/results/selection mechanics are headless-testable (movies/video-games return `[]` on dev — no TMDB/RAWG keys — so demo with **restaurant/location**, which use key-free OSM data; pre-seed `whoeverwants_user_location` so the reference auto-fills and search isn't `searchDisabled`). The iOS soft-keyboard handoff (primer) can ONLY be confirmed on a real device.
- **Options selected from autocomplete are styled as "chips"**: underlined text (Tailwind `underline decoration-blue-500/50 underline-offset-2`) and a favicon/image on the left edge (`pl-8` + absolutely positioned `<img>`). Plain-typed options have no special styling.
- **Chip-like clear behavior**: on focus, all text is selected (`input.select()`), so backspace or any keystroke replaces the entire value. After `selectSuggestion`, `requestAnimationFrame(() => input.select())` auto-selects; on re-focus when `isRichSelection`, text is selected again.
- **Metadata lifecycle**: `isRichSelection` is derived from `!!optionsMetadata?.[option]`. When the user edits a rich selection (any keystroke), `onRichValueCleared` fires and the parent calls `clearMetadataForOption()` to remove the metadata entry. The underline/icon disappear and the field reverts to plain text. Deleting an option via the trash button also cleans up its metadata.
- **`clearMetadataForOption()`** in `OptionsInput.tsx` is the single helper for metadata cleanup — used by both `removeOption` and `onRichValueCleared`. Don't duplicate this pattern.

### Previously-Referenced Category Options (autocomplete priming)

Opening an option field for an autocomplete category (restaurant / location / movie / video_game) surfaces — in the keyboard-aware overlay, **before typing** — options of that category previously *referenced* (given as ballot options OR submitted as suggestions), group-recency first then cross-group, ABOVE the live search results, with their rich graphics (favicon / poster / cover art) + a distance recomputed for the current poll. They stay until the typed text filters them out.

- **Backend: `GET /api/users/me/category-options?category=&group=`** (`server/services/category_options.py: load_category_options` + endpoint/models in `routers/users.py`). Pulls option strings from `questions.options` (finalized, recency = `questions.created_at`) UNION `votes.suggestions` (recency = `votes.created_at`), scoped by `questions.category`, metadata from `questions.options_metadata` (NOT votes — `_merge_suggestion_metadata` already folds vote metadata into the question row, so it's the single source for both label sources). Visibility = **`load_user_visibility` (account-aware membership union)** — don't reinvent the SQL. Dedups case-insensitively keeping most-recent casing + best-available metadata; returns `{group, general}` where `general` excludes labels already in `group`. Tolerant (anonymous / blank category / unknown group → `[]`). SIBLING of `load_category_recency` (`poll_categories.py`, which orders the bubble bar) — keep them parallel.
- **Distance is recomputed CLIENT-SIDE, never stored-and-reused.** The stored `distance_miles` was relative to the reference that existed when the option was first referenced, so it's dropped; `AutocompleteInput.priorResults` recomputes it via `haversineMiles(currentRef, storedLatLon)` against the NEW poll's reference (preserved `lat`/`lon` in metadata). No distance shows until the poll has a reference location. The server CAN'T do this (it doesn't know the new poll's reference) — FE is the correct layer.
- **Prior-vs-live dedup is CLIENT-SIDE by design.** Live Nominatim results are fetched in the browser, unknown to the category-options endpoint, so the merge can only happen on the FE. `AutocompleteInput.displaySuggestions` drops a live result matching a shown prior option by label (case-insensitive, every category) OR near-equal coordinates (`COORD_EPS = 1e-4` ≈ 11m — same place under a different label string, e.g. "Shake Shack" vs "Shake Shack, West 42nd Street, …"). Don't push this server-side.
- **`AutocompleteInput` owns the merge + bottom-anchored ordering.** `priorOptions?: PriorCategoryOption[]` prop → `priorToSearchResult` converts each to a `SearchResult` (renders identically to a live hit). The overlay list is bottom-anchored (best/most-prominent nearest the input bar), so the merge builds logical order **prior (recency) then live (best-first)** and `.reverse()`s it — putting the most-recent prior option nearest the bar with live results stacked above. Attribution row is gated on `naturalCount > 0` (hidden when only prior options show). **`searchDisabled` + non-empty `priorOptions` opens the overlay anyway** (live search off, prior options shown + text-filterable) — `openOverlay` allows it and the plain-input early-return is gated on `searchDisabled && priorResults.length === 0`. Backward-compatible: empty `priorOptions` ⇒ behavior identical to before #678. `OptionsInput` is a thin pass-through of the prop to every row.
- **Prefetch on form open, cached per (category, group).** `app/create-poll/page.tsx`: a `useEffect` keyed `[isModalOpen, category, currentGroupId]`, gated on `isAutocompleteCategory(category)`, fetches in the background the moment the modal opens (before the field is tapped) and caches in `categoryOptionsCacheRef` (`Map`, bounded by `CATEGORY_OPTIONS_CACHE_MAX = 50`, evict-oldest, since `CreateQuestionContent` is layout-persistent). Keyed on `(category, group)` — distinct from the adjacent `bubbleRecency` prefetch (keyed only on group; the bubble bar shows ALL categories). The divergence is intentional; don't unify.
- Tests: `server/tests/test_category_options.py` (7). Demo caveat: the dev tier has no TMDB/RAWG keys, so live movie/video-game search returns `[]` there — those posters/covers only appear from seeded/real-prod metadata, not from a fresh dev pick.

### AI Poll Suggestions (migration 145)

The create-poll search box predicts the user's NEXT polls with an LLM, tailored per `(user, group)` from the group's recent polls + the user's polls across groups. The predictions are STRUCTURED drafts (not titles) shown "ready to go" when the box opens, then re-ranked/filtered in real time against what the user types by the on-device embedding model. **Normal groups only** (explore has the variant feed). Distinct from the keyword/embedding CATEGORY ranker above (which only orders the deterministic heuristic rows) — this generates whole predicted polls.

- **Each suggestion is `{category, title?, options?, context?}` — fields, NOT a title.** The app auto-generates the displayed title from the fields (same as every poll), so the LLM only emits fields and a hallucinated/malformed one is dropped by DETERMINISTIC validation on both server + FE. `title` is the typed prompt (yes_no) / item (limited_supply); `options` is a fixed ballot list (≥2) for choice categories; `context` is the short "for X" subject. Suggestable categories = the creatable set minus `showtime` (needs a live catalog) — see `SUGGESTABLE_CATEGORIES`.
- **Server (`services/poll_suggest.py`):** `gather_history(conn, user_id, group_id)` (group's recent polls, any creator + the user's recent polls across non-explore groups → compact prompt lines + dedup signatures; None for explore groups) → `generate_from_history(ctx)` (builds the prompt, calls the LLM, validates + dedups; NO DB so the slow call holds no pooled conn) → `store_suggestions` / `load_cached_suggestions` (the `poll_suggestions` cache, PK `(user_id, group_id)`, JSONB). `refresh_poll_suggestions(user_id, group_id)` is the decoupled BackgroundTask (own connections, logs+swallows, won't clobber a good cache with an empty LLM blip). `generate_suggestions(conn,...)` is the eval/test convenience (gather+generate on one conn).
- **`services/llm_client.py`** is the generic OpenAI-compatible chat client (separate from `variant_llm.py`'s variant-specific helpers; both read the SAME `POLL_VARIANT_LLM_*` Ollama env so every tier inherits working config). `chat(system, user, *, model, temperature)` returns the content or None on any failure. `suggest_model()` = `POLL_SUGGEST_LLM_MODEL` override, else `POLL_VARIANT_LLM_MODEL` (graceful fallback to the variant model when the override/model isn't present).
- **Triggers:** `create_poll` schedules `refresh_poll_suggestions(creator_user_id, group_id)` on every non-explore poll create (the creator just made a poll → predict the next). `GET /api/users/me/poll-suggestions?group=<routeId>` (`routers/users.py`) returns the cached list + schedules a background regen when stale (`_REFRESH_AFTER` 12h) or missing — so a first-time open primes the next one. Tolerant: anonymous / no account / unresolvable group / explore group all return `[]`.
- **FE:** `apiGetPollSuggestions(groupRouteId)` (`lib/api/users.ts`); the create-poll page fetches per `currentGroupId` (+ refetch on `categoryRefreshTick`, which bumps after this user creates a poll). `suggestionToOverrides` (in `createPollHelpers.ts`, unit-tested) maps a suggestion → draft overrides with the SAME per-type mapping as `pollToRecentEntry` (recent-poll reuse), so a tapped suggestion prefills the form + auto-derives its title. `searchSuggestions` (`page.tsx`): EMPTY box → AI predictions appended after the category menu (top pick nearest the bar = the "ready to go" default); TYPED box → predictions matching the query spliced just above the parsed primary (parsed interpretation stays nearest the bar). Recents sit nearest the primary, AI just above.
- **Real-time fine-tune = `lib/aiSuggestionRank.ts: scoreSuggestions(query, texts)`** — cosine of the typed query to each suggestion's title via `embedTexts` (new export on `aiCategoryClassify.ts`, reusing the SAME ~30 MB model). A debounced effect scores the AI list; `searchSuggestions` filters out below `AI_SUGGESTION_MIN_SCORE` (0.22) and sorts best-nearest-the-bar. Falls back to token-prefix filtering before scores land / when the model is unavailable (all fail-open → null → no re-rank). The model is now **EAGERLY warmed on mount** (idle-scheduled) instead of only on focus, so it's ready to re-rank the instant the user types.
- **Model: `qwen3:14b`, UNIFIED for BOTH poll suggestions AND variant evolution (one model loads, not two — June 2026).** Previously suggestions ran `qwen2.5:14b` (via `POLL_SUGGEST_LLM_MODEL`) while variants ran `nous-hermes2:10.7b`, so a concurrent spawn + suggestion gen loaded two models (~16GB peak). Now `POLL_VARIANT_LLM_MODEL=qwen3:14b` drives both (suggestions fall back to it; `POLL_SUGGEST_LLM_MODEL` is left unset), so the worst case is ONE ~9GB model resident (~9GB peak). Chosen by a same-version bake-off (`prototypes/poll-suggest/eval.py`, Ollama 0.30.8): **qwen3:14b beat qwen2.5:14b decisively** — 5.57 vs 3.57 suggestions/run, 4.14 vs 3.43 distinct categories/run, 0/7 empty runs both, and qualitatively far more specific options *with locale grounding* (it inferred the group's metro from history and named real local restaurants where qwen2.5 omitted options). Variant generation was sanity-checked on qwen3:14b too (meaningfully-different yes/no angles, not synonym rewrites).
